18_1_1: There was a man in the land of Uz, whose name was Job; and that man was blameless and upright, a man of complete integrity who feared God and turned away from evil.
18_3_3: Let the day perish on which I was born, and the night that said, "A boy is conceived."
1_1_1: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
1_1_3: Then God said, "Let there be light," and there was light.
5_6_4: Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD alone is one.
1_1_2: The earth was formless and void, and darkness covered the surface of the deep, and the Spirit of God was hovering over the surface of the waters.
1_1_4: And God saw the light, that it was good, and God separated the light from the darkness.
1_1_5: God called the light Day, and the darkness He called Night, and evening passed and morning came, marking the first day.
1_1_6: Then God said, "Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, and let it separate the waters from the waters."
1_1_7: Thus God made the expanse and separated the waters which were under the expanse from the waters which were above the expanse, and it was so.
1_1_8: And God called the expanse Heaven. So the evening and the morning were the second day.
1_1_9: Then God said, "Let the waters beneath the heavens be gathered together into one place, and let the dry ground appear"; and it was so.
1_1_10: And God called the dry ground Earth, and the gathering together of the waters He called Seas, and God saw that it was good.
1_1_11: Then God said, "Let the earth bring forth vegetation, the herb yielding seed, and the fruit tree yielding fruit according to its kind, whose seed is in itself, on the earth"; and it was so.
1_1_12: The earth brought forth vegetation, the herb yielding seed according to its kind, and the tree bearing fruit whose seed is in itself according to its kind, and God saw that it was good.
1_1_13: And there was evening and there was morning, the third day.
1_1_14: Then God said, "Let there be lights in the expanse of the heavens to separate the day from the night, and let them serve as signs for seasons and for days and years."
1_1_15: And let them be for lights in the expanse of the heavens to shine light on the earth, and it was so.
1_1_16: God made the two great lights, the greater light to govern the day and the lesser light to govern the night, and He made the stars also.
1_1_17: God placed them in the expanse of the heavens to give light on the earth.
1_1_18: and to rule over the day and over the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. And God saw that it was good.
1_1_19: So the evening and the morning were the fourth day.
1_1_20: Then God said, "Let the waters teem with an abundance of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the open expanse of the heavens."
1_1_21: So God created great sea creatures and every living creature that moves, with which the waters swarmed, according to their kind, and every winged bird according to its kind, and God saw that it was good.
1_1_22: And God blessed them, saying, "Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth."
1_1_23: So the evening and the morning were the fifth day.
1_1_24: Then God said, "Let the earth produce living creatures according to their kinds: livestock and creatures that crawl and wild animals of the earth, each according to its kind"; and it was so.
1_1_25: And God made the beasts of the earth according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and everything that creeps on the ground according to its kind, and God saw that it was good.
1_1_26: Then God said, "Let Us make man in Our image, according to Our likeness; and let them rule over the fish of the sea, over the birds of the heavens, and over the livestock, over all the earth and over every creeping thing that creeps on the earth."
1_1_27: So God created man in His own image; in the image of God He created him; male and female He created them.
1_1_28: Then God blessed them, and God said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it; have dominion over the fish of the sea, over the birds of the heavens, and over every living creature that moves on the earth."
1_1_29: And God said, "See, I have given you every plant yielding seed which is on the face of all the earth, and every tree whose fruit yields seed; to you it shall be for food.
1_1_30: Also, to every beast of the earth, to every bird of the air, and to everything that creeps on the earth, everything that has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food; and it was so.
1_1_31: Then God saw everything that He had made, and behold it was very good indeed, and there was evening and there was morning, the sixth day.
1_2_1: Thus the heavens and the earth and all the host of them were completed.
1_2_2: And on the seventh day God finished His work which He had done, and He rested on the seventh day from all His work which He had done.
1_2_3: Then God blessed the seventh day and made it holy, because on it He rested from all His work which God had created and made.
1_2_4: This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created, in the day that the LORD God made the earth and the heavens.
1_2_5: Before any plant of the field was yet in the earth and before any herb of the field had sprouted, for the LORD God had not caused it to rain on the earth, and there was no man to cultivate the ground.
1_2_6: But a mist used to go up from the earth and water the whole surface of the ground.
1_2_7: Then the LORD God formed the man from the dust of the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life, and the man became a living being.
1_2_8: The LORD God planted a garden in Eden in the east, and there He placed the man whom He had formed.
1_2_9: And out of the ground the LORD God made every tree grow that was pleasing to the sight and good for food, and the tree of life was in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
1_2_10: Now a river flowed out of Eden to water the garden, and from there it divided and became four rivers.
1_2_11: The name of the first is Pishon; it is the one which flows around the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold.
1_2_12: The gold of that land is good, and bdellium and the onyx stone are also there.
1_2_13: The name of the second river is Gihon; it flows around the whole land of Cush.
1_2_14: The name of the third river is Hiddekel, which flows east of Assyria, and the fourth river is the Euphrates.
1_2_15: Then the LORD God took the man and placed him in the garden of Eden to work it and keep it.
1_2_16: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, "You are free to eat from every tree of the garden;
1_2_17: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you must not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you will surely die.
1_2_18: And the LORD God said, "It is not good for the man to be alone; I will make him a helper suitable for him."
1_2_19: Out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the air, and brought each to Adam to see what he would call them, and whatever Adam called each living creature, that was its name.
1_2_21: So the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam, and while he slept God took one of his ribs and closed up the opening with flesh.
1_2_22: Then the rib which the LORD God had taken from the man He fashioned into a woman, and He brought her to the man.
1_2_23: Then Adam said, "This one at last is bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man."
1_2_24: Therefore a man shall leave his father and mother and hold fast to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh.
1_2_25: And they were both naked, the man and his wife, and felt no shame.
1_3_1: Now the serpent was more cunning than any beast of the field which the LORD God had made, and he said to the woman, "Did God really say, 'You shall not eat of every tree of the garden'?"
1_3_2: And the woman replied to the serpent, "We may eat from the fruit of the trees of the garden;
1_3_3: But of the fruit of the tree which is in the middle of the garden, God has said, 'You shall not eat it, nor shall you touch it, or you will die.'
1_3_4: Then the serpent said to the woman, "You will surely not die."
1_3_5: For God knows that in the day you eat of it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing both good and evil.
1_3_6: So when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, that it was a delight to the eyes, and a tree desirable to make one wise, she took of its fruit and ate, and she also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate.
1_3_7: Then the eyes of both of them were opened, and they knew that they were naked; so they sewed fig leaves together and made themselves coverings.
1_3_8: And they heard the sound of the LORD God walking in the garden in the cool of the day, and Adam and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the LORD God among the trees of the garden.
1_3_9: Then the LORD God called out to the man and said to him, "Where are you?"
1_3_10: So he said, "I heard the sound of You walking in the garden, and I was afraid because I was naked; and I hid myself."
1_3_11: And He said, "Who told you that you were naked? Have you eaten from the tree whose fruit I commanded you not to eat?"
1_3_12: Then the man replied, "The woman whom You gave to be with me, she gave me fruit from the tree, and I ate it."
1_3_13: Then the LORD God said to the woman, "What is this you have done?" The woman said, "The serpent deceived me, and I ate."
1_3_14: So the LORD God said to the serpent, "Because you have done this, you are cursed more than all livestock and more than every beast of the field; on your belly you shall go, and dust you shall eat all the days of your life."
1_3_15: And I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your offspring and her offspring; He shall strike your head, and you shall strike His heel.
1_3_16: To the woman He said, "I will greatly multiply your pain in childbearing; in pain you shall bring forth children; your desire shall be contrary to your husband, and he shall rule over you."
1_3_17: Then to Adam He said, "Because you have listened to the voice of your wife and have eaten from the tree of which I commanded you, saying, 'You shall not eat of it,' cursed is the ground because of you; in painful toil you shall eat of it all the days of your life."
1_3_18: Both thorns and thistles it shall grow for you, and you shall eat the plants of the field.
1_3_19: By the sweat of your face you will eat bread until you return to the ground, for out of it you were taken; for you are dust, and to dust you will return.
1_3_20: And Adam called his wife's name Eve, because she would be the mother of all living.
1_3_21: The LORD God made garments of animal skins for Adam and his wife, and clothed them.
1_3_22: Then the LORD God said, "Behold, the man has become like one of Us, knowing good and evil, and now, lest he reach out his hand and take also from the tree of life, and eat, and live forever"—
1_3_23: Therefore the LORD God sent him out from the garden of Eden to work the ground from which he was taken.
1_3_24: So He drove out the man, and He stationed cherubim at the east of the garden of Eden, and a flaming sword which turned every direction, to guard the way to the tree of life.
1_4_1: Now Adam knew Eve his wife, and she conceived and bore Cain, and said, "I have acquired a man with the help of the LORD."
1_4_2: Then she bore again, this time his brother Abel, and when they grew up, Abel became a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground.
1_4_3: And in the course of time it came to pass that Cain presented an offering of the fruit of the ground to the LORD.
1_4_4: Abel also brought of the firstborn of his flock and of their fat portions, and the LORD had regard for Abel and his offering.
1_4_5: But He did not regard Cain and his offering, and Cain became very angry and his countenance fell.
1_4_6: So the LORD said to Cain, "Why are you angry? And why do you look so dejected?
1_4_7: If you do well, will you not be accepted? And if you do not do well, sin is crouching at the door, and its desire is for you, but you must master it.
1_4_8: Now Cain talked with Abel his brother, and when they were in the field, Cain rose up and attacked Abel his brother and killed him.
1_4_9: Then the LORD said to Cain, "Where is Abel your brother?" He said, "I do not know. Am I my brother's keeper?"
1_4_10: And He said, "What have you done? Listen! The voice of your brother's blood cries out to Me from the ground.
1_4_11: So now you are cursed and alienated from the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother's blood from your hand.
1_4_12: When you till the ground, it shall no longer yield its strength to you, and you shall be a restless fugitive and a wanderer on the earth.
1_4_13: And Cain said to the LORD, "My punishment is too great to bear!"
1_4_14: Surely You have driven me out this day from the face of the ground; I shall be hidden from Your presence; I shall be a restless fugitive and a wanderer on the earth, and it will happen that anyone who finds me will kill me.
1_4_15: And the LORD said to him, "Therefore whoever kills Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold," and the LORD put a mark on Cain so that no one finding him would kill him.
1_4_16: Then Cain went away from the presence of the LORD and settled in the land of Nod on the east of Eden.
1_4_17: Cain knew his wife, and she conceived and bore Enoch, and he built a city and named the city after his son Enoch.
1_4_18: To Enoch was born Irad, and Irad became the father of Mehujael, and Mehujael became the father of Methushael, and Methushael became the father of Lamech.
1_4_19: Lamech took for himself two wives: one named Adah and the other named Zillah.
1_4_20: And Adah bore Jabal, who was the father of those who dwell in tents and raise livestock.
1_4_21: His brother's name was Jubal, and he was the father of all those who play the harp and flute.
1_4_22: And as for Zillah, she also bore Tubal-Cain, the forger of all implements of bronze and iron, and the sister of Tubal-Cain was Naamah.
1_4_23: Then Lamech said to his wives, "Adah and Zillah, hear my voice; wives of Lamech, listen to my speech, for I have killed a man for wounding me, even a young man for striking me."
1_4_24: If Cain is avenged sevenfold, then Lamech seventy-sevenfold.
1_4_25: And Adam knew his wife again, and she bore a son and named him Seth, for she said, "God has appointed me another offspring in place of Abel, whom Cain killed."
1_4_26: And as for Seth, to him also a son was born, and he named him Enosh. At that time people began to call upon the name of the LORD.
1_5_1: This is the written account of the generations of Adam. In the day that God created man, He made him in the likeness of God.
1_5_2: He created them male and female, and blessed them and named them Mankind in the day they were created.
1_5_3: When Adam had lived one hundred and thirty years, he fathered a son in his own likeness, after his image, and named him Seth.
1_5_4: After he begot Seth, the days of Adam were eight hundred years, and he had other sons and daughters.
1_5_5: So all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years, and then he died.
1_5_6: When Seth had lived one hundred and five years, he became the father of Enosh.
1_5_7: After he begot Enosh, Seth lived another eight hundred and seven years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_5_8: So all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years, and then he died.
1_5_9: Enosh lived ninety years, and became the father of Kenan.
1_5_10: After he begot Cainan, Enosh lived another eight hundred and fifteen years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_5_11: So all the days of Enosh were nine hundred and five years, and then he died.
1_5_12: Kenan lived seventy years, and became the father of Mahalalel.
1_5_13: After he begot Mahalalel, Cainan lived another eight hundred and forty years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_5_14: So all the days of Kenan were nine hundred and ten years, and he died.
1_5_15: Mahalalel lived sixty-five years, and became the father of Jared.
1_5_16: After he begot Jared, Mahalalel lived another eight hundred and thirty years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_5_17: So all the days of Mahalalel's life were eight hundred and ninety-five years, and then he died.
1_5_18: Jared lived one hundred and sixty-two years, and became the father of Enoch.
1_5_19: After he fathered Enoch, Jared lived another eight hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_5_20: So all the days of Jared's life were nine hundred and sixty-two years, and then he died.
1_5_21: Enoch lived sixty-five years, and became the father of Methuselah.
1_5_22: After he begot Methuselah, Enoch walked with God three hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_5_23: So all the days of Enoch's life were three hundred and sixty-five years.
1_5_24: And Enoch walked in close fellowship with God, and then he was not, for God took him.
1_5_25: When Methuselah had lived one hundred and eighty-seven years, he became the father of Lamech.
1_5_26: After he begot Lamech, Methuselah lived seven hundred and eighty-two years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_5_27: So all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred and sixty-nine years, and then he died.
1_5_28: Lamech lived one hundred and eighty-two years, and became the father of a son.
1_5_29: And he called his name Noah, saying, "This one will bring us relief from our work and the painful toil of our hands, because of the ground which the LORD has cursed."
1_5_30: After he begot Noah, Lamech lived five hundred and ninety-five years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_5_31: So all the days of Lamech were seven hundred and seventy-seven years, and then he died.
1_5_32: After Noah was five hundred years old, Noah became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
1_6_1: Now it came to pass, when mankind began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born to them,
1_6_2: The sons of God saw that the daughters of mankind were beautiful, and they took as wives any they chose.
1_6_3: And the LORD said, "My Spirit shall not strive with man forever, for he is indeed flesh; nevertheless his days shall be one hundred and twenty years."
1_6_4: The Nephilim were on the earth in those days, and also afterward, when the sons of God came in to the daughters of men and they bore children to them, and those were the mighty men who were of old, the famous men of renown.
1_6_5: Then the LORD saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every intention of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually.
1_6_6: And the LORD was sorry that He had ever made man on the earth, and He was grieved in His heart.
1_6_7: So the LORD said, "I will blot out man whom I have created from the face of the earth, both man and beast, creeping thing and birds of the air, for I am sorry that I have made them."
1_6_8: But Noah found favor in the eyes of the LORD.
1_6_9: This is the account of Noah: Noah was a righteous man, blameless in his generation, and Noah walked with God.
1_6_10: And Noah became the father of three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
1_6_11: The earth also was corrupt in God's sight, and the earth was filled with violence.
1_6_12: So God looked upon the earth, and behold, it was corrupt, for all flesh had corrupted their way on the earth.
1_6_13: And God said to Noah, "I have determined that the end of all flesh has come before Me, for the earth is filled with violence because of them; and behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
1_6_14: Make yourself an ark of gopherwood, make rooms in the ark, and cover it inside and outside with pitch.
1_6_15: And this is how you shall make it: The length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits, its breadth fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits.
1_6_16: You shall make a roof for the ark, and you shall finish it to a cubit from above; and set the door of the ark in its side. You shall make it with lower, middle, and third decks.
1_6_17: And behold, I Myself am bringing a flood of waters on the earth, to destroy from under heaven all flesh in which is the breath of life; everything that is on the earth shall perish.
1_6_18: But I will establish My covenant with you, and you shall enter the ark—you, your sons, your wife, and your sons' wives with you.
1_6_19: And of every living creature of all flesh you shall bring two of every kind into the ark, to keep them alive with you; they shall be male and female.
1_6_20: Of the birds according to their kinds, of animals according to their kinds, and of every creeping thing of the ground according to its kind, two of every sort will come to you to keep them alive.
1_6_21: And you shall take for yourself every kind of food that is eaten, and you shall gather and store it up to yourself; and it shall be food for you and for them.
1_6_22: Thus Noah did everything; according to all that God commanded him, so he did.
1_7_1: Then the LORD said to Noah, "Come into the ark, you and all your household, because I have seen that you alone are righteous before Me in this generation.
1_7_2: You shall take with you seven pairs of every clean animal, a male and his female, and two of the animals that are not clean, a male and his female.
1_7_3: Also seven pairs of birds of the air, male and female, to keep offspring alive on the face of all the earth.
1_7_4: For after seven more days I will cause it to rain on the earth forty days and forty nights, and I will blot out from the face of the earth every living thing that I have made.
1_7_5: And Noah did everything according to all that the LORD commanded him.
1_7_6: Noah was six hundred years old when the flood of waters came upon the earth.
1_7_7: So Noah, with his sons, his wife, and his sons' wives, entered the ark to escape the waters of the flood.
1_7_8: Of clean animals, of animals that are not clean, of birds, and of everything that creeps on the ground,
1_7_9: Two by two they came into the ark to Noah, male and female, just as God had commanded Noah.
1_7_10: And it came to pass after seven days that the waters of the flood came upon the earth.
1_7_11: In the six hundredth year of Noah's life, in the second month, the seventeenth day of the month, on that same day all the fountains of the great deep burst open, and the windows of heaven were opened.
1_7_12: And the rain fell upon the earth forty days and forty nights.
1_7_13: On that very same day Noah and his sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and Noah's wife and the three wives of his sons with them, entered the ark.
1_7_14: They and every beast after its kind, all livestock after their kind, every creature that creeps on the earth after its kind, and every bird after its kind, every winged creature of every sort.
1_7_15: And they went into the ark to Noah, two by two, of every creature of flesh in which is the breath of life.
1_7_16: So those that entered, male and female of all flesh, went in as God had commanded him, and then the LORD shut the door behind him.
1_7_17: Now the flood continued on the earth forty days, and the waters increased and lifted up the ark, and it rose high above the earth.
1_7_18: The waters prevailed and increased greatly on the earth, and the ark floated safely on the surface of the waters.
1_7_19: And the waters prevailed exceedingly upon the earth, and all the high mountains under the whole heaven were covered.
1_7_20: The waters prevailed fifteen cubits higher, and the mountains were covered.
1_7_21: And all flesh died that moved on the earth: birds and livestock and beasts and every swarming creature that swarms on the earth, and all mankind.
1_7_22: All in whose nostrils was the breath of the spirit of life, everything that was on the dry land, died.
1_7_23: So He blotted out every living thing which was on the face of the ground, both man and cattle, creeping thing and bird of the air, and they were destroyed from the earth. Only Noah was left, and those who were with him in the ark.
1_7_24: And the waters surged and prevailed on the earth one hundred and fifty days.
1_8_1: Then God remembered Noah, and every living thing, and all the animals that were with him in the ark, and God sent a wind to blow over the earth, and the waters subsided.
1_8_2: The fountains of the deep and the floodgates of heaven were closed, and the rain from heaven was restrained.
1_8_3: The waters receded steadily from the earth, and at the end of one hundred and fifty days the waters had decreased.
1_8_4: Then the ark came to rest in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, on the mountains of Ararat.
1_8_5: The waters continued to decrease steadily until the tenth month, and on the first day of the tenth month, the tops of the mountains became visible.
1_8_6: So it came to pass, after forty days, that Noah opened the window of the ark which he had made.
1_8_7: Then he sent out a raven, which flew back and forth until the waters had dried up from the earth.
1_8_8: He also sent out from himself a dove, to see if the waters had subsided from the face of the ground.
1_8_9: But the dove found no resting place for the sole of her foot, and she returned to him into the ark, for the waters were still on the surface of the whole earth, so he put out his hand and took her and brought her into the ark to himself.
1_8_10: And he waited yet another seven days, and again he sent forth the dove out from the ark.
1_8_11: Then the dove came to him in the evening, and behold, a freshly plucked olive leaf was in her beak; and Noah knew that the waters had subsided from the earth.
1_8_12: So he waited yet another seven days and sent forth the dove, which did not return to him anymore.
1_8_13: And it came to pass in the six hundred and first year, in the first month, on the first day of the month, that the waters were dried up from the earth; and Noah removed the covering of the ark and looked, and behold the surface of the ground was dry.
1_8_14: And in the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, the earth had dried out.
1_8_15: Then God spoke to Noah, saying,
1_8_16: Go out of the ark, you and your wife, and your sons and your sons' wives with you.
1_8_17: Bring out with you every living thing of all flesh that is with you: birds and cattle and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth, so that they may breed abundantly on the earth, and be fruitful and multiply on the earth.
1_8_18: So Noah went out, and his sons and his wife and his sons' wives with him.
1_8_19: Every animal, every creeping thing, every bird, and everything that moves on the earth went out of the ark by their families.
1_8_20: Then Noah built an altar to the LORD, and took some of every clean animal and of every clean bird, and offered burnt offerings on the altar.
1_8_21: And the LORD smelled the soothing aroma, and the LORD said to himself, "I will never again curse the ground because of man, for the intention of man's heart is evil from his youth; nor will I again strike down every living thing as I have done."
1_8_22: While the earth endures, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease.
1_9_1: So God blessed Noah and his sons, and told them, "Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth."
1_9_2: The fear and dread of you shall be on every beast of the earth, on every bird of the air, on all that move on the ground, and on all the fish of the sea, for they are given into your hand.
1_9_3: Every moving thing that lives shall be food for you, and as I gave you the green plants, I have given you everything.
1_9_4: But you shall not eat meat with its life, that is, its blood.
1_9_5: Surely for your lifeblood I will demand an accounting; from the hand of every beast I will require it, and from the hand of man, from the hand of every man's brother I will require the life of man.
1_9_6: Whoever sheds human blood, by man his blood shall be shed, for in the image of God He made man.
1_9_7: And as for you, be fruitful and multiply; increase greatly on the earth and multiply in it.
1_9_8: Then God spoke to Noah and to his sons with him, saying:
1_9_9: And as for Me, behold, I Myself establish My covenant with you and with your descendants after you,
1_9_10: and with every living creature that is with you: the birds, the livestock, and every beast of the earth with you, all the animals that came out of the ark, every beast of the earth.
1_9_11: Thus I establish My covenant with you: Never again shall all flesh be cut off by the waters of the flood; never again shall there be a flood to destroy the earth.
1_9_12: And God said: "This is the sign of the covenant which I make between Me and you and every living creature that is with you, for all future generations:
1_9_13: I have set My rainbow in the clouds, and it shall be the sign of the covenant between Me and the earth.
1_9_14: It shall be, when I send clouds over the earth, that the rainbow will appear in the clouds.
1_9_15: And I will remember My covenant which is between Me and you and every living creature of all flesh; the waters shall never again become a flood to destroy all life.
1_9_16: When the rainbow is in the clouds, I will look upon it to remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is on the earth.
1_9_17: And God said to Noah, "This is the sign of the covenant which I have established between Me and all flesh that is on the earth."
1_9_18: Now the sons of Noah who came out of the ark were Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and Ham was the father of Canaan.
1_9_19: These three were the sons of Noah, and from these the people of the whole earth were populated.
1_9_20: And Noah began to be a farmer, and he planted a vineyard.
1_9_21: Then he drank some of the wine and became drunk, and lay uncovered inside his tent.
1_9_22: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw that his father was naked and went outside and told his two brothers.
1_9_23: But Shem and Japheth took a garment, laid it on both their shoulders, and walked backward and covered the nakedness of their father, and their faces were turned away so they did not see their father's nakedness.
1_9_24: So Noah awoke from his wine and learned what his youngest son had done to him.
1_9_25: Then he said, "Cursed be Canaan; the lowest of servants he shall be to his brothers."
1_9_26: And he said, Blessed be the LORD, the God of Shem, and let Canaan be his servant.
1_9_27: May God enlarge Japheth, and may he dwell in the tents of Shem, and may Canaan be his servant.
1_9_28: And Noah lived another three hundred and fifty years after the flood.
1_9_29: So all the days of Noah's life were nine hundred and fifty years, and he died.
1_10_1: Now this is the account of the sons of Noah: Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and sons were born to them after the flood.
1_10_2: The sons of Japheth were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
1_10_3: The sons of Gomer were Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
1_10_4: The sons of Javan were Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.
1_10_5: From these the coastland peoples spread out into their lands, everyone according to his language, according to their clans, into their nations.
1_10_6: The sons of Ham were Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.
1_10_7: The sons of Cush were Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabtechah, and the sons of Raamah were Sheba and Dedan.
1_10_8: Cush fathered Nimrod; he was the first to become a mighty one on the earth.
1_10_9: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD; therefore it became proverbial, "Like Nimrod the mighty hunter before the LORD."
1_10_10: The beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.
1_10_11: From that land he went forth into Assyria and built Nineveh, Rehoboth Ir, Calah,
1_10_12: and Resen between Nineveh and Calah, that is the great city.
1_10_13: Mizraim became the father of Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naphtuhim,
1_10_14: Pathrusim, and Casluhim from which came the Philistines, and Caphtorim.
1_10_15: Canaan became the father of Sidon his firstborn, and Heth.
1_10_16: the Jebusite, the Amorite, and the Girgashite;
1_10_17: the Hivite, the Arkite, and the Sinite;
1_10_18: the Arvadite, the Zemarite, and the Hamathite. Afterward the clans of the Canaanites were dispersed.
1_10_19: The territory of the Canaanites extended from Sidon as you go toward Gerar, as far as Gaza; then as you go toward Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha.
1_10_20: These were the sons of Ham, according to their clans, according to their languages, in their lands and in their nations.
1_10_21: And children were born also to Shem, the father of all the children of Eber, the older brother of Japheth.
1_10_22: The sons of Shem were Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram.
1_10_23: The sons of Aram were Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.
1_10_24: Arphaxad became the father of Shelah, and Shelah became the father of Eber.
1_10_25: To Eber were born two sons: the name of one was Peleg, for in his days the earth was divided, and his brother's name was Joktan.
1_10_26: Joktan fathered Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
1_10_29: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab; all these were the descendants of Joktan.
1_10_30: And their dwelling place extended from Mesha as you go toward Sephar, the hill country of the east.
1_10_31: These were the sons of Shem, according to their clans, according to their languages, in their lands, according to their nations.
1_10_32: These were the families of the sons of Noah, according to their genealogies, in their nations, and from these the nations spread out on the earth after the flood.
1_11_1: Now the whole earth used one language and the same words.
1_11_2: And it came to pass, as the people journeyed from the east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar, and they settled there.
1_11_3: Then they said to one another, "Come, let us make bricks and burn them thoroughly." And they used brick for stone, and they used asphalt for mortar.
1_11_4: And they said, "Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower whose top is in the heavens; let us make a name for ourselves, otherwise we be scattered abroad over the face of the whole earth."
1_11_5: But the LORD came down to look at the city and the tower which the sons of men had built.
1_11_6: And the LORD said, "Indeed the people are united and they all have one language, and this is only the beginning of what they will do; now nothing that they propose to do will be impossible for them.
1_11_7: Come, let Us go down and there confuse their language so that they may not understand one another's speech.
1_11_8: So the LORD scattered them abroad from there over the face of the whole earth, and they stopped building the city.
1_11_9: Therefore its name is called Babel, because there the LORD confused the language of the whole earth; and from there the LORD scattered them abroad over the face of all the earth.
1_11_10: This is the account of Shem: Shem was one hundred years old, and became the father of Arphaxad two years after the flood.
1_11_11: After he begot Arphaxad, Shem lived another five hundred years, and begot sons and daughters.
1_11_12: When Arphaxad had lived thirty-five years, he became the father of Shelah.
1_11_13: After he fathered Shelah, Arphaxad lived another four hundred and three years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_11_14: Salah lived thirty years and became the father of Eber.
1_11_15: After he begot Eber, Salah lived four hundred and three years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_11_16: Eber lived thirty-four years and became the father of Peleg.
1_11_17: After he begot Peleg, Eber lived another four hundred and thirty years, and begot other sons and daughters.
1_11_18: When Peleg had lived thirty years, he became the father of Reu.
1_11_19: After he fathered Reu, Peleg lived another two hundred and nine years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_11_20: When Reu had lived thirty-two years, he became the father of Serug.
1_11_21: After he fathered Serug, Reu lived two hundred and seven years, and had other sons and daughters.
1_11_22: When Serug had lived thirty years, he became the father of Nahor.
1_11_23: After he begot Nahor, Serug lived another two hundred years, and begot other sons and daughters.
1_11_24: Nahor lived twenty-nine years, and became the father of Terah.
1_11_25: After he fathered Terah, Nahor lived another one hundred and nineteen years, and fathered other sons and daughters.
1_11_26: When Terah had lived seventy years, he became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
1_11_27: This is the account of Terah: Terah begot Abram, Nahor, and Haran, and Haran begot Lot.
1_11_28: And Haran died in the presence of his father Terah in his native land, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
1_11_29: Abram and Nahor both took wives for themselves: the name of Abram's wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahor's wife was Milcah, the daughter of Haran the father of Milcah and Iscah.
1_11_30: But Sarai was barren and unable to conceive; she had no child.
1_11_31: Terah took his son Abram and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, his son Abram's wife, and they set out together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go to the land of Canaan, but when they came to Haran, they settled there.
1_11_32: So the days of Terah were two hundred and five years, and Terah died while in Haran.
1_12_1: Now the LORD had said to Abram, "Leave your country, your relatives, and your father's house, and go to the land that I will show you.
1_12_2: I will make you into a great nation, I will bless you and make your name great, and you shall be a blessing.
1_12_3: I will bless those who bless you, and I will curse him who treats you with contempt, and in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed.
1_12_4: So Abram went forth as the LORD had instructed him, and Lot went with him, and Abram was seventy-five years old when he departed from Haran.
1_12_5: Then Abram took Sarai his wife and Lot his nephew, and all their possessions that they had accumulated, and the people whom they had acquired in Haran, and they set out to go to the land of Canaan, and so they came to the land of Canaan.
1_12_6: Abram passed through the land as far as the site of Shechem, to the oak of Moreh, and at that time the Canaanites were in the land.
1_12_7: Then the LORD appeared to Abram and said, "To your offspring I will give this land." So he built an altar there to the LORD, who had appeared to him.
1_12_8: And he moved from there to the hill country east of Bethel, and he pitched his tent with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east; there he built an altar to the LORD and called upon the name of the LORD.
1_12_9: So Abram journeyed on by stages toward the Negev.
1_12_10: Now there was a famine in the land, and Abram went down to Egypt to stay there for a while, for the famine was severe in the land.
1_12_11: And it came to pass, when he was close to entering Egypt, that he said to Sarai his wife, "Look, I know that you are a beautiful woman in appearance.
1_12_12: Therefore it will happen, when the Egyptians see you, they will say, 'This is his wife,' and they will kill me, but they will let you live.
1_12_13: Please say you are my sister, that it may go well with me for your sake, and that my life may be spared because of you.
1_12_14: So it was, when Abram entered Egypt, that the Egyptians saw the woman, that she was very beautiful.
1_12_15: The officials of Pharaoh saw her and praised her to Pharaoh, and the woman was taken into Pharaoh's palace.
1_12_16: He treated Abram well for her sake, and gave him sheep, oxen, male donkeys, male and female servants, female donkeys, and camels.
1_12_17: But the LORD struck Pharaoh and his household with great plagues because of Sarai, Abram's wife.
1_12_18: So Pharaoh summoned Abram and said, "What is this you have done to me? Why did you not tell me that she was your wife?"
1_12_19: Why did you say, 'She is my sister,' so that I took her as my wife? Now then, here is your wife; take her and go your way.
1_12_20: So Pharaoh commanded his men concerning him, and they escorted him away with his wife and all that he had.
1_13_1: So Abram went up from Egypt to the Negev, he and his wife and all that he had, and Lot with him.
1_13_2: Abram was very wealthy in livestock, in silver, and in gold.
1_13_3: And he went on his journey by stages from the Negev as far as Bethel, to the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai.
1_13_4: To the place of the altar which he had built there at first, and there Abram worshiped the LORD.
1_13_5: Lot also, who was traveling with Abram, had flocks and herds and tents.
1_13_6: Now the land was not able to support them so that they might dwell together, for their possessions were so great that they could not remain together.
1_13_7: And there was strife between the herdsmen of Abram's livestock and the herdsmen of Lot's livestock, and at that time the Canaanites and the Perizzites were dwelling in the land.
1_13_8: So Abram said to Lot, "Please let there be no quarreling between you and me, and between my herdsmen and your herdsmen, for we are kinsmen.
1_13_9: Is not the whole land before you? Please separate yourself from me; if you take the left, then I will go to the right, or if you take the right, then I will go to the left.
1_13_10: And Lot lifted up his eyes and saw that the entire plain of Jordan was well watered everywhere before the LORD destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah, like the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt as you go toward Zoar.
1_13_11: So Lot chose for himself the entire plain of Jordan, and Lot journeyed eastward, and they separated from each other.
1_13_12: Abram settled in the land of Canaan, while Lot dwelt in the cities of the plain and moved his tent as far as Sodom.
1_13_13: But the men of Sodom were extremely wicked and sinful against the LORD.
1_13_14: And the LORD said to Abram, after Lot had separated from him, "Lift up your eyes now and look from the place where you are—northward and southward and eastward and westward.
1_13_15: For all the land which you see I will give to you and to your offspring forever.
1_13_16: And I will make your offspring as the dust of the earth, so that if anyone could number the dust of the earth, then your offspring also could be numbered.
1_13_17: Arise, walk through the land in every direction through its length and breadth, for I am giving it to you.
1_13_18: Then Abram moved his tent and came and settled by the oaks of Mamre, which are at Hebron, and there he built an altar to the LORD.
1_14_1: In those days Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of nations waged war.
1_14_2: These kings made war with Bera king of Sodom, Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, that is, Zoar.
1_14_3: All these came as allies to the Valley of Siddim, that is, the Salt Sea.
1_14_4: Twelve years they had served Chedorlaomer, but in the thirteenth year they rebelled.
1_14_5: In the fourteenth year Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him came and defeated the Rephaim in Ashteroth Karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, the Emim in Shaveh Kiriathaim.
1_14_6: and the Horites in their hill country of Seir, as far as El Paran, which is by the wilderness.
1_14_7: Then they turned back and came to En Mishpat (that is, Kadesh), and conquered all the territory of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites who dwelt in Hazezon Tamar.
1_14_8: And the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar) went out and arrayed themselves together in battle in the Valley of Siddim.
1_14_9: Against Chedorlaomer king of Elam, Tidal king of Goiim, Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar—four kings against five.
1_14_10: Now the Valley of Siddim was full of tar pits, and as the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, some fell into them, and the rest escaped to the mountains.
1_14_11: Then the victorious invaders took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah, and all their provisions, and went their way.
1_14_12: They also captured Lot, Abram's brother's son who was living in Sodom, and his possessions, and departed.
1_14_13: Then a fugitive came and told Abram the Hebrew, for he dwelt by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and brother of Aner, and they were allies with Abram.
1_14_14: When Abram heard that his nephew was taken captive, he led forth his three hundred and eighteen trained men born in his own household and went in pursuit as far as Dan.
1_14_15: He divided his forces against them by night, and he and his servants attacked them and defeated them and pursued them as far as Hobah, which is north of Damascus.
1_14_16: So he brought back all the goods, and also brought back his nephew Lot and his possessions, as well as the women and the people.
1_14_17: After Abram returned from the defeat of Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sodom went out to meet him at the Valley of Shaveh, that is, the King's Valley.
1_14_18: Then Melchizedek king of Salem brought out bread and wine for Abram; he was the priest of God Most High.
1_14_19: And he blessed him and said, Blessed be Abram by God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
1_14_20: And blessed be God Most High, who has delivered your enemies into your hand, and Abram gave him a tenth of everything.
1_14_21: Now the king of Sodom said to Abram, "Give me the people, and take the possessions for yourself."
1_14_22: But Abram said to the king of Sodom, "I have raised my hand in an oath to the LORD, God Most High, the Creator of heaven and earth,
1_14_23: I will not take anything from a thread to a sandal strap or anything that belongs to you, so that you can never say, 'I have made Abram rich.'
1_14_24: I will take nothing except what the young men have already eaten, and the share of the men who went with me: Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre; let them take their share.
1_15_1: After these things the word of the LORD came to Abram in a vision, saying, "Do not be afraid, Abram, for I am your shield, your reward shall be very great."
1_15_2: But Abram said, "Lord GOD, what will You give me, since I continue childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?"
1_15_3: Then Abram said, "Look, You have given me no offspring, so a member of my household born in my house will be my heir."
1_15_4: And behold, the word of the LORD came to him, saying, "This man shall not be your heir, but one who will come from your own body shall be your heir."
1_15_5: Then He brought him outside and said, "Look now toward the heavens, and count the stars if you are able to number them." And He said to him, "So shall your offspring be."
1_15_6: And he believed in the LORD, and He credited it to him as righteousness.
1_15_7: Then He said to him, "I am the LORD, who brought you out from Ur of the Chaldeans, to give you this land to possess it."
1_15_8: And he said, "Lord GOD, how can I be sure that I will inherit it?"
1_15_9: So He said to him, "Bring Me a three-year-old heifer, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a turtledove, and a young pigeon."
1_15_10: Then he brought all these to Him and cut them in two, down the middle, and laid each half opposite the other; but he did not cut the birds in two.
1_15_11: And when the vultures swooped down on the carcasses, Abram drove them away.
1_15_12: Now when the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell upon Abram; and behold, dreadful terror and great darkness fell upon him.
1_15_13: Then He said to Abram: "Know for certain that your descendants will be strangers in a land that is not theirs, and will serve them, and they will be oppressed four hundred years.
1_15_14: And also the nation whom they serve I will judge, and afterward they shall come out with great possessions.
1_15_15: Now as for you, you will go to your fathers in peace and be buried at a ripe old age.
1_15_16: But in the fourth generation your descendants will return here, for the iniquity of the Amorites has not yet reached its full measure.
1_15_17: And it came to pass, when the sun had gone down and it was dark, that behold, there appeared a smoking firepot and a flaming torch that passed between those pieces.
1_15_18: On that day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying, "To your descendants I have given this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the River Euphrates—
1_15_19: The land of the Kenites, the Kenezzites, the Kadmonites,
1_15_20: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim,
1_15_21: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites."
1_16_2: So Sarai said to Abram, "See now, the LORD has prevented me from bearing children. Please, go in to my maid; perhaps I shall obtain children through her." And Abram heeded the voice of Sarai.
1_16_3: So after Abram had lived ten years in the land of Canaan, Sarai, Abram's wife, took Hagar her servant, the Egyptian, and gave her to her husband Abram to be his wife.
1_16_4: So he went in to Hagar, and she conceived, and when she saw that she had conceived, she looked with contempt on her mistress.
1_16_5: Then Sarai said to Abram, "My wrong be upon you! I gave my maid into your arms; and when she saw that she had conceived, I became despised in her eyes. The LORD judge between you and me."
1_16_6: So Abram said to Sarai, "Indeed your maid is in your power; do to her as you please." And when Sarai dealt harshly with her, she fled from her presence.
1_16_7: Now the Angel of the LORD found her beside a spring of water in the wilderness, the spring on the way to Shur.
1_16_8: And He said, "Hagar, Sarai's servant, where have you come from, and where are you going?" She said, "I am running away from the presence of my mistress Sarai."
1_16_9: The Angel of the LORD said to her, "Return to your mistress, and submit yourself to her authority."
1_16_10: Then the Angel of the LORD said to her, "I will surely multiply your descendants exceedingly, so that they will be too many to count."
1_16_11: And the Angel of the LORD said to her, "Behold, you are with child and you shall bear a son, and you shall call his name Ishmael, because the LORD has listened to your affliction."
1_16_12: He shall be a wild donkey of a man; his hand shall be against everyone, and everyone's hand against him, and he shall dwell in the presence of all his brethren.
1_16_13: Then she called the name of the LORD who spoke to her, You-Are-the-God-Who-Sees; for she said, "Have I truly here seen the One who looks after me?"
1_16_14: Therefore the well was called Beer Lahai Roi; it lies between Kadesh and Bered.
1_16_15: So Hagar gave birth to Abram's son, and Abram named his son, whom Hagar bore, Ishmael.
1_16_16: Abram was eighty-six years old when Hagar bore Ishmael to him.
1_16_1: Now Sarai, Abram's wife, had borne him no children, and she had an Egyptian servant whose name was Hagar.
1_17_1: When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the LORD appeared to Abram and said to him, "I am Almighty God; walk faithfully before Me and be blameless.
1_17_2: I will establish My covenant between Me and you, and will multiply you exceedingly.
1_17_3: Then Abram fell facedown, and God spoke with him, saying:
1_17_4: As for Me, behold, My covenant is with you, and you shall be the father of a multitude of nations.
1_17_5: No longer shall your name be called Abram, but your name shall be Abraham; for I have made you the father of a multitude of nations.
1_17_6: I will make you exceedingly fruitful, and I will make nations of you, and kings will come forth from you.
1_17_7: And I will establish My covenant between Me and you and your offspring after you throughout their generations, as an everlasting covenant, to be your God and the God of your offspring after you.
1_17_8: And I will give to you and your offspring after you the land where you are residing as a foreigner, all the land of Canaan, as an everlasting possession, and I will be their God.
1_17_9: And God said to Abraham, "As for you, you shall keep My covenant, you and your offspring after you throughout their generations.
1_17_10: This is My covenant which you must keep, between Me and you and your descendants after you: Every male among you shall be circumcised.
1_17_11: You shall be circumcised in the flesh of your foreskins, and it shall serve as a sign of the covenant between Me and you.
1_17_12: Every male among you who is eight days old shall be circumcised throughout your generations, whether born in your household or bought with money from any foreigner who is not your descendant.
1_17_13: He who is born in your house and he who is bought with your money must surely be circumcised, and My covenant shall be marked in your flesh as an everlasting covenant.
1_17_14: And the uncircumcised male child who is not circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin shall be cut off from his people; he has broken My covenant.
1_17_15: Then God said to Abraham, "As for Sarai your wife, you shall no longer call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall be her name.
1_17_16: I will bless her and indeed give you a son by her; then I will bless her, and she shall become a mother of nations; kings of peoples shall come from her.
1_17_17: Then Abraham fell on his face and laughed, and said to himself, "Can a child be born to a man who is one hundred years old? And can Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear a child?"
1_17_18: And Abraham said to God, "Oh, that Ishmael might live under your special blessing!"
1_17_19: Then God said, "No, Sarah your wife shall bear you a son, and you shall call his name Isaac; I will confirm My covenant with him as an everlasting covenant, and with his descendants after him."
1_17_20: And as for Ishmael, I have heard you; behold, I have blessed him and will make him fruitful and multiply him greatly, he shall father twelve princes, and I will make him into a great nation.
1_17_21: But My covenant I will establish with Isaac, whom Sarah will bear to you at this appointed time next year.
1_17_22: Then He finished talking with him, and God withdrew up from Abraham.
1_17_23: So Abraham took Ishmael his son, all who were born in his house and all who were bought with his money, every male among the members of Abraham's household, and circumcised the flesh of their foreskins that very day, just as God had said to him.
1_17_24: Abraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
1_17_25: And Ishmael his son was thirteen years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
1_17_26: On that very same day Abraham was circumcised, and his son Ishmael.
1_17_27: And all the men of his household, born in the house or bought with money from a foreigner, were circumcised with him.
1_18_1: Then the LORD appeared to him by the oaks of Mamre, as he was sitting at the entrance of his tent in the heat of the day.
1_18_2: So he lifted his eyes and looked, and behold, three men were standing nearby, and when he saw them, he ran from the tent entrance to meet them and bowed himself to the ground.
1_18_3: And he said, "My Lord, if I have now found favor in Your sight, please do not pass by Your servant."
1_18_4: Please let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves in the shade under the tree.
1_18_5: And I will bring a bit of bread so that you may refresh yourselves, and after that you may continue on your journey, since you have honored your servant with this visit, and they said, Do as you have said.
1_18_6: So Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah and said, "Quickly, prepare three measures of your best flour, knead it into dough, and make cakes."
1_18_7: And Abraham ran to the herd, took a tender and choice calf, gave it to a young man, and he quickly prepared it.
1_18_8: So he took curds and milk and the calf which he had prepared, and set it before them, and he served them under the tree as they ate.
1_18_9: Then they said to him, "Where is Sarah your wife?" So he answered, "There, in the tent."
1_18_10: And He said, "I will certainly return to you about this time next year, and behold, Sarah your wife shall have a son." Sarah was listening at the tent entrance which was behind him.
1_18_11: Now Abraham and Sarah were old, well advanced in years, and Sarah had passed the age of childbearing.
1_18_12: Therefore Sarah laughed within herself, saying, "After I have grown old and worn out, shall I have pleasure, my lord being old also?"
1_18_13: And the LORD said to Abraham, "Why did Sarah laugh, saying, 'Shall I indeed bear a child, now that I am old?'
1_18_14: Is anything too hard for the LORD? At the appointed time I will return to you, about this time next year, and Sarah shall have a son.
1_18_15: But Sarah denied it, saying, "I did not laugh," for she was afraid, and He said, "No, you did laugh."
1_18_16: Then the men rose from there and looked down toward Sodom, and Abraham was walking with them to send them on their way.
1_18_17: And the LORD said, "Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do,
1_18_18: Since Abraham will certainly become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth will be blessed through him?
1_18_19: For I have chosen him, in order that he may command his children and his household after him to keep the way of the LORD by doing righteousness and justice, so that the LORD may bring to Abraham what He has promised him.
1_18_20: And the LORD said, "Because the outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and because their sin is exceedingly grave,
1_18_21: I will go down now and see whether their actions are altogether according to the outcry against it that has come to Me; and if not, I will know.
1_18_22: Then the men turned away from there and headed toward Sodom, while Abraham remained standing before the LORD.
1_18_23: And Abraham drew near and said, "Will You indeed sweep away the righteous with the wicked?"
1_18_24: Suppose there are fifty righteous within the city; would You really sweep away the place and not spare it for the sake of the fifty righteous who are in it?
1_18_25: Far be it from You to do such a thing as this, to slay the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous should be treated the same as the wicked; far be it from You! Shall not the Judge of all the earth do what is just?
1_18_26: So the LORD said, "If I find in Sodom fifty righteous people within the city, then I will spare the entire place for their sake."
1_18_27: Then Abraham answered and said, "Indeed now, I who am but dust and ashes have ventured to speak to the Lord:
1_18_28: Suppose there were five less than the fifty righteous; would You destroy the whole city for lack of five?" So He said, "If I find forty-five there, I will not destroy it."
1_18_29: And he spoke to Him yet again and said, "Suppose there should be forty found there?" So He answered, "I will not do it for the sake of forty."
1_18_30: Then he said, "Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak further: Suppose thirty are found there?" So He said, "I will not do it if I find thirty there."
1_18_31: And he said, "Indeed now, I have ventured to speak to the Lord: Suppose twenty should be found there?" So He said, "I will not destroy it for the sake of twenty."
1_18_32: Then he said, "Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak just this once more: Suppose ten are found there?" And He said, "I will not destroy it for the sake of ten."
1_18_33: So the LORD went His way as soon as He had finished speaking with Abraham, and Abraham returned to his tent.
1_19_1: Now the two angels came to Sodom in the evening, and Lot was sitting in the gateway of Sodom, and when Lot saw them, he rose to meet them and bowed himself with his face to the ground.
1_19_2: And he said, "Here now, my lords, please turn aside to your servant's house and spend the night, and wash your feet; then you may rise early and go on your way." But they said, "No, we will spend the night in the town square."
1_19_3: But he urged them strongly, so they turned aside to him and entered his house, and he prepared a feast for them and baked unleavened bread, and they ate.
1_19_4: Before they lay down, the men of the city, the men of Sodom, both young and old, the whole population from every quarter, surrounded the house.
1_19_5: And they called to Lot and shouted to him, "Where are the men who came to you tonight? Bring them out to us that we may have sex with them!"
1_19_6: So Lot went out to them at the entrance, shut the door behind him,
1_19_7: and said, "Please, my brothers, do not act so wickedly!
1_19_8: Look now, I have two daughters who have not known a man; please, let me bring them out to you, and you may do to them as you wish; only do nothing to these men, for they have come under the protection of my roof.
1_19_9: And they said, "Stand back!" Then they said, "This fellow came in to stay here as an alien, and he keeps acting as a judge; now we will deal worse with you than with them." So they pressed hard against the man Lot, and came near to break down the door.
1_19_10: But the men reached out their hands and pulled Lot into the house with them and bolted the door.
1_19_11: And they struck the men who were at the entrance of the house with blindness, both young and old, so that they wore themselves out groping to find the door.
1_19_12: Then the men said to Lot, "Do you have anyone else here? Sons-in-law, your sons, your daughters, and anyone who belongs to you in the city—bring them out of this place!"
1_19_13: For we will destroy this place, because the outcry against them has become so great before the LORD, and the LORD has sent us to destroy it.
1_19_14: So Lot went out and spoke to his sons-in-law, who were to marry his daughters, and said, "Get up, get out of this place, for the LORD is about to destroy this city!" But to his sons-in-law he seemed to be joking.
1_19_15: When the morning dawned, the angels urged Lot to hurry, saying, "Arise, take your wife and your two daughters who are here, lest you be swept away in the punishment of the city."
1_19_16: And while he hesitated, the men seized his hand, his wife's hand, and the hands of his two daughters, the LORD being merciful to him, and they brought him out and set him outside the city.
1_19_17: So it came to pass, when they had brought them outside, that he said, "Run for your life! Do not look back or stop anywhere in the plain. Escape to the mountains, or you will be swept away."
1_19_18: Then Lot said to them, "Oh no, please, my lords!
1_19_19: Indeed now, your servant has found favor in your sight, and you have shown me great kindness by saving my life, but I cannot escape to the mountains, lest the disaster overtake me and I die.
1_19_20: Look now, this city is near enough to flee to, and it is a little one; please let me escape there (is it not a little one?) so that my life will be saved.
1_19_21: And he said to him, "See, I have favored you concerning this request also, in that I will not overthrow this town for which you have spoken.
1_19_22: Hurry, escape there quickly, for I cannot do anything until you arrive there." Therefore the name of the city was called Zoar.
1_19_23: The sun had risen over the earth when Lot reached Zoar.
1_19_24: Then the LORD rained down burning sulfur and fire on Sodom and Gomorrah from the LORD out of heaven.
1_19_25: So He overthrew those cities, the entire plain, all the inhabitants of the cities, and what grew on the ground.
1_19_26: But his wife looked back from behind him, and she became a pillar of salt.
1_19_27: And Abraham arose early in the morning to the place where he had stood before the LORD.
1_19_28: Then he looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah, and toward all the land of the plain; and he saw, and behold, the smoke of the land ascended like the smoke of a furnace.
1_19_29: And it came to pass, when God destroyed the cities of the valley, that God remembered Abraham and brought Lot out of the midst of the catastrophe when He overthrew the cities in which Lot had lived.
1_19_30: Then Lot went up out of Zoar and lived in the mountains, and his two daughters were with him, for he was afraid to dwell in Zoar, and he and his two daughters dwelt in a cave.
1_19_31: Now the firstborn said to the younger, "Our father is old, and there is no man anywhere in this entire area to come in to us as is the custom of all the earth."
1_19_32: Come, let us get our father to drink wine, and we will lie with him, that we may preserve offspring from our father.
1_19_33: So they made their father drink wine that night, and the firstborn went in and lay with her father, and he was unaware when she lay down or when she arose.
1_19_34: The next day the firstborn said to the younger, "Indeed I lay with my father last night; let us make him drink wine tonight also, and you go in and lie with him, that we may preserve offspring through our father."
1_19_35: Then they made their father drink wine that night also, and the younger arose and lay with him, and he did not know when she lay down or when she arose.
1_19_36: Thus both of Lot's daughters became pregnant by their father.
1_19_37: The firstborn gave birth to a son and called his name Moab; he is the father of the Moabites to this day.
1_19_38: And the younger also bore a son and called his name Ben-Ammi; he is the father of the Ammonites to this day.
1_20_1: Abraham journeyed from there toward the Negev, and lived between Kadesh and Shur, and sojourned in Gerar.
1_20_2: Now Abraham said of Sarah his wife, "She is my sister," so Abimelech king of Gerar sent and took Sarah.
1_20_3: But God came to Abimelech in a dream by night and said to him, "You are a dead man because of the woman you have taken, for she is already married."
1_20_4: But Abimelech had not come near her, and he said, "Lord, will You destroy an innocent nation also?"
1_20_5: Did he not himself say to me, 'She is my sister'? And she herself said, 'He is my brother.' In the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands I have done this.
1_20_6: And God said to him in the dream, "Yes, I know that you did this in the integrity of your heart, and it was I who kept you from sinning against me; therefore I did not let you touch her."
1_20_7: Now therefore, return the man's wife, for he is a prophet and he will pray for you and you shall live, but if you do not return her, know that you shall surely die, you and all who are yours.
1_20_8: So Abimelech rose early in the morning, called all his servants together, and told all these things in their hearing, and the men were terrified.
1_20_9: Then Abimelech called Abraham and said to him, "What have you done to us? How have I sinned against you, that you have brought on me and on my kingdom such a great sin? You have done things to me that ought not to be done."
1_20_10: Then Abimelech said to Abraham, "What did you see that possessed you to do this thing?"
1_20_11: And Abraham said, "Because I thought, surely there is no fear of God at all in this place, and they will kill me because of my wife."
1_20_12: Besides, she actually is my sister, the daughter of my father but not the daughter of my mother, and she became my wife.
1_20_13: And it came to pass, when God caused me to wander from my father's house, that I said to her, 'This is the loyalty you must show me: at every place wherever we go, say of me, "He is my brother."'
1_20_14: Then Abimelech took sheep, oxen, and male and female servants, and presented them to Abraham, and he restored Sarah his wife to him.
1_20_15: And Abimelech said, "Look, my land is before you; settle wherever it pleases you."
1_20_16: Then to Sarah he said, "Behold, I have given your brother a thousand pieces of silver; indeed this is a verification of your innocence before all who are with you and before everybody." Thus she was vindicated.
1_20_17: So Abraham prayed to God, and God healed Abimelech, his wife, and his female servants so that they could bear children.
1_20_18: For the LORD had completely closed up all the wombs of the house of Abimelech because of Sarah, Abraham's wife.
1_21_22: At that time Abimelech and Phicol, the commander of his army, spoke to Abraham, saying, "God is obviously with you in everything you do."
1_21_23: Now therefore, swear to me here by God that you will not deal falsely with me, with my offspring, or with my posterity; but that according to the kindness that I have shown to you, you will do to me and to the land in which you have dwelt.
1_21_24: And Abraham said, "I will swear it."
1_21_25: Then Abraham complained to Abimelech because of a well of water which Abimelech's servants had seized.
1_21_26: And Abimelech said, "I do not know who has done this thing; you did not report it to me, nor had I heard of it until today."
1_21_27: So Abraham took sheep and oxen and gave them to Abimelech, and the two men made a covenant.
1_21_28: Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock apart by themselves.
1_21_29: Then Abimelech asked Abraham, "What is the meaning of these seven ewe lambs that you have set apart by themselves?"
1_21_30: And he said, "You will accept these seven ewe lambs from my hand, that this may be a witness for me that I have dug this well."
1_21_31: Therefore he called that place Beersheba, because there the two of them swore an oath.
1_21_32: Thus they made a covenant at Beersheba, and Abimelech rose with Phichol, the commander of his army, and they returned home to the land of the Philistines.
1_21_33: Then Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba, and there he worshiped the LORD by calling on the name of the Everlasting God.
1_21_34: And Abraham sojourned in the land of the Philistines many days.
1_22_1: Now it came to pass some time after these things that God tested Abraham, and said to him, "Abraham!" And he said, "Here I am."
1_22_2: Then He said, "Take now your son, your only son Isaac, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah, and sacrifice him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains of which I will tell you."
1_22_3: So Abraham rose early in the morning and saddled his donkey, and took two of his young men with him and his son Isaac, and he split the wood for the burnt offering and set out for the place of which God had told him.
1_22_4: Then on the third day Abraham lifted up his eyes and saw the place in the distance.
1_22_5: And Abraham said to his young men, "Stay here with the donkey; the boy and I will go over there and worship, and we will come back to you."
1_22_6: So Abraham took the wood for the burnt offering and laid it on Isaac his son, and he took the fire and the knife in his hand, and the two of them walked on together.
1_22_7: But Isaac spoke to Abraham his father and said, "My father!" And he said, "Here I am, my son." Then he said, "We have the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?"
1_22_8: And Abraham answered, "My son, God will provide for Himself the lamb for a burnt offering." So the two of them walked on together.
1_22_9: When they arrived at the place of which God had told him, Abraham built an altar there and arranged the wood in order, and he bound Isaac his son and laid him on the altar, on top of the wood.
1_22_10: And Abraham reached out his hand and took the knife to slay his son.
1_22_11: But the Angel of the LORD called to him from heaven and said, "Abraham, Abraham!" And he replied, "Here I am."
1_22_12: And He said, "Do not lay your hand on the boy, or do anything to him; for now I know that you truly fear God, since you have not withheld your son, your only son, from Me."
1_22_13: Then Abraham lifted his eyes and looked, and behold, behind him was a ram caught in a thicket by its horns, so Abraham went and took the ram and offered it up as a burnt offering in place of his son.
1_22_14: And Abraham called the name of that place, The-LORD-Will-Provide, as it is said to this day, "On the mountain of the LORD it shall be provided."
1_22_15: Then the Angel of the LORD called to Abraham a second time from heaven.
1_22_16: And said, "By Myself I have sworn, declares the LORD, because you have obeyed me and done this thing, and have not withheld your son, your only son—
1_22_17: I will surely bless you and greatly multiply your descendants as the stars of heaven and as the sand which is on the seashore, and your descendants shall possess the gates of their enemies.
1_22_18: In your seed all the nations of the earth shall be blessed, because you have obeyed my command.
1_22_19: So Abraham returned to his servants, and they rose and went together to Beersheba, and Abraham continued to live at Beersheba.
1_22_20: Now it came to pass after these things that Abraham heard that Milcah also has borne children to your brother Nahor.
1_22_21: Uz his firstborn, Buz his brother, and Kemuel the father of Aram.
1_22_22: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.
1_22_23: And Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. These eight sons Milcah bore to Nahor, Abraham's brother.
1_22_24: His concubine, whose name was Reumah, also bore Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
1_25_1: Abraham took another wife, and her name was Keturah.
1_25_2: And she bore him Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
1_25_3: Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and the sons of Dedan were Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
1_25_4: The sons of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah, and all these were the descendants of Keturah.
1_25_5: Abraham gave everything he had to Isaac.
1_25_6: But Abraham gave gifts to the sons of the concubines which Abraham had, and while he was still living he sent them away from Isaac his son, eastward to the land of the east.
1_25_7: This is the sum of all the years of Abraham's life which he lived: one hundred and seventy-five years.
1_25_8: Then Abraham breathed his last and died at a ripe old age, an old man full of years and satisfied, and was gathered to his people.
1_25_9: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave of Machpelah, in the field of Ephron the son of Zohar the Hittite, east of Mamre.
1_25_10: The field which Abraham purchased from the sons of Heth, where Abraham was buried with Sarah his wife.
1_25_11: And it came to pass, after the death of Abraham, that God blessed his son Isaac, and Isaac settled at Beer Lahai Roi.
1_25_12: Now this is the account of Ishmael, Abraham's son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarah's maidservant, bore to Abraham.
1_25_13: These are the names of the sons of Ishmael, listed in the order of their birth: The firstborn of Ishmael, Nebajoth; then Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
1_25_15: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
1_25_16: These were the sons of Ishmael and these were their names, by their towns and their encampments, twelve tribal leaders according to their nations.
1_25_17: These were the years of the life of Ishmael: one hundred and thirty-seven years; and he took his last breath and died, and was gathered to his people.
1_25_18: They settled from Havilah as far as Shur, which is east of Egypt in the direction of Assyria, and he died in the presence of all his brethren.
1_25_19: This is the account of Isaac, Abraham's son, whom Abraham fathered.
1_25_20: Isaac was forty years old when he took Rebekah as his wife, the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean of Paddan Aram, the sister of Laban the Aramean.
1_25_21: Now Isaac pleaded with the LORD on behalf of his wife, because she was barren, and the LORD answered his plea, and Rebekah his wife conceived.
1_25_22: But the children struggled together within her womb, and she said, "If it is so, why is this happening to me?" So she went to inquire of the LORD.
1_25_23: And the LORD said to her, "Two nations are in your womb, two peoples shall be divided from your body; one people shall be stronger than the other, and the older shall serve the younger."
1_25_24: So when the time came to give birth, there were indeed twins in her womb.
1_25_25: And the first came out red, all over like a hairy garment, so they named him Esau.
1_25_26: Afterward his brother came out with his hand grasping Esau's heel, so his name was called Jacob, and Isaac was sixty years old when she bore them.
1_25_27: So the boys grew up, and Esau became a skillful hunter, a man of the field, but Jacob was a quiet man, dwelling in tents.
1_25_28: Isaac loved Esau because he had a taste for his game, but Rebekah loved Jacob.
1_25_29: Once when Jacob was cooking a stew, Esau came in from the field and he was exhausted.
1_25_30: And Esau said to Jacob, "Please feed me with that same red stew, for I am exhausted." Therefore his name was called Edom.
1_25_31: But Jacob said, "First sell me your birthright as of this day."
1_25_32: And Esau said, "Look, I am about to die; so what good is this birthright to me?"
1_25_33: Then Jacob said, "Swear to me first." So he swore an oath to him and sold his birthright to Jacob.
1_25_34: And Jacob gave Esau bread and lentil stew, and he ate and drank, got up, and went his way. Thus Esau despised his birthright.
1_26_1: There was a famine in the land, besides the previous famine that was in the days of Abraham, and Isaac went to Abimelech king of the Philistines in Gerar.
1_26_2: Then the LORD appeared to him and said, "Do not go down to Egypt; stay in the land of which I shall tell you.
1_26_3: Dwell in this land, and I will be with you and bless you, for to you and your descendants I will give all these lands, and I will establish the oath which I swore to Abraham your father.
1_26_4: I will make your descendants multiply as the stars of heaven; I will give to your descendants all these lands; and through your seed all the nations of the earth shall be blessed.
1_26_5: Because Abraham obeyed My voice and kept My charge, My commandments, My statutes, and My instructions.
1_26_7: When the men of the place asked about his wife, he said, "She is my sister," for he was afraid to say, "She is my wife," thinking, "lest the men of the place kill me on account of Rebekah, because she is beautiful to behold."
1_26_8: Now it came to pass, when he had been there a long time, that Abimelech king of the Philistines looked out through a window and saw Isaac caressing Rebekah his wife.
1_26_9: Then Abimelech called Isaac and said, "Quite obviously she is your wife; so how could you say, 'She is my sister'?" Isaac said to him, "Because I thought, 'Lest I die on account of her.'"
1_26_10: Abimelech said, "What is this you have done to us? One of the people might easily have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guilt upon us."
1_26_11: So Abimelech warned all his people, saying, "He who touches this man or his wife shall surely be put to death."
1_26_12: Then Isaac planted his crops in that land, and reaped in the same year a hundredfold; and the LORD blessed him.
1_26_13: The man began to prosper, and continued to grow richer until he became very wealthy.
1_26_14: He had possessions of flocks and possessions of herds and a great household of servants, so that the Philistines envied him.
1_26_15: Now the Philistines had stopped up all the wells which his father's servants had dug in the days of Abraham his father, filling them with dirt.
1_26_16: And Abimelech said to Isaac, "Go away from us, for you have become much mightier than we."
1_26_17: Then Isaac departed from there and pitched his tent in the Valley of Gerar, and settled there.
1_26_18: And Isaac dug again the wells of water which had been dug in the days of Abraham his father, for the Philistines had stopped them up after the death of Abraham, and he called them by the same names which his father had given them.
