1_46_20: And to Joseph in the land of Egypt were born Manasseh and Ephraim, whom Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera priest of On, bore to him.
1_46_21: The sons of Benjamin were Bela, Becher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard.
1_46_22: These were the sons of Rachel who were born to Jacob: fourteen persons in all.
1_46_23: The son of Dan was Hushim.
1_46_24: The sons of Naphtali were Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem.
1_46_25: These were the sons of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to his daughter Rachel, and she bore these to Jacob: seven persons in all.
1_46_26: All the persons belonging to Jacob who went with him to Egypt, who were his direct descendants from his own body, not including Jacob's sons' wives, were sixty-six persons in all.
1_46_27: And the sons of Joseph who were born to him in Egypt were two persons, and all those of the house of Jacob who came into Egypt were seventy.
1_46_28: Then he sent Judah ahead of him to Joseph, to prepare for his arrival and show the way to Goshen, and they came into the land of Goshen.
1_46_29: So Joseph prepared his chariot and went up to Goshen to meet his father Israel, and he presented himself to him and fell on his neck and wept on his neck a long time.
1_46_30: And Israel said to Joseph, "Now let me die, since I have seen your face and know that you are still alive."
1_46_31: Then Joseph said to his brothers and to his father's household, "I will go up and inform Pharaoh, and say to him, 'My brothers and those of my father's house, who were in the land of Canaan, have come to me.
1_46_32: The men are shepherds, for they have been keepers of livestock, and they have brought their flocks, their herds, and all that they have.
1_46_33: So it shall be, when Pharaoh calls you and asks, 'What is your occupation?'
1_46_34: You shall say, 'Your servants' occupation has been with livestock from our youth even until now, both we and our fathers,' so that you may dwell in the land of Goshen, for every shepherd is detestable to the Egyptians.
1_47_1: Then Joseph went in and told Pharaoh, "My father and my brothers, their flocks and their herds and all that they possess, have come from the land of Canaan; and they are now in the land of Goshen."
1_47_2: And he took five men from among his brothers and presented them before Pharaoh.
1_47_3: Then Pharaoh said to his brothers, "What is your occupation?" And they said to Pharaoh, "Your servants are shepherds, just as our fathers were."
1_47_4: And they said to Pharaoh, "We have come to sojourn in the land, because your servants have no pasture for their flocks, for the famine is severe in the land of Canaan. Now therefore, please let your servants settle in the land of Goshen."
1_47_5: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Your father and your brothers have come to you.
1_47_6: The land of Egypt is before you; settle your father and brothers in the best of the land, let them dwell in the land of Goshen, and if you know any capable men among them, then make them chief herdsmen over my livestock.
1_47_7: Then Joseph brought in his father Jacob and presented him before Pharaoh, and Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
1_47_8: Pharaoh said to Jacob, "How many are the years of your life?"
1_47_9: And Jacob said to Pharaoh, "The days of the years of my sojourning are one hundred and thirty years; few and hard have been the days of the years of my life, and they have not attained to the days of the years of the life of my fathers in the days of their sojourning."
1_47_10: So Jacob blessed Pharaoh and went out from his presence.
1_47_11: And Joseph settled his father and his brothers and gave them property in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of Rameses, as Pharaoh had commanded.
1_47_12: Then Joseph provided his father, his brothers, and all his father's household with food, according to the number of their dependents.
1_47_13: Now there was no food in all the land, for the famine was very severe, so that the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan were exhausted because of the famine.
1_47_14: And Joseph collected all the money that was found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan in exchange for the grain which they bought, and Joseph brought the money into Pharaoh's house.
1_47_15: So when the money was all spent in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan, all the Egyptians came to Joseph and said, "Give us bread, for why should we die in your presence? For the money has failed."
1_47_16: Then Joseph said, "Since your money is gone, give me your livestock, and I will give you food in exchange for your livestock."
1_47_17: So they brought their livestock to Joseph, and Joseph gave them food in exchange for the horses, the flocks, the cattle of the herds, and the donkeys, and he supplied them with food in exchange for all their livestock that year.
1_47_18: When that year had ended, they came to him the next year and said to him, "We cannot hide from my lord that our money is all gone; my lord also has our herds of livestock. There is nothing left in the sight of my lord but our bodies and our lands.
1_47_19: Why should we die here before your eyes, both we and our land? Buy us and our land in exchange for food, and we with our land will be slaves to Pharaoh; give us seed that we may live and not die, and that the land may not become desolate.
1_47_20: So Joseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh, for every Egyptian sold his field because the famine was so severe upon them, and the land became Pharaoh's.
1_47_21: And as for the people, he removed them to the cities from one end of the borders of Egypt to the other.
1_47_22: Only the land of the priests he did not buy, for the priests had a regular allowance from Pharaoh, and they lived on the allowance which Pharaoh gave them; therefore they did not sell their lands.
1_47_23: Then Joseph said to the people, "Understand that I have bought you and your land this day for Pharaoh. Now here is seed for you, and you shall sow the land.
1_47_24: And it shall come to pass at the harvest that you shall give one-fifth to Pharaoh, and four-fifths shall be your own, as seed for the field and as food for yourselves and your households and for your little ones.
1_47_25: So they said, "You have saved our lives; let us find favor in the sight of my lord, and we will be Pharaoh's slaves."
1_47_26: So Joseph made it a statute over the land of Egypt to this day, that Pharaoh should have one-fifth, except for the land of the priests only, which did not become Pharaoh's.
1_47_27: So Israel settled in the land of Egypt, in the country of Goshen, and they acquired possessions there and were fruitful and multiplied exceedingly.
1_47_28: Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years, so the length of Jacob's life was one hundred and forty-seven years in all.
1_47_29: When the time drew near that Israel must die, he called his son Joseph and said to him, "Now if I have found favor in your sight, please put your hand under my thigh and promise to deal kindly and faithfully with me. Please do not bury me in Egypt,
1_47_30: But let me lie with my fathers; you shall carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their burial place, and Joseph answered, I will do as you have said.
1_47_31: Then he said, "Swear to me," and he swore to him, so Israel bowed in worship at the head of his bed.
1_48_1: Now it came to pass after these things that Joseph was told, "Behold, your father is sick"; and he took with him his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim.
1_48_2: And Jacob was told, "Look, your son Joseph is coming to you"; and Israel summoned his strength and sat up on the bed.
1_48_3: Then Jacob said to Joseph, "God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan and blessed me,
1_48_4: and said to me, 'Behold, I will make you fruitful and multiply you, and I will make of you a company of peoples, and give this land to your descendants after you as an everlasting possession.'
1_48_5: And now your two sons, Ephraim and Manasseh, who were born to you in the land of Egypt before I came to you in Egypt, are now mine; as Reuben and Simeon, they shall belong to me.
1_48_6: Your offspring whom you beget after them shall be yours; they will be recorded by the name of their brothers in their inheritance.
1_48_7: But as for me, when I came from Padan, to my sorrow Rachel died beside me in the land of Canaan on the way, when there was still some distance to go to Ephrath; and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath, that is, Bethlehem.
1_48_8: Then Israel saw Joseph's sons and asked, "Who are these?"
1_48_9: Joseph said to his father, "They are my sons, whom God has given me here in this place," and Jacob said, "Please bring them closer to me, and I will bless them."
1_48_10: Now the eyes of Israel were dim with age, so that he could hardly see, and Joseph brought them close to him, and he kissed them and embraced them.
1_48_11: And Israel said to Joseph, "I never expected to see your face again, but in fact God has also let me see your offspring!"
1_48_12: So Joseph removed them from beside his knees, and he bowed down with his face to the ground.
1_48_13: And Joseph took them both, Ephraim with his right hand toward Israel's left hand, and Manasseh with his left hand toward Israel's right hand, and brought them close to him.
1_48_14: Then Israel stretched out his right hand and laid it on Ephraim's head, who was the younger, and his left hand on Manasseh's head, crossing his hands intentionally, for Manasseh was the firstborn.
1_48_15: And he blessed Joseph and said, "God, before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, the God who has been my shepherd all my life long to this day,
1_48_16: The Angel who has redeemed me from all harm, bless the boys; let my name be carried on in them, and the name of my fathers Abraham and Isaac; and let them grow into a multitude in the midst of the earth.
1_48_17: Now when Joseph saw that his father laid his right hand on the head of Ephraim, he thought it was a mistake, so he took hold of his father's hand to move it from Ephraim's head to Manasseh's head.
1_48_18: And Joseph said to his father, "Not that way, my father, for this one is the firstborn; place your right hand on his head."
1_48_19: But his father refused and said, "I know, my son, I know. He also shall become a people, and he also shall be great; nevertheless, his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his offspring shall become a multitude of nations."
1_48_20: So he blessed them that day, saying, "By you Israel will pronounce blessings, saying, 'May God make you as Ephraim and as Manasseh!'" And thus he put Ephraim before Manasseh.

1_49_29: Then he charged them and said to them, "I am about to be gathered to my people; bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite,

1_49_31: There they buried Abraham and Sarah his wife, there they buried Isaac and Rebekah his wife, and there I buried Leah.

1_50_1: Then Joseph fell on his father's face and wept over him and kissed him.
1_50_2: Joseph commanded his servants the physicians to embalm his father, so the physicians embalmed Israel.
1_50_3: The embalming process required forty days for him, for such is the period required for those who are embalmed, and the Egyptians mourned for him seventy days.
1_50_4: When the period of mourning was past, Joseph spoke to the household of Pharaoh, saying, "If now I have found favor in your sight, please speak to Pharaoh on my behalf, saying,
1_50_5: My father made me swear, saying, "Behold, I am about to die; in my tomb which I prepared for myself in the land of Canaan, there you shall bury me." Now therefore, please let me go up and bury my father, and I will return.
1_50_6: And Pharaoh said, "Go up and bury your father, as he made you promise."
1_50_7: So Joseph went up to bury his father, and with him went up all the officials of Pharaoh, the elders of his household, and all the elders of the land of Egypt.
1_50_8: Along with all the household of Joseph, his brothers, and his father's household, only their little ones, their flocks, and their herds they left in the land of Goshen.
1_50_9: And there went up with him both chariots and horsemen, and it was a very impressive gathering.
1_50_10: When they came to the threshing floor of Atad, which is beyond the Jordan, they lamented there with a very great and grievous mourning, and Joseph observed seven days of mourning for his father.
1_50_11: When the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, saw the mourning at the threshing floor of Atad, they said, "This is a grievous mourning of the Egyptians," therefore the place was named Abel Mizraim, which is beyond the Jordan.
1_50_12: So his sons did for him just as he had charged them.
1_50_13: For his sons carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the cave of the field of Machpelah before Mamre, which Abraham had bought along with the field from Ephron the Hittite as a permanent burial site.
1_50_14: And after he had buried his father, Joseph returned to Egypt with his brothers and all who had accompanied him to bury his father.
1_50_15: When Joseph's brothers saw that their father was dead, they said, "Perhaps Joseph will hate us and may actually pay us back for all the evil which we did to him."
1_50_16: So they sent a message to Joseph, saying, "Before your father died he commanded, saying,
1_50_17: Thus you shall say to Joseph: "I beg you, please forgive the transgression of your brothers and their sin, for they did you wrong." Now please forgive the transgression of the servants of the God of your father, and Joseph wept when they spoke to him.
1_50_18: Then his brothers also came and fell down before him, and they said, "Behold, we are your servants."
1_50_19: Joseph said to them, "Do not be afraid, for am I in the place of God?"
1_50_20: But as for you, you intended evil against me, but God intended it for good, in order to bring about this present result, to save many people alive.
1_50_21: Now therefore, do not be afraid; I will provide for you and your little ones, and he reassured them and spoke kindly to them.
1_50_22: So Joseph remained in Egypt, he and his father's household, and Joseph lived one hundred and ten years.
1_50_23: Joseph saw Ephraim's sons to the third generation, and the children of Machir the son of Manasseh were also born on Joseph's knees.
1_50_24: And Joseph said to his brothers, "I am about to die, but God will surely come to your aid and bring you up out of this land to the land of which He swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob."
1_50_25: Then Joseph made the sons of Israel swear an oath, saying, "God will surely come to your aid, and you shall carry up my bones from here."
1_50_26: So Joseph died at the age of one hundred and ten years, and they embalmed him, and his body was placed in a coffin in Egypt.
2_4_1: Then Moses answered and said, "But what if they will not believe me or listen to my voice; suppose they say, 'The LORD has not appeared to you.'"

2_4_3: And He said, "Throw it on the ground." So he threw it on the ground, and it became a serpent, and Moses fled from it.
2_4_4: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Stretch out your hand and grab it by the tail," and he stretched out his hand and caught it, and it became a staff in his hand.
2_4_5: This will take place so that they may believe that the LORD God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has appeared to you.

2_4_12: Now therefore go, and I will be with your mouth and teach you what you shall say.
2_4_13: But he said, "O my Lord, please send someone else by the hand of whomever You will."
2_4_14: Then the anger of the LORD was kindled against Moses, and He said, "Is not Aaron the Levite your brother? I know that he speaks fluently. And look, he is on his way now to meet you, and when he sees you, he will be glad in his heart."
2_4_15: You shall speak to him and put the words in his mouth, and I will be with your mouth and with his mouth, and I will teach you both what you shall do.
2_4_16: So he shall be your spokesman to the people, and he himself shall serve as a mouth for you, and you shall be to him as God.
2_4_17: And you shall take this staff in your hand, with which you shall perform the signs.
2_4_18: So Moses went and returned to Jethro his father-in-law, and said to him, "Please let me go back and return to my relatives who are in Egypt, and see whether they are still alive." And Jethro said to Moses, "Go in peace."
2_4_19: Now the LORD said to Moses in Midian, "Go, return to Egypt, for all the men who wanted to kill you are dead."
2_4_20: So Moses took his wife and his sons and had them ride on a donkey, and he returned to the land of Egypt, and Moses took the staff of God in his hand.
2_4_21: And the LORD said to Moses, "When you go back to Egypt, see that you perform all those wonders before Pharaoh which I have put in your power, but I will harden his heart so that he will not let the people go."
2_4_22: Then you shall say to Pharaoh, 'Thus says the LORD: Israel is My son, My firstborn.
2_4_23: So I say to you, let My son go that he may serve Me, but if you refuse to let him go, behold I will kill your son, your firstborn.
2_4_24: And it came to pass on the way, at the lodging place, that the LORD confronted him and sought to put him to death.
2_4_25: Then Zipporah took a flint and cut off the foreskin of her son and threw it at Moses' feet, and said, "Surely you are a bridegroom of blood to me!"
2_4_26: So He let him alone, and then she said, "You are a bridegroom of blood," referring to the circumcision.
2_4_27: And the LORD said to Aaron, "Go into the wilderness to meet Moses." So he went and met him at the mountain of God, and embraced him.
2_4_28: So Moses told Aaron everything the LORD who had sent him had commanded him to say, and all the signs which He had commanded him to perform.
2_4_29: Then Moses and Aaron went and assembled all the elders of the children of Israel.
2_4_30: And Aaron spoke all the words which the LORD had spoken to Moses, and he performed the signs in the sight of the people.
2_4_31: So the people believed, and when they heard that the LORD was concerned about the children of Israel and that He had seen their affliction, then they bowed their heads and worshiped.
2_5_1: Afterward Moses and Aaron went in and said to Pharaoh, "Thus says the LORD God of Israel: 'Let My people go, that they may hold a festival to Me in the wilderness.'"
2_5_2: And Pharaoh said, "Who is the LORD, that I should obey His voice to let Israel go? I do not know the LORD, and moreover, I will not let Israel go."
2_5_3: So they said, "The God of the Hebrews has met with us. Please, let us go a three days' journey into the wilderness and sacrifice to the LORD our God, otherwise He will fall upon us with pestilence or with the sword."

2_5_9: Let heavier work be laid on the men, that they may labor at it and pay no attention to these lies.

2_5_12: So the people scattered throughout all the land of Egypt to gather stubble to use instead of straw.
2_5_13: And the taskmasters pressed them to hurry, saying, "Complete your work, your daily quota, as when there was straw."
2_5_14: Also the Israelite foremen, whom Pharaoh's taskmasters had set over them, were beaten and were asked, "Why have you not fulfilled your quota in making brick both yesterday and today, as before?"
2_5_15: Then the foremen of the children of Israel came and cried out to Pharaoh, saying, "Why are you treating your servants this way?"
2_5_16: There is no straw given to your servants, yet they keep saying to us, 'Make bricks!' And behold, your servants are being beaten, but the fault is in your own people.
2_5_17: But he said, "You are lazy! Lazy! That is why you say, 'Let us go and sacrifice to the LORD.'"
2_5_18: Therefore go now and get back to work; for no straw will be given you, yet you must still deliver the full quota of bricks.
2_5_19: The officers of the children of Israel saw that they were in serious trouble after it was said, "You shall not reduce any bricks from your daily quota."
2_5_20: Then, as they came out from Pharaoh's presence, they met Moses and Aaron who were waiting there to meet them.
2_5_21: And they said to them, "Let the LORD look on you and judge, because you have made us odious in the sight of Pharaoh and in the sight of his servants, putting a sword in their hand to kill us."
2_5_22: So Moses returned to the LORD and said, "Lord, why have You brought trouble on this people, and why did You ever send me?"
2_5_23: For ever since I came to Pharaoh to speak in Your name, he has done evil to this people, and You have not delivered Your people at all.
2_6_1: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Now you shall see what I will do to Pharaoh, for under compulsion he will let them go, and under compulsion he will drive them out of his land."
2_6_2: God spoke to Moses and said to him, "I am Yahweh."
2_6_3: I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob as God Almighty, but I did not reveal myself to them by my name LORD.
2_6_4: I have also established My covenant with them, to give them the land of Canaan, the land where they lived as foreigners.
2_6_5: And I have also heard the groaning of the children of Israel whom the Egyptians hold in bondage, and I have remembered My covenant.
2_6_6: Therefore say to the children of Israel: I am the LORD; I will bring you out from under the burdens of the Egyptians, I will deliver you from their bondage, and I will redeem you with an outstretched arm and with great acts of judgment.
2_6_7: I will take you as My own people, and I will be your God. Then you shall know that I am the LORD your God who brings you out from under the forced labor of the Egyptians.
2_6_8: And I will bring you into the land which I swore to give to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and I will give it to you as your own possession: I am the LORD.

2_6_10: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,

2_6_26: These are the same Aaron and Moses to whom the LORD said, "Lead the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt according to their armies."
2_6_27: These are the ones who spoke to Pharaoh king of Egypt about bringing out the children of Israel from Egypt, this same Moses and Aaron.

2_7_1: So the LORD said to Moses, "See, I have made you like God to Pharaoh, and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet."
2_7_2: You shall speak all that I command you, and Aaron your brother shall tell Pharaoh to let the children of Israel go out of his land.
2_7_3: And I will harden Pharaoh's heart and multiply My miraculous signs and My wonders in the land of Egypt.
2_7_4: But Pharaoh will not listen to you, so that I may lay My hand on Egypt and bring out My armies and My people, the children of Israel, from the land of Egypt by great acts of judgment.
2_7_5: And the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD, when I stretch out My hand against Egypt and bring out the children of Israel from among them.
2_7_6: Moses and Aaron did this; they did just as the LORD had commanded them.
2_7_7: Moses was eighty years old and Aaron eighty-three years old when they made their demands to Pharaoh.
2_7_8: Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
2_7_9: When Pharaoh speaks to you, saying, 'Prove yourselves by working a miracle,' then you shall say to Aaron, 'Take your staff and throw it down before Pharaoh, and it will become a serpent.'
2_7_10: So Moses and Aaron went in to Pharaoh, and they did just as the LORD had commanded, and Aaron threw down his rod before Pharaoh and before his servants, and it became a serpent.
2_7_11: But Pharaoh also called the wise men and the sorcerers, so the magicians of Egypt also did the same thing with their secret arts.
2_7_12: For every man threw down his staff, and they turned into serpents, but Aaron's staff swallowed up their staffs.
2_7_13: And Pharaoh's heart grew hard, and he would not listen to them, as the LORD had said.
2_7_14: So the LORD said to Moses, "Pharaoh's heart is hard and stubborn; he refuses to let the people go."
2_7_15: Go to Pharaoh in the morning as he is going out to the water, and stand ready by the river's bank to meet him, and the rod which was turned to a serpent you shall take in your hand.
2_7_16: And you shall say to him, 'The LORD God of the Hebrews has sent me to you, saying, "Let My people go, that they may worship Me in the wilderness"; but so far you have not listened!
2_7_17: Thus says the LORD: "By this you shall know that I am the LORD. Behold, I will strike the water that is in the Nile with the staff that is in my hand, and it shall be turned to blood.
2_7_18: And the fish that are in the river shall die, the river will become foul, and the Egyptians will be unable to drink the water of the river.
2_7_19: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, "Say to Aaron, 'Take your staff and stretch out your hand over the waters of Egypt, over their streams, over their rivers, over their ponds, and over all their reservoirs of water, that they may become blood. And there shall be blood throughout all the land of Egypt, even in vessels of wood and pitchers of stone.'"
2_7_20: Moses and Aaron did so, just as the LORD commanded, and he lifted up the staff and struck the water that was in the Nile, in the sight of Pharaoh and in the sight of his officials, and all the water that was in the Nile was turned to blood.
2_7_21: The fish that were in the river died, the river became foul, and the Egyptians could not drink the water of the river, so there was blood throughout all the land of Egypt.
2_7_22: But the magicians of Egypt did the same with their secret arts, and Pharaoh's heart remained hardened, and he did not listen to them, as the LORD had said.
2_7_23: And Pharaoh turned and went into his house, and he did not take even this to heart.
2_7_24: So all the Egyptians dug along the Nile for water to drink, because they could not drink the water of the river.
2_7_25: Seven full days passed after the LORD had struck the Nile.
2_8_1: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, "Go in to Pharaoh and say to him, 'Thus says the LORD: Let My people go, that they may serve Me.
2_8_2: But if you refuse to let them go, behold, I will plague all your territory with frogs.
2_8_3: The Nile will swarm with frogs which will come up and go into your palace, into your bedroom and on your bed, and into the houses of your officials and your people, and into your ovens and into your kneading bowls.
2_8_4: And the frogs shall come up on you, on your people, and on all your officials.
2_8_5: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Tell Aaron, 'Stretch out your hand with your staff over the streams, over the rivers, and over the ponds, and cause frogs to come up on the land of Egypt.'"
2_8_6: So Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt, and the frogs came up and covered the whole land of Egypt.
2_8_7: And the magicians did the same with their secret arts, and brought up frogs on the land of Egypt.
2_8_8: Then Pharaoh summoned Moses and Aaron and said, "Plead with the LORD to take away the frogs from me and from my people, and I will let the people go that they may sacrifice to the LORD."
2_8_9: And Moses said to Pharaoh, "Accept the honor of telling me when I shall plead for you, for your servants, and for your people, that the frogs be cut off from you and your houses, and remain in the Nile only."
2_8_10: So he said, "Tomorrow." And Moses said, "Let it be according to your word, so that you may know that there is no one like the LORD our God.
2_8_11: The frogs shall depart from you, from your houses, from your officials, and from your people. They shall remain only in the Nile.
2_8_12: Then Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh, and Moses cried out to the LORD for help concerning the frogs which He had brought against Pharaoh.
2_8_13: So the LORD did according to the word of Moses, and the frogs died out of the houses, out of the courtyards, and out of the fields.
2_8_14: They gathered them together in heaps, and a terrible stench filled the land.
2_8_15: But when Pharaoh saw that there was relief, he hardened his heart and would not listen to them, as the LORD had said.
2_8_16: So the LORD said to Moses, "Tell Aaron, 'Stretch out your staff and strike the dust of the earth, so that it may become gnats throughout all the land of Egypt.'"
2_8_17: And they did so, for Aaron stretched out his hand with his staff and struck the dust of the earth, and there were gnats on man and beast, and all the dust of the land became gnats throughout all the land of Egypt.
2_8_18: Now the magicians tried with their secret arts to bring forth lice, but they could not. So there were lice on man and beast.
2_8_19: Then the magicians said to Pharaoh, "This is the finger of God." But Pharaoh's heart remained hard, and he did not listen to them, just as the LORD had predicted.
2_8_20: And the LORD said to Moses, "Rise early in the morning and present yourself before Pharaoh as he comes out to the water, then say to him, 'Thus says the LORD: Let My people go, that they may serve Me.
2_8_21: Or else, if you will not let My people go, behold, I will send swarms of flies on you and your servants, on your people and into your houses, and the houses of the Egyptians shall be filled with swarms of flies, and also the ground where they live.
2_8_22: And on that day I will set apart the land of Goshen, where My people dwell, so that no swarms of flies shall be there, in order that you may know that I am the LORD in the midst of the earth.
2_8_23: I will make a clear distinction between My people and your people, and tomorrow this sign shall happen.
2_8_24: And the LORD did so, and thick swarms of flies came into the house of Pharaoh, into his servants' houses, and into all the land of Egypt, and the land was ruined because of the swarms of flies.
2_8_25: Then Pharaoh summoned Moses and Aaron, and said, "Go, sacrifice to your God within the land."
2_8_26: And Moses said, "It would not be right to do so, for we would be sacrificing what is detestable to the Egyptians to the LORD our God. If we sacrifice what is an abomination to the Egyptians before their eyes, will they not stone us?"
2_8_27: We must go three days' journey into the wilderness and sacrifice to the LORD our God as He commands us.
2_8_28: So Pharaoh said, "I will let you go, that you may sacrifice to the LORD your God in the wilderness; only you shall not go very far away. Plead for me."
2_8_29: Then Moses said, "Indeed I am going out from you, and I will plead with the LORD that the swarms of flies may depart tomorrow from Pharaoh, from his servants, and from his people, but let Pharaoh not act deceitfully anymore in not letting the people go to sacrifice to the LORD."
2_8_30: So Moses went out from Pharaoh and pleaded with the LORD.
2_8_31: And the LORD did according to the word of Moses; He removed the swarms of flies from Pharaoh, from his officials, and from his people. Not one remained.
2_8_32: But Pharaoh hardened his heart this time also; neither would he let the people go.
2_9_1: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Go in to Pharaoh and speak to him, 'Thus says the LORD God of the Hebrews: Let My people go, that they may serve Me.
2_9_2: For if you refuse to let them go and continue to hold them,
2_9_3: Behold, the hand of the LORD will strike your livestock in the field, on the horses, on the donkeys, on the camels, on the oxen, and on the sheep—a very severe pestilence.
2_9_4: And the LORD will make a distinction between the livestock of Israel and the livestock of Egypt, so that nothing shall die of all that belongs to the children of Israel.
2_9_5: Then the LORD appointed a definite time, saying, "Tomorrow the LORD will do this thing in the land."
2_9_6: So the LORD did this thing the next morning, and all the livestock of Egypt died, but of the livestock of the children of Israel, not one died.
2_9_7: Then Pharaoh sent messengers, and indeed, not even one of the livestock of the Israelites was dead, but Pharaoh's heart became hard and he did not let the people go.
2_9_8: So the LORD said to Moses and Aaron, "Take for yourselves handfuls of soot from a furnace, and let Moses throw it toward the heavens in the sight of Pharaoh."
2_9_9: And it will become fine dust over all the land of Egypt, and it will cause festering boils that break out in sores on man and beast throughout all the land of Egypt.
2_9_10: So they took soot from the furnace and stood before Pharaoh, and Moses threw it toward heaven, and it became festering boils breaking out in sores on people and animals alike.
2_9_11: The magicians could not stand before Moses because of the boils, for the boils had broken out on the magicians and on all the Egyptians.
2_9_12: But the LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh, and he refused to listen to them, just as the LORD had predicted to Moses.
2_9_13: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Rise early in the morning and present yourself before Pharaoh, and say to him, 'Thus says the LORD God of the Hebrews: Let My people go, that they may worship Me.
2_9_14: For at this time I will send all My plagues against your very heart, and on your servants and on your people, so that you may know that there is none like Me in all the earth.
2_9_15: For by now I could have stretched out My hand and struck you and your people with pestilence, and you would have been cut off from the earth.
2_9_16: But indeed for this purpose I have allowed you to remain, that I may show My power in you, and that My name may be proclaimed in all the earth.
2_9_17: You are still exalting yourself against My people and will not let them go.
2_9_18: Behold, tomorrow about this time I will send very heavy hail to rain down, such as has not been in Egypt from the day it was founded until now.
2_9_19: Therefore send now and gather your livestock and all that you have in the field into safe shelter, for the hail shall come down on every man and every animal which is found in the field and is not brought home, and they shall die.
2_9_20: He who feared the word of the LORD among the servants of Pharaoh hurried his servants and his livestock to flee into the houses.
2_9_21: But whoever did not pay attention to the word of the LORD left his servants and his livestock in the field.
2_9_22: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Stretch out your hand toward heaven, so that there may be hail in all the land of Egypt—on man, on beast, and on every plant of the field, throughout the land of Egypt."
2_9_23: And Moses stretched out his rod toward heaven, and the LORD sent thunder and hail, and lightning flashed to the ground, and the LORD rained hail on the land of Egypt.
2_9_24: So there was hail, and fire flashing continually in the midst of the hail, very severe, such as had never been in all the land of Egypt since it became a nation.
2_9_25: And the hail struck throughout the whole land of Egypt, everything that was in the field, both man and beast, and the hail struck down every plant of the field and shattered every tree of the field.
2_9_26: Only in the land of Goshen, where the children of Israel lived, there was no hail.
2_9_27: Then Pharaoh sent and called for Moses and Aaron, and said to them, "This time I have sinned; the LORD is righteous, and I and my people are wicked."
2_9_28: Plead with the LORD, for there has been enough of this mighty thunder and hail, and I will let you go and you shall stay no longer.
2_9_29: So Moses said to him, "As soon as I have gone out of the city, I will stretch out my hands to the LORD; the thunder will cease, and there will be no more hail, so that you may know that the earth belongs to the LORD."
2_9_30: But as for you and your officials, I know that you do not yet fear the LORD God.
2_9_31: Now the flax and the barley were ruined, for the barley was in the ear and the flax was in bud.
2_9_32: But the wheat and the spelt were not struck down, for they ripen late.
2_9_33: So Moses went out of the city from Pharaoh and spread out his hands to the LORD; then the thunder and the hail ceased, and the rain no longer poured on the earth.
2_9_34: But when Pharaoh saw that the rain, the hail, and the thunder had ceased, he sinned again and hardened his heart, he and his officials.
2_9_35: So the heart of Pharaoh was hard, and he refused to let the children of Israel go, just as the LORD had spoken through Moses.
2_10_1: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Go in to Pharaoh, for I have hardened his heart and the hearts of his servants, that I may perform these miraculous signs of Mine among them.
2_10_2: And that you may tell in the hearing of your son and your son's son how I dealt severely with the Egyptians and My signs which I have done among them, that you may know that I am the LORD.
2_10_3: So Moses and Aaron came in to Pharaoh and said to him, "Thus says the LORD God of the Hebrews: How long will you refuse to humble yourself before Me? Let My people go, that they may worship and serve Me.
2_10_4: Or else, if you refuse to let My people go, behold, tomorrow I will bring a swarm of locusts into your territory.
2_10_5: They shall cover the face of the land so that no one will be able to see the ground, and they shall devour the remainder of what escaped and is left to you from the hail, and they shall eat every tree which grows up for you out of the field.
2_10_6: They will fill your houses, the houses of all your servants, and the houses of all the Egyptians—something which neither your fathers nor your grandfathers have seen, from the day that they came upon the earth to this day, and he turned and went out from Pharaoh's presence.
2_10_7: Then Pharaoh's servants said to him, "How long shall this man be a snare to us? Let the men go, that they may serve the LORD their God. Do you not yet realize that Egypt is destroyed?"
2_10_8: So Moses and Aaron were brought back to Pharaoh, and he said to them, "Go, serve the LORD your God. But who exactly are the ones that are going?"
2_10_9: Moses said, "We will go with our young and our old, with our sons and our daughters, with our flocks and our herds we will go, for we must hold a festival to the LORD."
2_10_10: Then he said to them, "The LORD had better be with you when I let you and your little ones go! Beware, for you have some evil purpose in mind.
2_10_11: Not so! Go now, you who are men, and serve the LORD, for that is what you requested, and they were driven out from Pharaoh's presence.
2_10_12: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Stretch out your hand over the land of Egypt for the locusts, so that they may come upon the land of Egypt and devour every plant of the land—all that the hail has left."
2_10_13: So Moses stretched out his rod over the land of Egypt, and the LORD directed an east wind on the land all that day and all that night, and when it was morning, the east wind had brought the locusts.
2_10_14: And the locusts went up over all the land of Egypt and settled on all the territory of Egypt in dense swarms; previously there had been no such locusts as they, nor shall there be such after them.
2_10_15: For they covered the face of the whole land so that the land was darkened, and they devoured every plant of the land and all the fruit of the trees which the hail had left, so there remained nothing green on the trees or on the plants of the field throughout all the land of Egypt.
2_10_16: Then Pharaoh hastily called for Moses and Aaron and said, "I have sinned against the LORD your God and against you."
2_10_17: Now therefore, please forgive my sin only this once, and plead with the LORD your God, that He may take away from me this death only.
2_10_18: So he went out from Pharaoh's presence and earnestly entreated the LORD.
2_10_19: And the LORD shifted the wind to a very strong west wind, which lifted the locusts and drove them into the Red Sea, and not a single locust was left in all the territory of Egypt.
2_10_20: But the LORD hardened Pharaoh's heart again, and he did not let the children of Israel go.
2_10_21: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Stretch out your hand toward heaven, that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt, a darkness so thick it can be felt."
2_10_22: So Moses stretched out his hand toward heaven, and there was thick darkness throughout all the land of Egypt for three days.
2_10_23: They did not see one another, nor did anyone move from his place for three days, but all the children of Israel had light in their dwellings.
2_10_24: Then Pharaoh summoned Moses and said, "Go, serve the LORD; only let your flocks and your herds be kept back. Let your little ones also go with you."
2_10_25: But Moses said, "You must also provide us with sacrifices and burnt offerings, that we may sacrifice to the LORD our God.
2_10_26: Our livestock also shall go with us; not a hoof shall be left behind, for we must take some of them to worship the LORD our God, and we ourselves do not know with what we must serve the LORD until we arrive there.
2_10_27: But the LORD hardened Pharaoh's heart once more, and he was unwilling to let them go.
2_10_28: Then Pharaoh said to him, "Get away from me! Beware and see my face no more, for on the day you see my face you shall die!"
2_10_29: So Moses said, "You are right. I will never see your face again."
2_11_1: Then the LORD said to Moses, "I will bring one more plague on Pharaoh and on Egypt, and afterward he will let you go from here; when he lets you go, he will surely drive you out of here completely."
2_11_2: Speak now in the hearing of the people, that every man ask from his neighbor and every woman from her neighbor for articles of silver and gold.
2_11_3: And the LORD gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians, and Moses himself was very great and highly regarded in the land of Egypt, in the sight of Pharaoh's servants and in the sight of the people.
2_11_4: Then Moses said, "Thus says the LORD: 'About midnight tonight I will go out throughout the midst of Egypt;
2_11_5: And all the firstborn in the land of Egypt shall die, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sits on his throne, even to the firstborn of the slave girl who is behind the handmill, and all the firstborn of the livestock.
2_11_6: Then there shall be a great cry of anguish throughout all the land of Egypt, such as has never been before nor ever will be again.
2_11_7: But against none of the children of Israel shall a dog move its tongue, against man or beast, that you may know that the LORD makes a distinction between the Egyptians and Israel.
2_11_8: And all these your servants shall come down to me and bow down to me, saying, 'Get out, you and all the people who follow you!' After that I will go out." Then he went out from Pharaoh in hot anger.
2_11_9: But the LORD said to Moses, "Pharaoh will not listen to you, so that My wonders may be multiplied in the land of Egypt."
2_11_10: So Moses and Aaron performed all these wonders before Pharaoh, yet the LORD hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not let the children of Israel go out of his land.
2_12_1: Now the LORD said to Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt,
2_12_2: This month shall be for you the beginning of months; it shall be the first month of the year to you.
2_12_3: Speak to all the congregation of Israel, saying: On the tenth day of this month every man shall take for himself a lamb, according to the house of his father, a lamb for a household.
2_12_4: And if the household is too small for the lamb, let him and his neighbor nearest to his house take it according to the number of persons; according to what each can eat you shall make your count for the lamb.
2_12_5: Your lamb shall be without blemish, an unblemished male a year old; you may take it from the sheep or from the goats.
2_12_6: Now you shall keep it until the fourteenth day of the same month, then the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel shall slaughter it at twilight.
2_12_7: And they shall take some of the blood and put it on the two doorposts and on the lintel of the houses where they eat the lamb.
2_12_8: They shall eat the flesh that same night, roasted over the fire, with unleavened bread and bitter herbs they shall eat it.
2_12_9: Do not eat any of it raw, nor boiled at all with water, but roasted in fire—its head with its legs and its inner parts.
2_12_10: You shall let none of it remain until morning, and anything that remains of it until morning you shall burn with fire.
2_12_11: And thus you shall eat it: with a belt fastened on your waist, your sandals on your feet, and your staff in your hand, and you shall eat it in haste, for it is the LORD's Passover.
2_12_12: For I will pass through the land of Egypt on that night, and will strike down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both man and beast; and against all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgments: I am the LORD.
2_12_13: The blood shall be a sign for you on the houses where you live, and when I see the blood I will pass over you, and no plague will befall you to destroy you when I strike the land of Egypt.
2_12_14: So this day shall be to you a memorial, and you shall celebrate it as a feast to the LORD throughout your generations as a permanent ordinance forever.
2_12_15: Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on the first day you shall remove all leaven from your houses, for whoever eats anything leavened from the first day until the seventh day, that person shall be cut off from Israel.
2_12_16: On the first day there shall be a sacred convocation, and on the seventh day there shall be a sacred convocation for you; no work at all shall be done on those days, but what everyone needs to eat—that alone may be prepared by you.
2_12_17: So you shall observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread, for on this very day I will have brought your armies out of the land of Egypt. Therefore you shall observe this day throughout your generations as an everlasting ordinance.
2_12_18: In the first month, from the evening of the fourteenth day of the month, you shall eat unleavened bread until the evening of the twenty-first day of the month.
2_12_19: For seven days no leaven shall be found in your houses, since whoever eats what is leavened, that same person shall be cut off from the congregation of Israel, whether he is a foreigner or a native of the land.
2_12_20: You shall eat nothing leavened; in all your homes you shall eat unleavened bread.
2_12_21: Then Moses called for all the elders of Israel and said to them, "Go and select lambs for yourselves according to your families, and slaughter the Passover lamb.
2_12_22: You shall take a bunch of hyssop, dip it in the blood that is in the basin, and brush the lintel and the two doorposts with the blood that is in the basin, and none of you shall go out the door of his house until morning.
2_12_23: For the LORD will pass through the land to strike the Egyptians, and when He sees the blood on the lintel and on the two doorposts, the LORD will pass over the door and will not permit the destroyer to enter your houses to strike you.
2_12_24: And you shall observe this thing as a permanent ordinance for you and your sons forever.
2_12_25: And when you enter the land which the LORD will give you, just as He has promised, you shall observe this ceremony.
2_12_26: And it shall be, when your children ask you, 'What do you mean by this ceremony?'
2_12_27: You shall say, 'It is the Passover sacrifice to the LORD, who passed over the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt when He struck the Egyptians and spared our households.'" So the people bowed their heads and worshiped.
2_12_28: Then the people of Israel went away and did so; just as the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.
2_12_29: At midnight the LORD struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who sat on his throne to the firstborn of the captive who was in the dungeon, and all the firstborn of livestock.
2_12_30: So Pharaoh rose in the night, he, all his officials, and all the Egyptians, and there was a great cry throughout Egypt, for there was not a house where there was not one dead.
2_12_31: Then he summoned Moses and Aaron by night, and said, "Rise, go out from among my people, both you and the children of Israel, and go, worship the LORD as you have said.
2_12_32: Take both your flocks and your herds, as you have said, and be gone, and bless me also.
2_12_33: And the Egyptians urgently pressured the people to send them out of the land in haste, for they said, "We shall all be dead."
2_12_34: So the people took their dough before it was leavened, with their kneading bowls wrapped up in their clothes on their shoulders.
2_12_35: Now the children of Israel had done according to the word of Moses, and they had requested from the Egyptians articles of silver, articles of gold, and clothing.
2_12_36: And the LORD had given the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians, so that they gave them what they requested, and thus they plundered the Egyptians.
2_12_37: Then the children of Israel journeyed from Rameses to Succoth, about six hundred thousand able-bodied men on foot, besides women and children.
2_12_38: A mixed multitude went up with them also, along with flocks and herds—a very large number of livestock.
2_12_39: And they baked unleavened cakes of the dough which they had brought out of Egypt, for it was not leavened, because they were driven out of Egypt and could not delay, nor had they prepared any provisions for themselves.
2_12_40: Now the sojourn of the people of Israel who lived in Egypt was four hundred and thirty years.
2_12_41: And it came to pass at the end of the four hundred and thirty years—on that very same day—all the divisions of the LORD went out from the land of Egypt.
2_12_42: It is a night of vigil to be observed for the LORD for bringing them out of the land of Egypt; this same night of the LORD is a solemn observance for all the children of Israel throughout their generations.
2_12_43: And the LORD said to Moses and Aaron, "This is the ordinance of the Passover: No foreigner is allowed to eat it.
2_12_44: But every man's slave purchased with money, after you have circumcised him, then he may eat of it.
2_12_45: A temporary resident and a hired servant shall not eat it.
2_12_46: In one house it shall be eaten; you shall not carry any of the flesh outside the house, nor shall you break any of its bones.
2_12_47: The whole congregation of Israel shall celebrate it.
2_12_48: And when a stranger dwells with you and wants to celebrate the Passover to the LORD, let all his males be circumcised, and then let him come near and keep it; and he shall be as a native of the land. For no uncircumcised person shall eat it.
2_12_49: One law shall apply to the native-born and to the stranger who sojourns among you.
2_12_50: Thus all the children of Israel did just as the LORD commanded Moses and Aaron, so they did.
2_12_51: And it came to pass, on that very same day, that the LORD brought the people of Israel out of the land of Egypt according to their divisions.
2_13_1: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
2_13_2: Consecrate to Me all the firstborn, the first offspring to open the womb among the children of Israel, both of man and beast; it is Mine.
2_13_3: And Moses said to the people, "Remember this day in which you went out of Egypt, out of the house of slavery, for by a powerful hand the LORD brought you out from this place. No leavened bread shall be eaten."
2_13_4: On this day you are going out, in the month of Abib in early spring.
2_13_5: And it shall be, when the LORD brings you into the land of the Canaanites and the Hittites and the Amorites and the Hivites and the Jebusites, which He swore to your fathers to give you, a land flowing with milk and honey, that you shall observe this service in this month.
2_13_6: For seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, and on the seventh day there shall be a festival to the LORD.
2_13_7: Unleavened bread shall be eaten for seven days, and no leavened bread shall be seen among you, nor shall any leaven be found among you in all your territory.
2_13_8: And you shall explain to your son on that day, saying, 'It is because of what the LORD did for me when I came out of Egypt.'
2_13_9: It shall be as a sign to you on your hand and as a reminder between your eyes, that the LORD's law may be in your mouth, for with a strong hand the LORD has brought you out of Egypt.
2_13_10: You shall therefore keep this ordinance at its appointed time from year to year.
2_13_11: And it shall be, when the LORD brings you into the land of the Canaanites, as He swore to you and your ancestors, and gives it to you,
2_13_12: You shall devote to the LORD all that first opens the womb, that is, every firstborn male that comes from the livestock which you own; they belong to the LORD.
2_13_13: But every firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb, and if you will not redeem it, then you shall break its neck, and all the firstborn of man among your sons you shall redeem.
2_13_14: So it shall be, when your son asks you in time to come, saying, 'What does this mean?' that you shall say to him, 'By strength of his mighty hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt, out of the place of slavery.'
2_13_15: And it came to pass, when Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, that the LORD killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both the firstborn of man and the firstborn of beast, therefore I sacrifice to the LORD all males that open the womb, but all the firstborn of my sons I redeem.
2_13_16: It shall be as a sign on your hand and as frontlets between your eyes, for by a mighty hand the LORD brought us out of Egypt.
2_13_17: When Pharaoh had let the people go, God did not lead them by way of the land of the Philistines, although that was the shortest route, for God said, "Lest the people change their minds when they face war and return to Egypt."
2_13_18: So God led the people around by way of the wilderness toward the Red Sea, and the children of Israel went up equipped for battle out of the land of Egypt.
2_13_19: And Moses took the bones of Joseph with him, for he had made the children of Israel solemnly swear, saying, "God will surely come to your aid, and you shall carry up my bones from here with you."
2_13_20: So they took their journey from Succoth and camped at Etham on the edge of the wilderness.
2_13_21: And the LORD went ahead of them by day in a pillar of cloud to lead them along the way, and by night in a pillar of fire to give them light, so that they might travel by day and by night.
2_13_22: He did not remove the pillar of cloud by day or the pillar of fire by night from its place before the people.
2_14_1: Now the LORD gave these instructions to Moses, saying:
2_14_2: Tell the children of Israel to turn back and camp before Pi Hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, opposite Baal Zephon; you shall camp facing it by the sea.
2_14_3: For Pharaoh will say of the children of Israel, 'They are wandering aimlessly in the land; the wilderness has shut them in.'
2_14_4: Then I will harden Pharaoh's heart, so that he will pursue them; and I will gain glory over Pharaoh and over all his army, that the Egyptians may know that I am the LORD." And they did so.
2_14_5: When it was reported to the king of Egypt that the people had fled, the heart of Pharaoh and his officials was turned against the people, and they said, "What have we done, letting Israel go from serving us?"
2_14_6: So he made ready his chariot and took his army with him.
2_14_7: Also, he took six hundred select chariots, and all the other chariots of Egypt with officers over every one of them.
2_14_8: And the LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he pursued after the children of Israel while the children of Israel went out with boldness and defiance.
2_14_9: So the Egyptians chased after them, all the horses and chariots of Pharaoh, his horsemen and his army, and overtook them encamped by the sea beside Pi Hahiroth, in front of Baal Zephon.
2_14_10: And when Pharaoh drew near, the children of Israel lifted their eyes, and behold, the Egyptians were marching after them, so they became very afraid and the children of Israel cried out to the LORD.
2_14_11: Then they said to Moses, "Is it because there were no graves in Egypt that you have taken us away to die in the wilderness? Why have you dealt with us in this way, bringing us out of Egypt?"
2_14_12: Isn't this what we told you in Egypt, saying, 'Leave us alone that we may serve the Egyptians'? For it would have been better for us to serve the Egyptians than to die in the wilderness.
2_14_13: And Moses said to the people, "Do not be afraid, stand firm and see the salvation of the LORD, which He will accomplish for you today, for the Egyptians whom you see today, you will never see again."
2_14_14: The LORD will fight for you, and you need only to be silent.
2_14_15: And the LORD said to Moses, "Why do you cry out to Me? Tell the children of Israel to go forward.
2_14_16: But lift up your staff and stretch out your hand over the sea and divide it, and the children of Israel shall go through the midst of the sea on dry ground.
2_14_17: And I indeed will harden the hearts of the Egyptians so that they will charge in after them, and I will gain glory over Pharaoh and over all his army, his chariots, and his horsemen.
2_14_18: Then the Egyptians shall know that I am the LORD, when I have gained glory for Myself over Pharaoh, his chariots, and his horsemen.
2_14_19: And the Angel of God, who had been going before the camp of Israel, moved and went behind them; and the pillar of cloud moved from before them and stood behind them.
2_14_20: So it came between the camp of the Egyptians and the camp of Israel, and there was the cloud and darkness to the one, and it gave light by night to the other, so that the one did not come near the other all that night.
2_14_21: Then Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and the LORD drove the sea back by a strong east wind all that night and turned the sea into dry land, and the waters were divided.
2_14_22: So the children of Israel walked through the midst of the sea on dry ground, and the waters were a wall to them on their right hand and on their left.
2_14_23: And the Egyptians pursued and went in after them into the midst of the sea, all Pharaoh's horses, his chariots, and his charioteers.
2_14_24: Now it came to pass, in the morning watch, that the LORD looked down upon the army of the Egyptians through the pillar of fire and cloud, and He threw the army of the Egyptians into confusion.
2_14_25: And He caused their chariot wheels to swerve, so that they drove them with difficulty; and the Egyptians said, "Let us flee from the face of Israel, for the LORD fights for them against the Egyptians."
2_14_26: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Stretch out your hand over the sea so that the waters may come back upon the Egyptians, on their chariots and on their horsemen."
2_14_27: So Moses stretched out his hand over the sea, and when the morning appeared the sea returned to its normal depth, while the Egyptians were fleeing right into it, and the LORD overthrew the Egyptians in the midst of the sea.
2_14_28: Then the waters returned and covered the chariots, the horsemen, and the entire army of Pharaoh that came into the sea after them, and not a single one of them remained.
2_14_29: But the children of Israel had walked through on dry ground in the midst of the sea, and the waters were a wall to them on their right hand and on their left.
2_14_30: So the LORD saved Israel that day from the hand of the Egyptians, and Israel saw the Egyptians dead on the seashore.
2_14_31: Thus Israel saw the great power which the LORD had used against the Egyptians, so the people feared the LORD and believed in the LORD and in His servant Moses.
2_15_1: Then Moses and the children of Israel sang this song to the LORD, saying, "I will sing to the LORD, for He has triumphed gloriously! The horse and its rider He has hurled into the sea!"
2_15_2: The LORD is my strength and my song, and He has become my salvation; this is my God, and I will praise Him, my father's God, and I will exalt Him.
2_15_3: The LORD is a warrior; the LORD is His name.
2_15_4: Pharaoh's chariots and his army He has cast into the sea; His elite captains also are drowned in the Red Sea.
2_15_5: The depths have covered them; they went down to the bottom like a stone.
2_15_6: Your right hand, O LORD, has become glorious in power; Your right hand, O LORD, has shattered the enemy in pieces.
2_15_7: And in the greatness of Your majesty You have overthrown those who rose against You; You unleashed Your burning wrath and it consumed them like stubble.
2_15_8: At the blast of Your nostrils the waters were piled up, the surging floods stood upright like a heap, the depths congealed in the heart of the sea.
2_15_9: The enemy said, 'I will pursue, I will overtake, I will divide the spoil; my desire shall have its fill of them. I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.'
2_15_10: You blew with Your breath, the sea covered them; they sank like lead in the mighty waters.
2_15_11: Who is like You, O LORD, among the gods? Who is like You, majestic in holiness, awesome in glorious deeds, performing wonders?
2_15_12: You stretched out Your right hand and the earth swallowed them.
2_15_13: You in Your unfailing love have led forth the people whom You have redeemed; You have guided them in Your strength to Your holy dwelling.
2_15_14: The peoples will hear and tremble; anguish will seize the inhabitants of Philistia.
2_15_15: Then the chiefs of Edom will be dismayed; trembling will seize the leaders of Moab; all the inhabitants of Canaan will melt away.
2_15_16: Fear and dread will fall on them; by the greatness of Your arm they will be motionless as stone, till Your people pass over, O LORD, till the people pass over whom You have purchased.
2_15_17: You will bring them in and plant them in the mountain of Your inheritance, in the place, O LORD, which You have prepared for Your own dwelling, the sanctuary, O Lord, which Your hands have established.
2_15_18: The LORD shall reign forever and ever.
2_15_19: For when the horses of Pharaoh went with his chariots and his horsemen into the sea, the LORD brought back the waters of the sea upon them, but the children of Israel walked on dry ground through the midst of the sea.
2_15_20: Then Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a tambourine in her hand, and all the women came out following her with tambourines and dancing.
2_15_21: And Miriam sang to them: "Sing to the LORD, for He has triumphed gloriously! The horse and its rider He has hurled into the sea!"
2_15_22: So Moses led Israel from the Red Sea, then they went out into the Wilderness of Shur and journeyed three days in the wilderness and found no water.
2_15_25: So he cried out to the LORD, and the LORD showed him a tree, and he threw it into the waters, and the waters were made sweet. There He made a statute and ordinance for them, and there He tested them,
2_15_26: And he said, "If you diligently listen to the voice of the LORD your God and do what is right in His sight, pay attention to His commandments and keep all His statutes, I will put none of the diseases on you which I have brought on the Egyptians, for I am the LORD who heals you."
2_15_27: Then they came to Elim, where there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, so they camped there beside the waters.
2_16_1: And they journeyed from Elim, and the whole congregation of the children of Israel came to the Wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after they departed from the land of Egypt.
2_16_2: Then the whole congregation of the children of Israel grumbled against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness.
2_16_3: And the children of Israel said to them, "If only we had died by the hand of the LORD in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the pots of meat and when we ate bread to the full, for you have brought us out into this wilderness to kill this whole assembly with hunger."
2_16_4: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you, and the people shall go out and gather a day's portion every day, that I may test them, whether they will walk in My instruction or not."
2_16_5: And it shall be on the sixth day that they shall prepare what they bring in, and it will be twice as much as they gather on other days.
2_16_6: Then Moses and Aaron said to all the children of Israel, "At evening you shall know that it was the LORD who brought you out of the land of Egypt.
2_16_7: And in the morning you shall see the glory of the LORD, because He hears your grumblings against the LORD. But what are we, that you complain against us?
2_16_8: Moses said, "This shall be seen when the LORD gives you meat to eat in the evening and in the morning bread to satisfy you to the full, for the LORD hears your grumbling which you make against Him. And what are we? Your grumbling is not against us but against the LORD."
2_16_9: Then Moses said to Aaron, "Say to all the congregation of the children of Israel, 'Come near before the LORD, for He has heard your grumbling.'"
2_16_10: And as Aaron spoke to the whole community of the children of Israel, they looked toward the wilderness, and behold, the glory of the LORD appeared in the cloud.
2_16_11: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
2_16_12: I have heard the grumbling of the children of Israel. Speak to them, saying, 'At twilight you shall eat meat, and in the morning you shall be filled with bread. Then you shall know that I am the LORD your God.'
2_16_13: So it came about that quails came up at evening and covered the camp, and in the morning there was a layer of dew all around the camp.
2_16_14: When the layer of dew evaporated, there on the surface of the wilderness was a fine flaky substance, as fine as frost on the ground.
2_16_15: So when the children of Israel saw it, they asked one another, "What is it?" For they did not know what it was, and Moses told them, "This is the bread which the LORD has given you to eat."
2_16_16: This is what the LORD has commanded: Let every man gather it according to how much he can eat, one omer per person, according to the number of people in his tent.
2_16_17: Then the children of Israel did as they were told and gathered, some more, some less.
2_16_18: So when they measured it by omers, he who gathered much had nothing left over, and he who gathered little had no lack; each one gathered as much as he needed to eat.
2_16_19: And Moses said to them, "Let no one keep any of it till morning."
2_16_20: However they did not listen to Moses, and some of them left part of it until morning, and it bred worms and became foul, and Moses was angry with them.
2_16_21: So they gathered it every morning, each person according to his need, and when the sun grew hot, it melted.
2_16_22: And so it was, on the sixth day, that they gathered twice as much bread, two omers for each person, and all the leaders of the congregation came and reported this to Moses.
2_16_23: Then he said to them, "This is what the LORD has commanded: Tomorrow is a Sabbath observance, a holy Sabbath to the LORD. Bake what you will bake today, and boil what you will boil, and set aside all that remains to be kept until morning."
2_16_24: So they laid it up till morning, as Moses commanded, and it did not become foul, nor were there any worms in it.
2_16_25: Then Moses said, "Eat this food today, for today is a Sabbath to the LORD; today you will not find it in the field."
2_16_26: Six days you shall gather it, but on the seventh day, which is the Sabbath, there will be none.
2_16_27: Now it happened that some of the people went out anyway on the seventh day to gather, but they found none.
2_16_28: And the LORD said to Moses, "How long will you refuse to keep My commandments and My instructions?"
2_16_29: See! For the LORD has given you the Sabbath; therefore He gives you on the sixth day bread for two days. Each of you remain in your place; let no one go out of his place on the seventh day.
2_16_30: So the people rested on the seventh day.
2_16_31: The house of Israel named the substance Manna, and it was like white coriander seed, and the taste of it was like wafers made with honey.
2_16_32: Then Moses said, "This is what the LORD has commanded: Fill an omer with it, to be preserved for your generations, that they may see the bread with which I fed you in the wilderness, when I brought you out of the land of Egypt."
2_16_33: And Moses said to Aaron, "Take a jar and put an omer of manna in it, and place it before the LORD, to be preserved throughout your generations."
2_16_34: As the LORD commanded Moses, so Aaron placed it before the Testimony, to be preserved.
2_16_35: The children of Israel ate manna forty years, until they came to an inhabited land where they would settle; they ate manna until they came to the border of the land of Canaan.
2_16_36: Now an omer is one-tenth of an ephah, the measure used for the manna.
2_18_1: And Jethro, the priest of Midian, Moses' father-in-law, heard about everything that God had done for Moses and for Israel His people—how the LORD had brought Israel out of Egypt.
2_18_2: Then Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, took in Zipporah, Moses' wife, after he had sent her back.
2_18_3: along with her two sons, one of whom was named Gershom, for Moses said, "I have been a stranger in a foreign land"
2_18_4: And the name of the other was Eliezer, for he said, "The God of my father was my helper, and rescued me from the sword of Pharaoh."
2_18_5: Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, came with his sons and his wife to visit Moses in the wilderness, where he was encamped at the mountain of God.
2_18_6: He sent word to Moses, "I, your father-in-law Jethro, am coming to you with your wife and her two sons with her."
2_18_7: So Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, bowed down, and kissed him, and they asked each other how they had been, and they went into the tent.
2_18_8: Moses told his father-in-law all that the LORD had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians for Israel's sake, all the hardships that had befallen them along the way, and how the LORD had delivered them.
2_18_9: Then Jethro rejoiced over all the goodness which the LORD had done for Israel, whom He had delivered out of the hand of the Egyptians.
2_18_10: And Jethro said, "Blessed be the LORD, who has rescued you from the power of the Egyptians and from the hand of Pharaoh, and who has delivered the people from under the hand of the Egyptians.
2_18_11: Now I know that the LORD is greater than all the gods; for in the very thing in which they dealt arrogantly against the people, He was above them.
2_18_12: Then Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, brought a burnt offering and other sacrifices to God, and Aaron came with all the elders of Israel to eat a meal with Moses' father-in-law before God.
2_18_13: The next day Moses sat to hear the people's disputes, and the people stood around Moses from morning until evening.
2_18_14: So when Moses' father-in-law saw all that he was doing for the people, he said, "What is this thing that you are doing for the people? Why do you alone sit as judge, and all the people stand around you from morning until evening?"
2_18_15: And Moses said to his father-in-law, "Because the people come to me to inquire of God and get a ruling.
2_18_16: When they have a dispute, they come to me, and I judge between one person and another, and I make known the statutes of God and His laws.
2_18_17: So Moses' father-in-law said to him, "What you are doing is not good."
2_18_18: Both you and these people who are with you will surely wear yourselves out, for this task is too heavy for you; you are not able to do it alone.
2_18_19: Listen now to my voice; I will give you counsel, and God will be with you: You be the people's representative before God, so that you may bring their disputes to God.
2_18_20: And you shall teach them the statutes and the laws, and make known to them the way in which they must walk and the work they must do.
2_18_21: Moreover you shall select from all the people capable men, such as fear God, trustworthy men, hating dishonest gain; and place such over them to be rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
2_18_22: Let them judge the people at all times, and let every major matter they shall bring to you, but every minor matter they themselves shall judge, so it will be easier for you, and they will bear the burden with you.
2_18_23: If you do this thing and God so commands you, then you will be able to endure, and all these people will also go home in peace.
2_18_24: So Moses heeded the voice of his father-in-law and did everything he had said.
2_18_25: And Moses chose capable men out of all Israel and made them leaders over the people: rulers of thousands, rulers of hundreds, rulers of fifties, and rulers of tens.
2_18_26: So they judged the people at all times; the difficult cases they brought to Moses, but every minor matter they judged themselves.
2_18_27: Then Moses let his father-in-law depart, and he went his way into his own land.
2_19_1: In the third month after the children of Israel had gone out of the land of Egypt, on that very day they came to the Wilderness of Sinai.
2_19_2: After they set out from Rephidim, they came to the Wilderness of Sinai and camped in the wilderness, and there Israel camped before the mountain.
2_19_3: Moses went up to God, and the LORD called to him from the mountain, saying, "This is what you shall say to the house of Jacob, and tell the children of Israel:
2_19_4: You yourselves have seen what I did to the Egyptians, and how I carried you on eagles' wings and brought you to myself.
2_19_5: Now therefore, if you will indeed obey My voice and keep My covenant, then you shall be My treasured possession above all peoples, for all the earth is Mine.
2_19_6: And you shall be to Me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation.' This is the message you shall speak to the children of Israel."
2_19_7: So Moses came and called together the elders of the people and set before them all these words which the LORD had commanded him.
2_19_8: Then all the people answered together and said, "All that the LORD has spoken we will do." So Moses brought back the people's answer to the LORD.
2_19_9: And the LORD said to Moses, "Behold, I am coming to you in the thick cloud, so that the people may hear when I speak with you, and may also believe you forever." Then Moses told the words of the people to the LORD.
2_19_10: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Go to the people and consecrate them today and tomorrow, and have them wash their clothes.
2_19_11: And let them be ready for the third day, for on the third day the LORD will come down upon Mount Sinai as all the people watch.
2_19_12: You shall set boundaries for the people all around, saying, 'Take heed to yourselves that you do not go up to the mountain or touch its base. Whoever touches the mountain shall surely be put to death.'
2_19_13: No hand shall touch him, but he shall surely be stoned or shot with an arrow; whether animal or man, he shall not live. When the ram's horn sounds a long blast, they may come near the mountain.
2_19_14: So Moses went down from the mountain to the people and consecrated the people, and they washed their garments.
2_19_15: And he said to the people, "Be ready for the third day; do not go near your wives."
2_19_16: On the morning of the third day there were thunders and lightning flashes, and a thick cloud on the mountain, and the sound of the trumpet was very loud, so that all the people who were in the camp trembled.
2_19_17: Then Moses brought the people out of the camp to meet with God, and they took their stand at the foot of the mountain.
2_19_18: Now Mount Sinai was completely enveloped in smoke, because the LORD descended upon it in fire, and its smoke ascended like the smoke of a furnace, and the whole mountain trembled violently.
2_19_19: And when the blast of the trumpet grew louder and louder, Moses spoke, and God answered him in thunder.
2_19_20: Then the LORD came down upon Mount Sinai, on the top of the mountain, and the LORD summoned Moses to the top of the mountain, so Moses went up.
2_19_21: And the LORD said to Moses, "Go down and warn the people, lest they break through the boundaries to gaze at the LORD, and many of them perish.
2_19_22: Also let the priests who come near to the LORD consecrate themselves, or else the LORD will break out against them.
2_19_23: But Moses said to the LORD, "The people cannot come up to Mount Sinai, for You yourself warned us, saying, 'Set bounds around the mountain and consecrate it.'"
2_19_24: Then the LORD said to him, "Go down and come back up bringing Aaron with you, but do not let the priests and the people break through to approach the LORD, lest he break out against them."
2_19_25: So Moses went down to the people and told them what the LORD had said.
2_20_1: And God spoke all these words, saying:
2_20_2: I am the LORD your God, who rescued you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.
2_20_3: You shall have no other gods besides Me.
2_20_4: You shall not make for yourself an idol or any likeness of anything that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth.
2_20_5: You shall not bow down to them nor worship them, for I, the LORD your God, am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children to the third and fourth generations of those who hate Me.
2_20_6: But showing steadfast mercy to thousands, to those who love Me and obey My commandments.
2_20_7: You shall not misuse the name of the LORD your God in vain, for the LORD will not leave unpunished him who takes His name in vain.
2_20_8: Remember to observe the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.
2_20_9: Six days you shall labor and do all your work.
2_20_10: But the seventh day is the Sabbath of the LORD your God, and on it you shall not do any work, you or your son or your daughter, your male servant or your female servant, your livestock, or the foreigner who is within your gates.
2_20_11: For in six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth, the sea, and everything in them, and rested on the seventh day. Therefore the LORD blessed the Sabbath day and made it holy.
2_20_12: Honor your father and your mother, so that your days may be long upon the land which the LORD your God is giving you.
2_20_16: You shall not give false testimony against your neighbor.
2_20_17: You shall not covet your neighbor's house; you shall not covet your neighbor's wife, nor his male servant, nor his female servant, nor his ox, nor his donkey, nor anything else that belongs to your neighbor.
2_20_18: Now all the people perceived the thunderings, the lightning flashes, the sound of the trumpet, and the mountain smoking; and when the people saw it, they trembled with fear and stood far off.
2_20_19: Then they said to Moses, "You speak to us, and we will listen; but do not let God speak directly to us, lest we die."
2_20_20: And Moses said to the people, "Do not be afraid; for God has come to test you, and so that the fear of Him may remain before you, that you may not sin."
2_20_21: So the people stood at a distance, but Moses drew near the thick darkness where God was.
2_20_22: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Thus you shall say to the children of Israel: 'You yourselves have seen that I have talked with you from heaven.
2_20_23: You shall not make anything to rival Me—gods of silver or gods of gold you shall not make for yourselves.
