2_20_24: An altar of earth you shall make for Me, and you shall sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your oxen. In every place where I cause My name to be remembered I will come to you and bless you.
2_20_25: And if you make Me an altar of stone, you shall not build it of cut stones, for if you wield your tool on it, you have profaned it.
2_20_26: Nor shall you go up by steps to My altar, so that your nakedness may not be exposed on it.
2_31_1: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
2_31_2: See, I have specifically called by name Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah.
2_31_3: And I have filled him with the Spirit of God, in wisdom, in understanding, in knowledge, and in ability in all kinds of craftsmanship.
2_31_4: to devise artistic designs, to work in gold, in silver, in bronze,
2_31_5: in cutting stones for setting, in carving wood, and to work in every craft.
2_31_6: And I, indeed I, have appointed with him Aholiab the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan; and I have put skill in the hearts of all the gifted artisans, that they may make all that I have commanded you.
2_31_7: The tabernacle of meeting, the ark of the Testimony and the mercy seat that is on top of it, and all the furnishings of the tabernacle—
2_31_8: The table and its utensils, the pure gold lampstand with all its accessories, the altar of incense,
2_31_9: the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils, and the washbasin with its stand—
2_31_10: The specially woven garments for ministry, the holy garments for Aaron the priest and the garments of his sons, to serve as priests.
2_31_11: and the anointing oil and the fragrant incense for the Holy Place. According to all that I have commanded you they shall do.
2_31_12: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
2_31_13: Speak to the children of Israel, saying, You must observe My Sabbaths, for it is a sign between Me and you throughout your generations, that you may know that I am the LORD who sanctifies you.
2_31_14: You shall keep the Sabbath, therefore, for it is a holy day for you. Everyone who profanes it shall surely be put to death; for whoever does any work on it, that person shall be cut off from among his people.
2_31_15: Work shall be done for six days, but the seventh day is the Sabbath of complete rest, holy to the LORD, and whoever does any work on the Sabbath day shall surely be put to death.
2_31_16: Therefore the children of Israel shall keep the Sabbath, observing the Sabbath throughout their generations as a perpetual covenant.
2_31_17: It is a sign between Me and the children of Israel forever; for in six days the LORD made the heavens and the earth, and on the seventh day He ceased from labor and was refreshed.
2_31_18: When the LORD had finished speaking with Moses on Mount Sinai, He gave him the two tablets of the Testimony, tablets of stone, written by the finger of God.
2_32_1: Now when the people saw how long Moses delayed coming down from the mountain, the people gathered around Aaron and said to him, "Come, make us gods that shall go before us; for as for this Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him."
2_32_2: So Aaron said to them, "Break off the golden rings which are in the ears of your wives, your sons, and your daughters, and bring them to me."
2_32_3: So all the people tore off the golden rings which were in their ears and brought them to Aaron.
2_32_4: And he received the gold from their hand, and he fashioned it with an engraving tool, and made a molded calf, and they said, "This is your god, O Israel, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt!"
2_32_5: So when Aaron saw this, he built an altar before it, and Aaron made a proclamation and said, "Tomorrow will be a feast to the LORD."
2_32_6: Then they rose early the next day, offered burnt offerings, and brought peace offerings, and the people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to revel.
2_32_7: And the LORD said to Moses, "Go, get down at once! For your people whom you brought out of the land of Egypt have corrupted themselves.
2_32_8: They have turned aside quickly out of the way which I commanded them, they have made for themselves a molded calf and worshiped it and sacrificed to it and said, 'This is your god, O Israel, that brought you up out of the land of Egypt!'
2_32_9: And the LORD said to Moses, "I have seen this people, and behold, they are indeed a stiff-necked people!"
2_32_10: Now therefore, leave Me alone, that My wrath may burn hot against them and I may destroy them, and I will make of you a great nation.
2_32_11: Then Moses pleaded with the LORD his God and said, "LORD, why does Your anger burn hot against Your people whom You have brought out of the land of Egypt with great power and with a mighty hand?
2_32_12: Why should the Egyptians speak and say, 'With evil intent He brought them out to kill them in the mountains and to consume them from the face of the earth'? Turn from Your burning anger and relent from this disaster against Your people.
2_32_13: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, Your servants, to whom You swore by Your own self and said to them, 'I will multiply your descendants as the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have promised I will give to your descendants, and they shall inherit it forever.'
2_32_14: So the LORD relented from the terrible harm which He said He would bring on His people.
2_32_15: And Moses turned and went down from the mountain, and the two stone tablets of the Testimony were in his hand, tablets that were written on both sides, on the front and on the back they were written.
2_32_16: Now the tablets were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God himself engraved on the tablets.
2_32_17: And when Joshua heard the noise of the people as they shouted, he said to Moses, "There is a sound of war in the camp."
2_32_18: But he said, "It is not the sound of the shout of victory, nor the sound of the cry of defeat, but the sound of singing I hear."
2_32_19: So it was, as soon as he came near the camp, that he saw the calf and the dancing, and Moses' anger burned hot, and he threw the tablets out of his hands and shattered them at the foot of the mountain.
2_32_20: Then he took the calf which they had made, burned it in the fire, and ground it to powder, and he scattered it over the surface of the water and made the children of Israel drink it.
2_32_21: And Moses said to Aaron, "What did these people do to you that you have brought such a great sin upon them?"
2_32_22: So Aaron said, "Do not let the anger of my lord burn hot. You yourself know the people, that they are prone to evil.
2_32_23: For they said to me, 'Make us gods who will go before us; as for this Moses, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.'
2_32_24: And I said to them, 'Whoever has any gold jewelry, let them break it off.' So they gave it to me, and I threw it into the fire, and out came this calf.
2_32_25: Now when Moses saw that the people were out of control, for Aaron had let them break loose, to the derision of their enemies.
2_32_26: Then Moses stood in the gate of the camp and said, "Whoever is on the LORD's side—come to me!" And all the sons of Levi gathered around him.
2_32_27: And he said to them, "Thus says the LORD God of Israel: 'Let every man put his sword on his side, and go back and forth from gate to gate throughout the camp, and let every man kill his brother, every man his companion, and every man his neighbor.'"
2_32_28: So the sons of Levi did as Moses commanded, and about three thousand men of the people fell dead that day.
2_32_29: Then Moses said, "Consecrate yourselves today to the LORD, each one even at the cost of your son and your brother, so that He may bestow a blessing upon you this day."
2_32_30: The next day Moses said to the people, "You have committed a terrible sin, so now I will go up to the LORD; perhaps I can make atonement for your sin."
2_32_31: Then Moses returned to the LORD and said, "Oh, what a terrible sin these people have committed, and they have made for themselves a god of gold!
2_32_32: Yet now, if You will forgive their sin—but if not, I pray, erase me out of Your book which You have written.
2_32_33: And the LORD said to Moses, "Whoever has sinned against Me, I will blot out of My book.
2_32_34: Now therefore go, lead the people to the place I have told you about; behold, My angel shall go before you, but on the day when I settle accounts, I will certainly hold them accountable for their sin.
2_32_35: So the LORD sent a plague on the people because of what they did with the calf which Aaron had made.
2_33_1: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Depart and go up from here, you and the people whom you have brought up out of the land of Egypt, to the land of which I swore to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, saying, 'To your offspring I will give it.'"
2_33_2: I will send My angel ahead of you, and I will drive out the Canaanite and the Amorite and the Hittite and the Perizzite and the Hivite and the Jebusite.
2_33_3: Go up to a land flowing with milk and honey, but I will not go up in your midst, lest I consume you on the way, for you are a stiff-necked and obstinate people.
2_33_4: When the people heard this disastrous news, they mourned, and no one put on his ornaments.
2_33_5: For the LORD had said to Moses, "Say to the children of Israel, 'You are a stiff-necked people; if for a single moment I should go up among you, I would consume you. Now therefore, take off your ornaments, that I may know what to do with you.'"
2_33_6: So the children of Israel stripped themselves of their ornaments from Mount Horeb onward.
2_33_7: Moses used to take the tent and pitch it outside the camp, far from the camp, and called it the tabernacle of meeting, and everyone who sought the LORD would go out to the tabernacle of meeting which was outside the camp.
2_33_8: Whenever Moses went out to the tent, all the people would rise and each man would stand at his tent door and gaze after Moses until he had gone into the tent.
2_33_9: And it came to pass, whenever Moses entered the tabernacle, that the pillar of cloud descended and stood at the entrance of the tabernacle, and the LORD talked with Moses.
2_33_10: All the people saw the pillar of cloud standing at the entrance of the tabernacle, and all the people rose and worshiped, each man at his tent door.
2_33_11: The LORD used to speak to Moses face to face, as a man speaks to his friend, and Moses would return to the camp, but his assistant Joshua the son of Nun, a young man, would not depart from the tent.
2_33_12: Then Moses said to the LORD, "See, You say to me, 'Bring up this people,' but You Yourself have not let me know whom You will send with me, yet You have said, 'I know you by name, and you have also found favor in My sight.'"
2_33_13: Now therefore, I pray, if I have found favor in Your sight, teach me Your ways, that I may know You so that I may find favor in Your sight, and consider that this nation is Your people.
2_33_14: And He said, "My Presence will go with you, and I will give you rest."
2_33_15: Then he said to Him, "If Your Presence does not go with us, do not lead us up from here."
2_33_16: For how then will it be known that Your people and I have found favor in Your sight, except You go with us? So we shall be distinguished, Your people and I, from all the people who are upon the face of the earth.
2_33_17: So the LORD said to Moses, "I will indeed do this very thing that you have spoken, for you have found favor in My sight, and I know you by name."
2_33_18: And Moses said, "Please, show me Your glory."
2_33_19: Then He said, "I Myself will make all My goodness pass before you, and I will proclaim the name of the LORD before you. I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and I will show mercy on whom I will show mercy."
2_33_20: But He said, "You cannot see My face, for no one shall see Me and live."
2_33_21: And the LORD said, "Here is a place near Me, and you shall stand on the rock.
2_33_22: So it shall be, while My glory passes by, that I will put you in the cleft of the rock and will cover you with My hand until I have passed by.
2_33_23: Then I will take away My hand, and you shall see Me from behind, but My face shall not be seen.
2_34_1: And the LORD said to Moses, "Cut out two tablets of stone like the first ones, and I will write on these tablets the words that were on the first tablets which you broke.
2_34_2: So be prepared in the morning, and come up in the morning to Mount Sinai, and present yourself to Me there on the top of the mountain.
2_34_3: And no man shall come up with you, and let no man be seen anywhere on the mountain; let neither flocks nor herds graze in front of that mountain.
2_34_4: So he cut two tablets of stone like the first ones, and Moses rose early in the morning and climbed Mount Sinai as the LORD had commanded him, and he took in his hand the two tablets of stone.
2_34_5: Now the LORD descended in the cloud and stood with him there, and proclaimed his own name, the LORD.
2_34_6: And the LORD passed before him and proclaimed, "The LORD, the LORD God, compassionate and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness,
2_34_7: Maintaining steadfast love for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, but by no means clearing the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children and the children's children to the third and the fourth generation.
2_34_8: So Moses made haste and bowed low toward the earth, and worshiped.
2_34_9: Then he said, "If now I have found grace in Your sight, O Lord, let my Lord, I pray, go along in our midst, even though we are a stiff-necked people; and pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us as Your own possession."
2_34_10: And He said, "Behold, I am making a covenant. Before all your people I will do marvels such as have not been done in all the earth or in any nation, and all the people among whom you are shall see the work of the LORD, for it is an awesome thing that I will do with you.
2_34_11: Be sure to observe what I command you this day, and behold, I am driving out before you the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
2_34_12: Be careful that you make no covenant with the inhabitants of the land where you are going, lest it become a snare in your midst.
2_34_13: But you shall tear down their altars, smash their sacred pillars, and cut down their Asherah poles.
2_34_14: You shall worship no other god, for the LORD, whose very name is Jealous, is a jealous God.
2_34_15: Lest you make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, and they prostitute themselves with their gods and make sacrifice to their gods, and someone invites you and you eat of his sacrifice.
2_34_16: And you take of their daughters for your sons, and their daughters play the harlot with their gods and cause your sons to play the harlot with their gods.
2_34_17: You shall make no molten gods of cast metal for yourselves.
2_34_18: You shall observe the Feast of Unleavened Bread; for seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the appointed time in the month of Abib, for in the month of Abib you came out from Egypt.
2_34_19: All that open the womb belong to Me, and every male firstborn among your livestock, whether cattle or sheep.
2_34_20: But the firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb, and if you will not redeem it then you shall break its neck. All the firstborn of your sons you shall redeem, and no one shall appear before Me empty-handed.
2_34_21: Six days you shall work, but on the seventh day you shall rest; even during plowing time and harvest you shall rest.
2_34_22: You shall celebrate the Feast of Weeks, of the firstfruits of wheat harvest, and the Feast of Ingathering at the turn of the year.
2_34_23: Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the Lord GOD, the God of Israel.
2_34_24: For I will drive out the nations before you and enlarge your borders; no one will covet your land when you go up to appear before the LORD your God three times in the year.
2_34_25: You shall not offer the blood of My sacrifice with anything leavened, nor shall the sacrifice of the Feast of the Passover be left over until morning.
2_34_26: The best of the firstfruits of your land you shall bring to the house of the LORD your God, and you shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.
2_34_27: Then the LORD said to Moses, "Write down these words, for in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Israel."
2_34_28: So he was there with the Lord forty days and forty nights; he neither ate bread nor drank water, and He wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Commandments.
2_34_29: When Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the Testimony in his hand as he descended from the mountain, Moses did not realize that the skin of his face shone because he had been talking with Him.
2_34_30: So when Aaron and all the children of Israel saw Moses, behold, the skin of his face shone with radiance, and they were afraid to come near him.
2_34_31: Then Moses called out to them, and Aaron and all the rulers of the congregation returned to him, and Moses talked with them.
2_34_32: Afterward all the children of Israel came near, and he commanded them to do everything that the LORD had spoken with him on Mount Sinai.
2_34_33: And when Moses had finished speaking with them, he covered his face with a veil.
2_34_34: But whenever Moses went in before the LORD to speak with Him, he would remove the veil until he came out, and he would come out and tell the children of Israel whatever he had been commanded.
2_34_35: And whenever the children of Israel saw the face of Moses, that the skin of Moses' face was radiant, then Moses would replace the veil on his face again until he went in to speak with Him.
2_36_1: And Bezalel and Aholiab, and every gifted artisan in whom the LORD has put skill and understanding to know how to do all manner of work for the construction of the sanctuary, shall do according to all that the LORD has commanded.
2_36_2: Then Moses called Bezalel and Aholiab, and every gifted artisan in whose heart the LORD had put wisdom and skill, everyone whose heart moved them to come and do the work.
2_36_3: And they received from Moses all the contributions which the children of Israel had brought for the work of constructing the sanctuary, and the people still continued bringing to him freewill offerings every morning.
2_36_4: Then all the skilled craftsmen who were performing all the work of the sanctuary came, each from the work he was doing.
2_36_5: And they spoke to Moses, saying, "The people bring much more than enough for the construction work which the LORD commanded us to do."
2_36_6: So Moses gave a command, and they caused it to be proclaimed throughout the camp, saying, "Let neither man nor woman do any more work for the contribution of the sanctuary." And the people were restrained from bringing.
2_36_7: For the material they had was sufficient and more than enough for all the work to be done.
2_36_8: Then all the gifted artisans among those who were performing the work made the tabernacle with ten curtains woven of fine linen, and of blue, purple, and scarlet thread; with artistic designs of cherubim they made them.
2_36_9: The length of each curtain was twenty-eight cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; all the curtains had the same measurements.
2_36_10: He joined five curtains to one another, and the other five curtains he joined to one another.
2_36_11: He made loops of blue yarn on the edge of the outermost curtain of the first set, and likewise he did the same on the outer edge of the last curtain of the second set.
2_36_12: Fifty loops he made on one curtain, and fifty loops he made on the edge of the curtain on the end of the second set; the loops were opposite one another so they held one curtain to another.
2_36_13: And he made fifty clasps of gold, and coupled the curtains to one another with the clasps, so that the tabernacle became one unified whole.
2_36_14: He made curtains of goats' hair for a tent covering over the tabernacle; he made eleven curtains in all.
2_36_15: The length of each curtain was thirty cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits; the eleven curtains had the same measurements.
2_36_16: He joined five curtains together by themselves and six curtains by themselves.
2_36_17: He made fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain in one set, and fifty loops he made on the edge of the corresponding curtain in the second set.
2_36_18: He also made fifty bronze clasps to join the tent together so that it might be one whole unit.
2_36_19: Then he made a covering for the tent of tanned ram skins dyed red, and a covering of fine leather above that.
2_36_20: For the tabernacle he made upright frames of acacia wood standing upright.
2_36_21: The length of each board was ten cubits, and the width of each board one and a half cubits.
2_36_22: Each board had two tenons for fitting together one to another, and thus he did for all the boards of the tabernacle.
2_36_23: He made the frames for the tabernacle: twenty frames for the south side.
2_36_24: Forty bases of silver he made to go under the twenty boards, two bases under each of the boards for its two tenons.
2_36_25: And for the second side of the tabernacle, the north side, he made twenty boards.
2_36_26: and their forty bases of silver: two bases under each of the boards.
2_36_27: For the rear of the tabernacle on the west side he made six boards.
2_36_28: He also made two boards for the two back corners of the tabernacle at the rear.
2_36_29: They were coupled at the bottom and joined together at the top by one ring, and thus he made both of them the same way for the two corners.
2_36_30: So there were eight frames with their bases of silver—sixteen bases in all—two bases under each of the frames.
2_36_31: Then he made crossbars of acacia wood, five for the boards on one side of the tabernacle.
2_36_32: Five bars for the boards on the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle at the rear westward.
2_36_33: And he made the middle bar to pass through the center of the boards from one end to the other.
2_36_34: He overlaid the frames with gold, made their rings of gold to be holders for the crossbars, and overlaid the crossbars with gold.
2_36_35: And he made a veil of blue, purple, and scarlet thread, and fine twined linen; it was worked with a skillfully crafted design of cherubim.
2_36_36: He made four pillars of acacia wood for it and overlaid them with gold, with their hooks of gold, and he cast four silver bases for them.
2_36_37: He also made a screen for the entrance of the tabernacle, of blue, purple, and scarlet thread, and fine woven linen, the work of an embroiderer.
2_36_38: He made its five pillars with their hooks and overlaid their tops and their bands with gold, but their five bases were bronze.
2_38_1: Bezalel made the altar of burnt offering of acacia wood, five cubits long and five cubits wide—it was square—and its height was three cubits.
2_38_2: He made its horns on its four corners; the horns were of one piece with it, and he overlaid the altar with bronze.
2_38_3: He made all the utensils of the altar: the pots, the shovels, the basins, the meat forks, and the firepans; all its utensils he made of bronze.
2_38_4: And he made a grate of bronze network for the altar, under its ledge, extending halfway up from the bottom.
2_38_5: He cast four rings on the four corners of the bronze grating as holders for the poles.
2_38_6: And he made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
2_38_7: Then he inserted the poles into the rings on the sides of the altar with which to carry it, and he made the altar hollow with boards.
2_38_8: He made the laver of bronze and its base of bronze from the bronze mirrors donated by the serving women who assembled at the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting.
2_38_9: Then he made the courtyard on the south side; the hangings of the court were of fine twined linen, one hundred cubits long.
2_38_10: There were twenty pillars for them with twenty bronze bases, and the hooks of the pillars and their bands were silver.
2_38_11: On the north side the curtains were one hundred cubits long, with twenty pillars and their twenty bronze bases, and the hooks of the pillars and their bands were silver.
2_38_12: And on the west side there were hangings of fifty cubits, with ten pillars and their ten bases, and the hooks of the pillars and their bands were of silver.
2_38_13: For the east side facing the sunrise the hangings were fifty cubits.
2_38_14: The hangings for one side of the gate were fifteen cubits long, with their three pillars and their three bases.
2_38_15: And the same for the other side of the courtyard gate; on both sides were curtains of fifteen cubits, with their three pillars and their three bases.
2_38_16: All the hangings of the court all around were of fine twined linen.
2_38_17: The bases for the pillars were bronze, the hooks of the pillars and their bands were silver, and the overlay of their tops was silver; and all the pillars of the court were banded with silver.
2_38_18: The screen for the gate of the court was embroidered needlework of blue, purple, and scarlet thread, and of fine woven linen, twenty cubits long and five cubits high in its width, corresponding to the hangings of the court.
2_38_19: And there were four pillars with their four bases of bronze; their hooks were silver, and the overlaying of their capitals and their bands was silver.
2_38_20: All the tent pegs of the tabernacle and of the court all around were made of bronze.
2_38_21: This is the inventory of the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the Testimony, which was recorded according to the commandment of Moses, for the service of the Levites, under the direction of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest.
2_38_22: Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made everything that the LORD had commanded Moses.
2_38_23: And with him was Aholiab the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver and designer, an embroiderer in blue, purple, and scarlet thread, and fine linen.
2_38_24: All the gold that was used for the work in all the construction of the holy place, that is, the gold of the presentation offering, was twenty-nine talents and seven hundred and thirty shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary.
2_38_25: And the silver from those who were recorded of the congregation was one hundred talents and one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary.
2_38_26: A bekah for each man, that is, half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary, for everyone who was listed in the records from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred and three thousand, five hundred and fifty men.
2_38_27: The hundred talents of silver were used for casting the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the veil, one hundred bases from the hundred talents, a talent for each base.
2_38_28: Then from the one thousand seven hundred and seventy-five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, overlaid their tops, and supplied bands for them.
2_38_29: The bronze of the wave offering was seventy talents and two thousand four hundred shekels.
2_38_30: And with it he made the bases for the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting, the bronze altar, the bronze grating for it, and all the utensils for the altar.
2_38_31: The bases for the courtyard all around, the bases for the courtyard gate, all the tent pegs of the tabernacle, and all the tent pegs of the courtyard all around.
2_39_32: Thus all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting was finished, and the children of Israel did everything according to all that the LORD had commanded Moses; so they did.
2_39_33: Then they brought the tabernacle to Moses, the tent and all its furnishings: its clasps, its frames, its crossbars, its pillars, and its bases.
2_39_34: the covering of ram skins dyed red, the covering of fine leather, and the screening veil of the covering
2_39_35: the ark of the Testimony with its carrying poles and the mercy seat;
2_39_36: the table, all its utensils, and the bread of the Presence;
2_39_37: The pure gold lampstand with its lamps arranged in order, all its utensils, and the oil for light.
2_39_38: The golden altar, the anointing oil and the fragrant incense, the screen for the entrance of the tent.
2_39_39: the bronze altar, its bronze grating, its carrying poles, and all its utensils; the basin with its stand;
2_39_40: The hangings of the court, its pillars and its bases, the screen for the court gate, its cords and its tent pegs, and all the utensils for the service of the tabernacle, for the tent of meeting.
2_39_41: The specially woven garments for ministering in the holy place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and his sons' garments to serve as priests.
2_39_42: According to all that the LORD had commanded Moses, so the children of Israel faithfully did all the work.
2_39_43: Then Moses inspected all the work, and indeed they had accomplished it; as the LORD had commanded, just so they had done it, and Moses blessed them.
3_1_1: Now the LORD summoned Moses and spoke to him from the tabernacle of meeting, saying,
3_1_2: Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: When any one of you brings an offering to the LORD, you shall bring your offering of livestock from the herd or from the flock.
3_1_3: If his offering is a burnt sacrifice of the herd, let him offer a male without blemish; he shall bring it to the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting that he may be accepted before the LORD.
3_1_4: Then he shall lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering, and it will be accepted on his behalf to make atonement for him.
3_1_5: He shall slaughter the young bull before the LORD, and the priests, Aaron's sons, shall present the blood and sprinkle the blood all around on the altar that is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
3_1_6: And he shall skin the burnt offering and cut it into pieces.
3_1_7: The sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire on the altar and arrange wood in order on the fire.
3_1_8: Then the priests, Aaron's sons, shall arrange the pieces, the head, and the fat in order on the wood that is on the fire upon the altar.
3_1_9: But he shall wash its entrails and its legs with water, and the priest shall burn all of it on the altar as a burnt offering, a food offering of a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
3_1_10: If his offering is from the flock, either sheep or goats, as a burnt offering, he shall bring a male without defect.
3_1_11: He shall slaughter it on the north side of the altar before the LORD, and the priests, Aaron's sons, shall splatter its blood all around on the altar.
3_1_12: And he shall cut it into its pieces, with its head and its fat, and the priest shall arrange them in order on the wood that is on the fire upon the altar.
3_1_13: But he shall wash the entrails and the legs with water, and the priest shall present it all and burn it on the altar; it is a burnt offering, a food offering made by fire, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
3_1_14: And if the burnt sacrifice of his offering to the LORD is of birds, then he shall bring his offering from the turtledoves or young pigeons.
3_1_15: The priest shall bring it to the altar, wring off its head, and offer it up in smoke on the altar; its blood shall be drained out at the side of the altar.
3_1_16: And he shall remove its crop with its contents and cast it beside the altar on the east side, into the place for ashes.
3_1_17: Then he shall tear it open by its wings, but shall not divide it completely; and the priest shall burn it on the altar, on the wood that is on the fire. It is a burnt offering, a food offering with a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
3_3_1: When his offering is a sacrifice of peace offering, if he offers an animal of the herd, whether male or female, he shall offer it without blemish before the LORD.
3_3_2: He shall lay his hand on the head of his offering and slaughter it at the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting, and Aaron's sons the priests shall sprinkle the blood all around on the altar.
3_3_3: Then he shall present from the sacrifice of the peace offering a food offering made by fire to the LORD, the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that is on the entrails.
3_3_4: The two kidneys and the fat that is on them at the flanks, and the long lobe of the liver attached above the kidneys, he shall remove.
3_3_5: Aaron's sons shall burn it on the altar on top of the burnt offering, which is on the wood that is on the fire, as an offering made by fire, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
3_3_6: If his offering as a sacrifice of a peace offering to the LORD is an animal from the flock, whether male or female, he shall offer it without blemish.
3_3_7: If he offers a lamb as his offering, then he shall bring it before the LORD.
3_3_8: He shall lay his hand on the head of his offering and slaughter it before the tent of meeting, and Aaron's sons shall splatter its blood all around on the altar.
3_3_9: Then he shall present from the sacrifice of the peace offering, as a food offering made by fire to the LORD, its fat and the entire fat tail which he shall remove close to the backbone, and the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that is on the entrails.
3_3_10: He shall remove the two kidneys and the fat that is on them by the loins, and the long lobe of the liver above the kidneys.
3_3_11: And the priest shall burn it on the altar as food, an offering made by fire to the LORD.
3_3_12: And if his offering is a goat, then he shall present it before the LORD.
3_3_13: He shall lay his hand on its head and slaughter it before the tabernacle of meeting, and the sons of Aaron shall splatter its blood all around on the altar.
3_3_14: Then he shall present from it his offering, as a special gift made by fire to the LORD: the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that is on the entrails,
3_3_15: The two kidneys and the fat that is on them by the loins, and the long lobe of the liver attached above the kidneys, he shall remove.
3_3_16: The priest shall burn them on the altar as food, an offering made by fire for a pleasing aroma; all the fat belongs to the LORD.
3_3_17: This shall be a permanent statute throughout your generations in all your dwellings: you shall not eat any fat or any blood.
3_4_1: Now the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_4_2: Speak to the children of Israel, saying: If a person sins unintentionally against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which ought not to be done, and does any of them,
3_4_3: If the anointed priest sins, bringing guilt on the people, then let him offer to the LORD for the sin he has committed a young bull without blemish as a sin offering.
3_4_4: He shall bring the bull to the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting before the LORD, lay his hand on the bull's head, and slaughter the bull before the LORD.
3_4_5: Then the anointed priest shall take some of the bull's blood and bring it into the tabernacle of meeting.
3_4_6: The priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times before the LORD in front of the inner curtain of the sanctuary.
3_4_7: The priest shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense before the LORD, which is in the tabernacle of meeting, and he shall pour out all the remaining blood of the bull at the base of the altar of burnt offering, which is at the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting.
3_4_8: He shall remove from it all the fat of the bull as the sin offering, the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat which is on the entrails.
3_4_9: The two kidneys and the fat that is on them by the loins, and the long lobe of the liver attached above the kidneys, he shall remove.
3_4_10: Just as it is removed from the ox of the sacrifice of peace offerings, and the priest shall burn them on the altar of burnt offering.
3_4_11: But the bull's hide and all its flesh, with its head and legs, its internal organs and dung—
3_4_12: The whole bull he shall carry outside the camp to a ceremonially clean place where the ashes are poured out, and burn it on wood with fire; where the ashes are poured out it shall be burned.
3_4_13: Now if the whole congregation of Israel sins unintentionally, and the matter escapes the notice of the assembly, and they have done something against any of the commandments of the LORD concerning things which ought not to be done, and they incur guilt.
3_4_14: When the sin which they have committed becomes known, the assembly shall offer a young bull from the herd for a sin offering and bring it before the tent of meeting.
3_4_15: And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands on the head of the bull before the LORD, and the bull shall be slaughtered before the LORD.
3_4_16: The anointed priest shall bring some of the bull's blood into the tabernacle of meeting.
3_4_17: Then the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the LORD in front of the curtain.
3_4_18: He shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar which is before the LORD in the tabernacle of meeting, and he shall pour out all the remaining blood at the base of the altar of burnt offering which is at the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting.
3_4_19: He shall remove all the fat from it and burn it on the altar.
3_4_20: He shall do with the bull just as he did with the bull of the sin offering, thus he shall do with it, and the priest shall make atonement for them and they will be forgiven.
3_4_21: Then he shall carry the bull outside the camp and burn it up as he burned the first bull; it is the sin offering for the assembly.
3_4_22: When a leader has sinned and done something unintentionally against any of the commandments of the LORD his God in anything which should not be done, and incurs guilt,
3_4_23: or if his sin which he has committed becomes known to him, he shall bring as his offering a male goat without blemish.
3_4_24: He shall lay his hand on the head of the male goat and slaughter it at the place where they kill the burnt offering before the LORD. It is a sin offering.
3_4_25: The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, apply it to the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and pour out the rest of its blood at the base of the altar of burnt offering.
3_4_26: And he shall burn all its fat on the altar, like the fat of the sacrifice of the peace offering, and through this process the priest shall make atonement for him concerning his sin, and it shall be forgiven him.
3_4_27: If anyone of the common people sins unintentionally by violating one of the commandments of the LORD in anything which ought not to be done, and incurs guilt,
3_4_28: Or if his sin which he has committed is made known to him, then he shall bring as his offering a female goat without blemish for his sin which he has committed.
3_4_29: He shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter the sin offering at the place of the burnt offering.
3_4_30: Then the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and apply it to the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and pour out all the remaining blood at the base of the altar.
3_4_31: He shall remove all its fat, as fat is removed from the sacrifice of the peace offering, and the priest shall burn it on the altar as a pleasing aroma to the LORD, and the priest shall make atonement for him, and he shall be forgiven.
3_4_32: If he brings a lamb as his sin offering, he shall bring a female without defect.
3_4_33: Then he shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter it as a sin offering at the place where they kill the burnt offering.
3_4_34: The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, apply it to the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and pour out all the remaining blood at the base of the altar.
3_4_35: He shall remove all its fat, as the fat of the lamb is removed from the sacrifice of the peace offering, and the priest shall burn it on the altar on top of the offerings made by fire to the LORD, and the priest shall make atonement for him for his sin that he has committed, and he shall be forgiven.
3_5_1: If a person sins in hearing a public oath to testify and is a witness, whether he has seen or known of the matter—if he does not tell it, he bears guilt.
3_5_2: Or if a person touches any unclean thing, whether it is the carcass of an unclean wild animal, or the carcass of unclean livestock, or the carcass of unclean swarming things, though it is hidden from him and he is unaware of it, he shall be unclean and guilty.
3_5_3: Or if he touches human uncleanness—whatever sort of uncleanness with which a man may be defiled, and he is unaware of it—when he comes to know it and realizes it, then he shall be guilty.
3_5_4: Or if a person swears, speaking rashly with his lips to do evil or to do good, whatever it is that a man may pronounce by an oath, and it is hidden from him—when he comes to know it, then he shall be guilty in any of these matters.
3_5_5: And it shall be, when he realizes his guilt in any of these matters, that he shall confess that he has sinned in that thing.
3_5_6: And he shall bring his guilt offering to the LORD for his sin which he has committed, a female from the flock, a lamb or a goat as a sin offering, and the priest shall make atonement on his behalf for his sin.
3_5_7: If he cannot afford a lamb, then he shall bring to the LORD as his penalty for the sin which he has committed two turtledoves or two young pigeons, one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering.
3_5_8: He shall bring them to the priest, who shall present first the one for the sin offering and wring its head from its neck, but shall not sever it completely.
3_5_9: Then he shall sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar, and the rest of the blood shall be drained out at the base of the altar; it is a sin offering.
3_5_10: And he shall prepare the second as a burnt offering according to the prescribed manner, and the priest shall make atonement on his behalf for his sin which he has committed, and it shall be forgiven him.
3_5_11: But if his means are not sufficient to bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons, then he who sinned shall bring for his offering one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a sin offering, and he shall put no oil on it nor any frankincense on it, for it is a sin offering.
3_5_12: Then he shall bring it to the priest, and the priest shall take his handful of it as a memorial portion and burn it on the altar on top of the LORD's food offerings; it is a sin offering.
3_5_13: The priest shall make atonement for him concerning his sin that he has committed in any of these matters, and it shall be forgiven him, and the remainder shall belong to the priest as a grain offering.
3_5_14: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
3_5_15: If a person commits a trespass and sins unintentionally in regard to the holy things of the LORD, then he shall bring to the LORD as his guilt offering a ram without blemish from the flock, with your valuation in shekels of silver according to the shekel of the sanctuary.
3_5_16: He shall make restitution for the harm that he has done in regard to the holy thing, and shall add one-fifth to it and give it to the priest, and the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering, and it shall be forgiven him.
3_5_17: If a person sins and commits any of these things which are forbidden by the commandments of the LORD, though he was unaware, he is still guilty and shall bear his punishment.
3_5_18: He shall bring to the priest a ram without blemish from the flock, according to your valuation, as a guilt offering, and the priest shall make atonement for him regarding the error he committed unintentionally and did not know it, and it shall be forgiven him.
3_5_19: It is a guilt offering; he has certainly incurred guilt before the LORD.
3_6_1: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
3_6_2: If a person sins and commits a trespass against the LORD by deceiving his neighbor about what was delivered to him for safekeeping, or about a security deposit, or about a robbery, or if he has extorted from his neighbor,
3_6_3: or if he has found something lost and lies concerning it, and swears falsely—in any one of these things that a person may do in which he sins:
3_6_4: Then it shall be, because he has sinned and is guilty, that he shall restore what he took by robbery, or the thing which he got by extortion, or what was delivered to him for safekeeping, or the lost thing which he found.
3_6_5: He shall restore in full all that about which he has sworn falsely, add one-fifth more to it, and give it to the one to whom it belongs on the day he acknowledges his guilt.
3_6_6: And he shall bring his guilt offering to the LORD, a ram without defect from the flock, according to your assessment of its value, to the priest.
3_6_7: So the priest shall make atonement for him before the LORD, and he shall be forgiven for any one of these things that he may have done to incur guilt.
3_6_8: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_6_9: Command Aaron and his sons, saying, 'This is the law of the burnt offering: The burnt offering itself shall remain on the hearth upon the altar all night until morning, and the fire of the altar shall be kept burning on it.
3_6_10: The priest shall put on his linen garment and his linen undergarments on his body, and take up the ashes to which the fire has reduced the burnt offering on the altar, and he shall put them beside the altar.
3_6_11: Then he shall take off his garments, put on other clothes, and carry the ashes outside the camp to a ceremonially clean place.
3_6_12: The fire on the altar shall be kept burning on it; it shall not go out, and the priest shall burn wood on it every morning and arrange the burnt offering on it, and he shall burn on it the fat portions of the peace offerings.
3_6_13: A fire shall be kept burning continually on the altar; it must never go out.
3_6_14: This is the law of the grain offering: The sons of Aaron shall present it before the LORD in front of the altar.
3_6_15: The priest shall take from it his handful of the fine flour of the grain offering, with its oil, and all the frankincense which is on the grain offering, and shall burn it on the altar as a pleasing aroma, as its memorial portion to the LORD.
3_6_16: Aaron and his sons shall eat the remainder of it as unleavened cakes in a holy place; they shall eat it in the courtyard of the tabernacle of meeting.
3_6_17: It shall not be baked with leaven; I have given it as their share of My offerings made by fire, and it is most holy, like the sin offering and the guilt offering.
3_6_18: All the males among the children of Aaron may eat it as a permanent statute throughout your generations concerning the offerings made by fire to the LORD, and whatever touches them will become holy.
3_6_19: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_6_20: This is the offering of Aaron and his sons, which they shall present to the LORD on the day when he is anointed: one-tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a regular grain offering, half of it in the morning and half of it in the evening.
3_6_21: It shall be prepared with oil on a griddle, and when it is well mixed you shall bring it in, presenting the baked pieces of the grain offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
3_6_22: The priest from among his sons who is anointed to succeed him shall offer it as a permanent statute to the LORD, and it shall be wholly burned.
3_6_23: For every grain offering of the priest shall be wholly burned. It shall not be eaten.
3_6_24: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_6_25: Speak to Aaron and to his sons, saying, This is the law of the sin offering: In the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered, the sin offering shall be slaughtered before the LORD; it is most holy.
3_6_26: The priest who offers the sacrifice for sin shall eat it in a holy place, in the courtyard of the tent of meeting.
3_6_27: Everyone who touches its flesh must be holy, and when any of its blood is splashed on a garment, you shall wash that on which it was splashed in a holy place.
3_6_28: But the earthen vessel in which it is boiled shall be broken, and if it is boiled in a bronze pot, it shall be scoured and thoroughly rinsed in water.
3_6_29: All the males among the priests may eat from this offering. It is most holy.
3_6_30: But no sin offering from which any of the blood is brought into the tabernacle of meeting to make atonement in the holy place shall be eaten; it must be completely burned in the fire.
3_7_1: Likewise this is the law of the guilt offering; it is most holy.
3_7_2: In the place where they kill the burnt offering they shall kill the guilt offering, and its blood he shall splatter all around on the altar.
3_7_3: And he shall offer from it all its fat: the fat tail and the fat that covers the internal organs,
3_7_4: The two kidneys with the fat that is on them by the flanks, and the long lobe of the liver attached above the kidneys, he shall remove.
3_7_5: The priest shall burn them on the altar as a food offering made by fire to the LORD; it is a guilt offering.
3_7_6: Every male among the priests may eat it, and it shall be eaten in a holy place, for it is most holy.
3_7_7: The guilt offering is like the sin offering; there is one law for them both: the priest who makes atonement with it shall have it.
3_7_8: The priest who presents anyone's burnt offering shall have for himself the hide of the burnt offering which he has offered.
3_7_9: Also every grain offering that is baked in the oven and all that is prepared in a pan or on a griddle shall belong to the priest who presents it.
3_7_10: Every grain offering, whether mixed with oil or dry, shall belong equally to all the sons of Aaron, to one as much as the other.
3_7_11: This is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings which shall be presented to the LORD:
3_7_12: If he offers it for a thanksgiving, then he shall offer with the sacrifice of thanksgiving unleavened cakes mixed with oil, unleavened wafers anointed with oil, or cakes of fine flour well mixed with oil.
3_7_13: Besides the cakes, as his offering he shall present leavened bread with the sacrifice of thanksgiving of his peace offering.
3_7_14: And from it he shall offer one portion from each kind of offering as a contribution to the LORD, and it shall belong to the priest who sprinkles the blood of the peace offering.
3_7_15: The meat of the sacrifice of his thanksgiving peace offering shall be eaten on the same day it is offered; he shall not leave any of it until morning.
3_7_16: But if the sacrifice of his offering is a vow or a freewill offering, it shall be eaten the same day that he offers his sacrifice, and on the next day what remains of it may be eaten.
3_7_17: The remainder of the flesh of the sacrifice on the third day must be completely burned with fire.
3_7_18: And if any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offering is eaten on the third day, it shall not be accepted, nor shall it be credited to him who offers it; it shall be an abomination, and the person who eats of it shall bear his iniquity.
3_7_19: The flesh that touches any unclean thing shall not be eaten but must be burned with fire, and as for the clean flesh, all who are ceremonially clean may eat of it.
3_7_20: But the person who eats the flesh of the sacrifice of the fellowship offering that belongs to the LORD while an uncleanness is on him, that person shall be cut off from his people.
3_7_21: Moreover the person who touches any unclean thing, whether human uncleanness, an unclean animal, or any abominable detestable thing, and then eats the flesh of the sacrifice of the peace offering that belongs to the LORD, that person shall be cut off from his people.
3_7_22: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_7_23: Speak to the children of Israel, saying: You shall not eat any fat, of ox or sheep or goat.
3_7_24: And the fat of an animal that dies naturally, and the fat of what is mauled by wild beasts, may be put to any other use; but you must certainly not eat it.
3_7_25: For whoever eats the fat of the animal from which a food offering is made to the LORD, the person who eats it shall be cut off from his people.
3_7_26: Moreover you shall not eat any blood whatever in any of your dwellings, whether of bird or beast.
3_7_27: Whoever eats any blood, that person shall be cut off from his people.
3_7_28: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_7_29: Speak to the children of Israel, saying: He who presents the sacrifice of his peace offering to the LORD shall bring his offering to the LORD from the sacrifice of his peace offering.
3_7_30: His own hands shall bring the food offerings made by fire to the LORD, bringing the fat together with the breast, that the breast may be waved as a wave offering before the LORD.
3_7_31: The priest shall offer up the fat in smoke on the altar, but the breast shall belong to Aaron and his sons.
3_7_32: Also the right thigh you shall give to the priest as a contribution from the sacrifices of your peace offerings.
3_7_33: He among the sons of Aaron who presents the blood of the peace offering and the fat shall have the right thigh as his portion.
3_7_34: For the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution I have taken from the children of Israel, from the sacrifices of their peace offerings, and I have given them to Aaron the priest and to his sons as a perpetual due from the children of Israel.
3_7_35: This is the consecrated portion for Aaron and his sons from the fire offerings to the LORD, from the day when Moses presented them to serve the LORD as priests.
3_7_36: The LORD commanded this to be given to them by the children of Israel on the day that He anointed them, as a perpetual due forever throughout their generations.
3_7_37: This is the law of the burnt offering, the grain offering, the sin offering, the guilt offering, the ordination offering, and the sacrifice of the peace offering.
3_7_38: which the LORD commanded Moses on Mount Sinai, on the day when He commanded the Israelites to present their offerings to the LORD in the wilderness of Sinai.
3_8_1: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
3_8_2: Take Aaron and his sons with him, along with the garments, the anointing oil, a bull as the sin offering, two rams, and a basket of unleavened bread.
3_8_3: And gather all the congregation together at the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting.
3_8_4: So Moses did just as the LORD commanded him, and the congregation assembled at the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting.
3_8_5: And Moses said to the congregation, "This is the thing the LORD commanded to be done."
3_8_6: Then Moses brought Aaron and his sons near and washed them with water.
3_8_7: He put the tunic on him, tied the sash around his waist, clothed him with the robe, and put the ephod on him; and he wrapped him with the skillfully woven band of the ephod, binding it to him with the band.
3_8_8: Then he put the breastpiece on him, and he placed the Urim and the Thummim in the breastpiece.
3_8_9: And he put the turban on his head, and on the turban at its front he attached the golden plate, the holy crown, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
3_8_10: Then Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle and everything in it, and consecrated them.
3_8_11: He sprinkled some of the oil on the altar seven times, anointed the altar and all its utensils, and the basin and its stand, to consecrate them.
3_8_12: And he poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron's head and anointed him, to consecrate and make him holy.
3_8_13: Then Moses brought Aaron's sons and clothed them with tunics, wrapped sashes around them, and bound caps on them, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
3_8_14: And he brought the bull for the sin offering, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the bull for the sin offering.
3_8_15: And Moses killed it, then took the blood and with his finger applied it on the horns of the altar all around and purified the altar, and he poured out the blood at the base of the altar and consecrated it to make atonement for it.
3_8_16: Then he took all the fat that was around the entrails, the long lobe attached to the liver, and the two kidneys with their fat, and Moses burned them on the altar.
3_8_17: But the bull, its hide, its flesh, and its waste, he burned with fire outside the camp, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
3_8_18: Then he brought the ram for the burnt offering, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram.
3_8_19: And Moses killed it, then he splattered the blood all around on the altar.
3_8_20: And he cut the ram into its pieces, and Moses burned the head, the pieces, and the fat.
3_8_21: Then he washed the entrails and the legs with water, and Moses burned the entire ram on the altar. It was a burnt offering for a pleasing aroma, a fire offering to the LORD, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
3_8_22: Then he brought the second ram, the ram of ordination, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram.
3_8_23: And Moses killed it, and he took some of its blood and applied it to the lobe of Aaron's right ear, on the thumb of his right hand, and on the big toe of his right foot.
3_8_24: Then he presented Aaron's sons, and Moses put some of the blood on the lobes of their right ears, on the thumbs of their right hands, and on the big toes of their right feet, and Moses splattered the rest of the blood all around on the altar.
3_8_25: Then he took the fat and the fat tail, all the fat that was on the entrails, the long lobe attached to the liver, the two kidneys with their fat, and the right thigh.
3_8_26: And from the basket of unleavened bread that was before the LORD he took one unleavened cake, one cake of bread mixed with oil, and one wafer, and placed them on the fat portions and on the right thigh.
3_8_27: And he put all these in Aaron's hands and in his sons' hands, and presented them as a wave offering before the LORD.
3_8_28: Then Moses took them from their hands and burned them on the altar with the burnt offering. They were ordination offerings for a pleasing aroma, an offering made by fire to the LORD.
3_8_29: And Moses took the breast and waved it as a wave offering before the LORD, and it was Moses' portion of the ram of ordination, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
3_8_30: Then Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar and sprinkled them on Aaron and his garments, and also on his sons and their garments with him, and he consecrated Aaron and his garments and his sons and their garments with him.
3_8_31: And Moses said to Aaron and his sons, "Boil the flesh at the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting, and eat it there together with the bread that is in the basket of consecration offerings, as I commanded, saying, 'Aaron and his sons shall eat it.'"
3_8_32: What remains of the flesh and the bread you must burn up with fire.
3_8_33: And you shall not go outside the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting for seven days, until the days of your ordination are completed, for it will take seven days to ordain you.
3_8_34: As he has done this day, so the LORD has commanded to be done to make atonement for you.
3_8_35: Therefore you shall remain at the entrance of the tent of meeting day and night for seven days, and keep the charge of the LORD, so that you may not die, for so I have been commanded.
3_8_36: So Aaron and his sons did everything that the LORD had commanded through Moses.
3_9_1: After the ordination ceremony, on the eighth day, Moses called together Aaron and his sons and the elders of Israel.
3_9_2: And he said to Aaron, "Take for yourself a young bull calf as a sin offering and a ram as a burnt offering, without blemish, and present them before the LORD.
3_9_3: And to the children of Israel you shall speak, saying, 'Take a male goat as a sin offering, and a calf and a lamb, both a year old, without blemish, as a burnt offering,
3_9_4: Also a bull and a ram for fellowship offerings, to sacrifice before the LORD, and a grain offering mixed with oil, for today the LORD is going to appear to you.
3_9_5: So they brought what Moses commanded to the front of the tabernacle of meeting, and all the congregation drew near and stood before the LORD.
3_9_6: Then Moses said, "This is what the LORD commanded you to do so that the glory of the LORD may appear to you."
3_9_7: Then Moses said to Aaron, "Draw near to the altar and sacrifice your sin offering and your burnt offering, and make atonement for yourself and for the people. Then present the offering of the people and make atonement for them, as the LORD has commanded."
3_9_8: Aaron therefore drew near to the altar and slaughtered the calf of the sin offering, which was for himself.
3_9_9: Then the sons of Aaron brought the blood to him, and he dipped his finger in the blood and applied it to the horns of the altar, and poured out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.
3_9_10: But the fat, the kidneys, and the long lobe of the liver from the sin offering he burned on the altar, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
3_9_11: The flesh and the skin he burned with fire outside the camp.
3_9_12: Then he killed the burnt offering, and Aaron's sons handed him the blood, which he splattered all around on the altar.
3_9_13: Then they presented the burnt offering to him piece by piece, along with the head, and he burned them on the altar.
3_9_14: And he washed the internal organs and the legs, and burned them with the burnt offering on the altar.
3_9_15: Then he presented the people's offering and took the goat which was the sin offering for the people, and slaughtered it and offered it for sin, just as he had done with the first one.
3_9_16: And he brought the burnt offering and offered it according to the prescribed ordinance.
3_9_17: Then he presented the grain offering, filled his hand with some of it, and burned it on the altar in addition to the burnt offering of the morning.
3_9_18: He also killed the bull and the ram as the sacrifice of peace offerings which were for the people, and Aaron's sons handed him the blood, which he splattered all around on the altar.
3_9_19: And the fat portions from the bull and the ram—the fat tail, what covers the internal organs and the kidneys, and the long lobe attached to the liver;
3_9_20: They put the fat portions on the breasts, and he burned the fat on the altar.
3_9_21: But the breasts and the right thigh Aaron presented as a wave offering before the LORD, just as Moses had commanded.
3_9_22: Then Aaron lifted up his hands toward the people and blessed them, and he stepped down after making the sin offering, the burnt offering, and the peace offerings.
3_9_23: And Moses and Aaron went into the tabernacle of meeting, and when they came out they blessed the people, and the glory of the LORD appeared to all the people.
3_9_24: And fire came out from before the LORD and consumed the burnt offering and the fat on the altar, and when all the people saw this they shouted with joy and fell on their faces.
3_10_1: Then Nadab and Abihu, the sons of Aaron, each took his censer and put fire in it, placed incense on it, and offered unauthorized fire before the LORD, which He had not commanded them.
3_10_2: So fire went out from the presence of the LORD and consumed them, and they died before the LORD.
3_10_3: Then Moses said to Aaron, "This is what the LORD spoke, saying: 'By those who come near Me I must be treated as holy, and before all the people I must be glorified.'" So Aaron remained silent.
3_10_4: Then Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan, the sons of Uzziel the uncle of Aaron, and said to them, "Come forward, carry your relatives from before the sanctuary to a place outside the camp."
3_10_5: So they came forward and carried them by their tunics to the outside of the camp, as Moses had said.
3_10_6: And Moses said to Aaron and to Eleazar and Ithamar his sons, "Do not uncover your heads nor tear your clothes, lest you die and wrath come upon all the congregation, but let your brothers, the whole house of Israel, bewail the burning which the LORD has kindled.
3_10_7: You shall not go out from the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting or you will die, for the anointing oil of the LORD is upon you, and they did according to the word of Moses.
3_10_8: Then the LORD spoke to Aaron, saying:
3_10_9: Do not drink wine or intoxicating drink, you nor your sons with you, when you enter the tabernacle of meeting, lest you die; this shall be a permanent statute throughout your generations.
3_10_10: that you may distinguish between holy and common, and between unclean and clean,
3_10_11: And that you may teach the people of Israel all the statutes which the LORD has given to them through Moses.
3_10_12: Moses spoke to Aaron and to Eleazar and Ithamar, his surviving sons: "Take the grain offering that remains of the offerings made by fire to the LORD, and eat it unleavened beside the altar, for it is most holy.
3_10_13: You shall eat it in a holy place, because it is your portion and your sons' portion from the offerings made by fire to the LORD, for so I have been commanded.
3_10_14: The breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution you shall eat in a ceremonially clean place, you, your sons, and your daughters with you; for they are your due and your sons' due, which are given from the sacrifices of peace offerings of the children of Israel.
3_10_15: The thigh of the contribution and the breast of the wave offering they shall bring with the offerings of fat made by fire, to present as a wave offering before the LORD, and it shall be yours and your sons' with you as a due forever, as the LORD has commanded.
3_10_16: Then Moses made careful inquiry about the goat of the sin offering, and there it was—burned up, so he was angry with Eleazar and Ithamar, the surviving sons of Aaron, saying,
3_10_17: Why have you not eaten the sin offering in the holy place, since it is most holy, and God has given it to you to bear away the guilt of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the LORD?
3_10_18: Look, its blood was not brought inside the sanctuary; you certainly should have eaten it in the sacred area, as I commanded.
3_10_19: And Aaron said to Moses, "Look, this very day they have presented their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD, and such things have happened to me! If I had eaten the sin offering today, would it have been acceptable in the sight of the LORD?"
3_10_20: So when Moses heard that, he was satisfied.
3_11_1: Now the LORD spoke again to Moses and Aaron, saying to them,
3_11_2: Speak to the children of Israel, saying, 'These are the living creatures which you may eat among all the animals that are on the earth.
3_11_3: Among the animals, whatever has completely divided hooves and chews the cud—that you may eat.
3_11_4: Nevertheless these you shall not eat among those that chew the cud or those that have divided hooves: the camel, because though it chews the cud it does not have divided hooves, is unclean to you.
3_11_5: The rock hyrax, because it chews the cud but does not have divided hooves, is unclean to you.
3_11_6: The hare, because it chews the cud but does not have split hooves, is unclean to you.
3_11_7: And the pig, though it divides the hoof and has split hooves, yet does not chew the cud, is unclean to you.
3_11_8: You shall not eat their flesh, and you shall not touch their carcasses; they are unclean to you.
3_11_9: These you may eat of all that are in the water: everything in the water that has fins and scales, whether in the seas or in the streams, you may eat.
3_11_10: But all in the seas or in the rivers that do not have fins and scales, all the swarming creatures in the water or any living thing which is in the water, they are detestable to you.
3_11_11: They shall be detestable to you; you shall not eat their flesh, but you shall regard their carcasses as an abomination.
3_11_12: Whatever in the water does not have both fins and scales shall be detestable to you.
3_11_13: And these you shall regard as detestable among the birds; they shall not be eaten, they are an abomination: the eagle, the bearded vulture, the black vulture.
3_11_14: the kite, and the falcon of any kind;
3_11_16: The ostrich, the nighthawk, the sea gull, and the hawk after its kind.
3_11_17: The little owl, the cormorant, and the great owl.
3_11_18: the white owl, the desert owl, and the carrion vulture;
3_11_19: The stork, the heron of any kind, the hoopoe, and the bat.
3_11_20: All winged insects that walk on all fours shall be detestable to you.
3_11_21: Yet these you may eat among all the winged insects that walk on all fours: those which have jointed legs above their feet with which to jump on the ground.
3_11_22: These you may eat: any kind of locust, the destroying locust after its kind, the cricket after its kind, and the grasshopper after its kind.
3_11_23: But all other winged insects which have four feet shall be detestable to you.
3_11_24: By these you shall become ceremonially unclean; whoever touches the carcass of any of them shall be unclean until evening.
3_11_25: Whoever picks up any part of the carcass of any of them shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening.
3_11_26: The carcass of any animal which divides the hoof but is not completely cloven-footed or does not chew the cud is unclean to you, and everyone who touches it shall be unclean.
3_11_27: And whatever walks on its paws, among all kinds of animals that go on all fours, those are unclean to you, and whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until evening.
3_11_28: Whoever picks up any such carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening; they are unclean to you.
3_11_29: These also shall be unclean to you among the swarming things that creep on the earth: the mole rat, the mouse, and the large lizard after its kind.
3_11_30: The gecko, the monitor lizard, the common lizard, the sand reptile, and the chameleon.
3_11_31: These are unclean to you among all that swarm, and whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until evening.
3_11_32: Anything on which any of them falls when they are dead shall be unclean, whether it is any article of wood or clothing or skin or sack, whatever item it is in which any work is done, it must be put in water and it shall be unclean until evening; then it shall be clean.
3_11_33: Any earthenware vessel into which any of them falls you shall break, and whatever is in it becomes unclean.
3_11_34: In such a vessel, any edible food upon which water comes becomes unclean, and any drinkable liquid from it becomes unclean.
3_11_35: And everything on which a part of any such carcass falls shall be unclean; whether it is an oven or cooking stove, it shall be broken down in pieces, for they are unclean and shall remain unclean to you.
3_11_36: Nevertheless a spring or a cistern collecting water shall remain clean, but whatever touches any such carcass becomes unclean.
3_11_37: And if a part of any such carcass falls on any seed grain which is to be sown, it remains clean.
3_11_38: But if water is put on the seed and any part of their carcass falls on it, it becomes unclean to you.
3_11_39: And if any animal which you may eat dies, anyone who touches its carcass shall be unclean until evening.
3_11_40: He who eats any of its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening, and he also who carries its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening.
3_11_41: Every swarming thing that creeps on the earth shall be detestable and it shall not be eaten.
3_11_42: Whatever crawls on its belly, whatever walks on all fours, or whatever has many feet among all creatures that swarm on the earth—these you shall not eat, for they are detestable.
3_11_43: You shall not make yourselves detestable with any swarming thing that swarms; nor shall you make yourselves unclean with them, lest you be defiled by them.
3_11_44: For I am the LORD your God. You shall therefore consecrate yourselves, and you shall be holy; for I am holy. Neither shall you defile yourselves with any swarming creature that creeps on the ground.
3_11_45: For I am the LORD who brought you up out of the land of Egypt to be your God, so you must be holy, for I am holy.
3_11_46: This is the law regarding the animals and the birds and every living creature that moves in the waters, and of every creature that swarms on the earth.
3_11_47: To distinguish between the unclean and the clean, and between the creature that may be eaten and the creature that may not be eaten.
3_12_1: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_12_2: Speak to the children of Israel, saying: If a woman becomes pregnant and gives birth to a male child, then she shall be ceremonially unclean seven days; as in the days of her menstruation she shall be unclean.
3_12_3: On the eighth day the flesh of the boy's foreskin shall be circumcised.
3_12_4: She shall then continue in the blood of her purification thirty-three days, and she shall not touch any consecrated thing nor enter the sanctuary until the days of her purification are completed.
3_12_5: But if she bears a female child, then she shall be ceremonially unclean two weeks, as in her menstruation, and she shall continue in the blood of her purification sixty-six days.
3_12_6: When the days of her purification are completed, whether for a son or a daughter, she shall bring to the priest at the entrance of the tabernacle of meeting a one-year-old lamb for a burnt offering and a young pigeon or a turtledove for a sin offering.
3_12_7: Then he shall offer it before the LORD and make atonement for her, and she shall be cleansed from the flow of her blood. This is the law for her who bears a child, whether male or female.
3_12_8: And if she cannot afford a lamb, then she may bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons—one as a burnt offering and the other as a sin offering, and the priest shall make atonement for her, and she will be clean.
3_13_1: The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
3_13_2: When a person has on the skin of his body a swelling, a scab, or a bright spot, and it becomes an infection of a serious skin disease on the skin of his body, then he shall be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons the priests.
3_13_3: The priest shall examine the affected area on the skin of the body, and if the hair in the sore has turned white and the sore appears to be deeper than the skin of his body, it is a serious skin disease, and the priest shall examine him and pronounce him unclean.
3_13_4: But if the bright spot is white on the skin of his body and does not appear to be deeper than the skin, and its hair has not turned white, then the priest shall quarantine the one who has the infection seven days.
3_13_5: The priest shall examine him on the seventh day, and if the infection appears to remain unchanged and has not spread on the skin, then the priest shall isolate him another seven days.
3_13_6: The priest shall examine him again on the seventh day, and if the diseased area has faded and the mark has not spread on the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him clean; it is only a scab, and he shall wash his clothes and be clean.
3_13_7: But if the scab should at all spread farther over the skin after he has shown himself to the priest for his cleansing, he shall appear before the priest again.
3_13_8: And if the priest sees that the rash has indeed spread on the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean, for it is a serious skin disease.
3_13_9: When a serious skin disease is on a person, then he shall be brought to the priest.
3_13_10: The priest shall examine him, and if there is a white swelling on the skin that has turned the hair white, and there is quick raw flesh in the swelling,
3_13_11: It is a chronic leprosy on the skin of his body, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean and shall not isolate him, for he is unclean.
3_13_12: And if leprosy breaks out all over the skin, and the leprosy covers all the skin of the diseased person from his head to his foot, as far as the priest can see,
3_13_13: The priest shall examine him, and if the leprous disease has covered all his body, he shall pronounce clean the one who has the infection, for it has all turned white and he is clean.
3_13_14: But whenever raw flesh appears on him, he shall be unclean.
3_13_15: The priest shall examine the raw flesh and pronounce him to be unclean, for the raw flesh is unclean and it is a serious skin disease.
3_13_16: Or if the raw flesh recovers and turns white again, he shall come to the priest.
3_13_17: The priest shall examine him, and if the infection has turned white, then the priest shall pronounce clean the one who has the sore; he is clean.
3_13_18: If the body develops a boil on the skin and it heals,
3_13_19: And in the place of the boil there appears a white swelling or a reddish-white bright spot, then it shall be shown to the priest.
3_13_20: And if, when the priest examines it, it indeed appears deeper than the skin, and its hair has turned white, the priest shall pronounce him unclean, for it is a leprous disease which has broken out of the boil.
3_13_21: But if the priest examines it and indeed there are no white hairs in it, and it is not deeper than the skin but has faded, then the priest shall quarantine him seven days.
3_13_22: And if it should at all spread over the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is an infectious disease.
3_13_23: But if the bright spot remains in its place and does not spread, it is only the scar of the boil, and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
3_13_24: Or if the body sustains a burn on its skin by fire, and the raw flesh of the burn becomes a bright spot, reddish-white or white,
3_13_25: Then the priest shall examine it, and indeed if the hair in the bright spot has turned white and it appears deeper than the skin, it is a serious skin disease broken out in the burn, therefore the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is a leprous infection.
3_13_26: But if the priest examines it and indeed there are no white hairs in the bright spot, and it is not deeper than the skin but has faded, then the priest shall quarantine him seven days.
3_13_27: The priest shall examine him on the seventh day, and if it has spread further over the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean, for it is a serious skin disease.
3_13_28: But if the bright spot remains in one place and has not spread on the skin, but has faded, it is a swelling from the burn, and the priest shall pronounce him clean, for it is only the scar from the burn.
3_13_29: If a man or woman has an infection on the head or the beard,
3_13_30: The priest shall examine the infection, and if it appears deeper than the skin and there is thin yellow hair in it, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean, for it is a scaly outbreak, a serious skin disease of the head or beard.
3_13_31: But if the priest examines the scaly infection and indeed it does not appear deeper than the skin, and there is no black hair in it, then the priest shall quarantine the one who has the scale seven days.
