4_9_17: Whenever the cloud lifted from above the tabernacle, the children of Israel would set out on their journey, and in the place where the cloud settled down, there the children of Israel would pitch their tents.
4_9_18: At the command of the LORD the children of Israel would set out, and at the command of the LORD they would camp; as long as the cloud rested above the tabernacle they remained encamped.
4_9_19: Even when the cloud lingered long, many days above the tabernacle, the children of Israel kept the charge of the LORD and did not set out.
4_9_20: Sometimes the cloud remained above the tabernacle only a few days: according to the command of the LORD they would remain encamped, and according to the command of the LORD they would set out.
4_9_21: So it was, when the cloud remained only from evening until morning, when the cloud lifted in the morning, then they would set out; whether by day or by night, whenever the cloud lifted, they would journey.
4_9_22: Whether it was two days, a month, or a year that the cloud lingered above the tabernacle, the children of Israel would remain camped and not set out; but when it lifted, they would journey.
4_9_23: At the command of the LORD they remained encamped, and at the command of the LORD they set out; they kept the charge of the LORD, according to the command of the LORD through Moses.
57_1_1: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, To Philemon our beloved friend and fellow worker,
57_1_2: to the beloved Apphia, Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the church in your house:
57_1_3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
57_1_4: I thank my God always when I remember you in my prayers,
57_1_5: hearing of your love and faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus and for all the saints,
57_1_6: that the sharing of your faith may become effective through the full knowledge of every good thing that is in us for Christ Jesus.
57_1_7: For I have great joy and comfort from your love, my brother, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you.
57_1_8: Therefore, though I might be very bold in Christ to command you to do what is right,
57_1_9: yet for love's sake I prefer to appeal to you — I, Paul, an old man and now also a prisoner of Christ Jesus —
57_1_10: I appeal to you for my son Onesimus, whom I have begotten while in my chains,
57_1_11: who once was useless to you, but now is useful both to you and to me.
57_1_12: I am sending him back to you — he is my very own heart.
57_1_13: whom I wished to keep with me, that on your behalf he might serve me in my imprisonment for the gospel;
57_1_14: but without your consent I wanted to do nothing, that your goodness might not be by compulsion but of your own free will.
57_1_15: For perhaps he was parted from you for a while for this very purpose, that you might receive him back forever,
57_1_16: no longer as a slave but more than a slave — a beloved brother, especially to me but how much more to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.
57_1_17: So if you consider me your partner, welcome him as you would welcome me.
57_1_18: But if he has wronged you in any way or owes you anything, charge that to my account.
57_1_19: I, Paul, write this with my own hand: I will repay it — to say nothing of your owing me even your own self.
57_1_20: Yes, brother, let me have joy from you in the Lord; refresh my heart in Christ.
57_1_21: Having confidence in your obedience, I write to you, knowing that you will do even more than I say.
57_1_22: At the same time, also prepare a guest room for me, for I trust that through your prayers I will be restored to you.
57_1_23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you,
57_1_24: as do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers.
57_1_25: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
62_1_1: That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our own eyes, which we looked upon and touched with our hands, concerning the Word of life —
62_1_2: the life was manifested, and we have seen it and testify to it and declare to you that eternal life, which was with the Father and was manifested to us —
62_1_3: that which we have seen and heard we declare to you, so that you also may have fellowship with us; and indeed our fellowship is with the Father and with His Son Jesus Christ.
62_1_4: And these things we write to you that your joy may be complete.
62_1_5: This is the message we have heard from Him and declare to you, that God is light and in Him is no darkness at all.
62_1_6: If we say that we have fellowship with Him, and yet walk in darkness, we lie and do not practice the truth.
62_1_7: But if we walk in the light as He is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus Christ His Son cleanses us from all sin.
62_1_8: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
62_1_9: If we confess our sins, He is faithful and righteous to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
62_1_10: If we say that we have not sinned, we make Him a liar, and His word is not in us.
64_1_1: The Elder, To the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth:
64_1_2: Beloved, I pray that in all respects you may prosper and be in good health, just as your soul prospers.
64_1_3: For I rejoiced greatly when brothers came and testified to your faithfulness to the truth, that you are walking in truth.
64_1_4: I have no greater joy than to hear that my children are walking in truth.
64_1_5: Beloved, you are acting faithfully in whatever you do for the brothers and sisters, especially when they are strangers;
64_1_6: who have testified to your love before the church. You will do well to send them on their journey in a manner worthy of God,
64_1_7: because they went out for the sake of the Name, accepting nothing from the Gentiles.
64_1_8: We therefore ought to support such people, that we may be fellow workers for the truth.
64_1_9: I wrote to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves to have the preeminence among them, does not receive us.
64_1_10: Therefore, if I come, I will bring up what he is doing — talking against us with malicious words. And not content with that, he himself does not receive the brothers, and forbids those who wish to and puts them out of the church.
64_1_11: Beloved, do not imitate what is evil, but what is good. The one who does good is of God; the one who does evil has not seen God.
64_1_12: Demetrius has received a good testimony from all, and from the truth itself. We also add our testimony, and you know that our testimony is true.
64_1_13: I had many things to write to you, but I do not wish to write with pen and ink;
64_1_14: but I hope to see you shortly, and we shall speak face to face.
64_1_15: Peace be to you. Our friends greet you. Greet each of the friends there by name.
63_1_1: The Elder, To the elect lady and her children, whom I love in truth — and not only I, but also all those who have known the truth —
63_1_2: because of the truth which abides in us and will be with us forever:
63_1_3: Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us from God the Father and from the Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, in truth and love.
63_1_4: I rejoiced greatly to find some of your children walking in truth, as we received commandment from the Father.
63_1_5: And now I ask you, dear lady — not as though I wrote a new commandment to you, but that which we have had from the beginning — that we love one another.
63_1_6: This is love, that we walk according to His commandments. This is the commandment, just as you have heard from the beginning, that you should walk in it.
63_1_7: For many deceivers have gone out into the world who do not confess Jesus Christ as coming in the flesh. This is the deceiver and the antichrist.
63_1_8: Watch yourselves, so that you do not lose what we have worked for, but that you may receive a full reward.
63_1_9: Whoever transgresses and does not abide in the doctrine of Christ does not have God. He who abides in the doctrine of Christ has both the Father and the Son.
63_1_10: If anyone comes to you and does not bring this doctrine, do not receive him into your house nor greet him;
63_1_11: for anyone who greets him shares in his evil deeds.
63_1_12: Having many things to write to you, I did not wish to do so with paper and ink; but I hope to come to you and speak face to face, so that our joy may be complete.
63_1_13: The children of your elect sister greet you. Amen.
56_1_1: Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God's elect and their knowledge of the truth that accords with godliness,
56_1_2: in hope of eternal life which God, who cannot lie, promised before time began,
56_1_3: but has at the proper time manifested His word through preaching, which was committed to me by the command of God our Savior;
56_1_4: To Titus, my true son in our common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior.
56_1_5: For this reason I left you in Crete, that you should set in order the things that are lacking, and appoint elders in every city as I directed you —
56_1_6: if a man is blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children not accused of wildness or insubordination.
56_1_7: For an overseer, as God's steward, must be blameless — not self-willed, not quick-tempered, not given to wine, not violent, not greedy for dishonest gain,
56_1_8: but hospitable, a lover of what is good, sensible, just, holy, and self-controlled,
56_1_9: holding fast the faithful word as taught, so that he may be able both to exhort with sound doctrine and to rebuke those who contradict.
56_1_10: For there are many insubordinate, both empty talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision party,
56_1_11: whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole households, teaching things they should not for the sake of dishonest gain.
56_1_12: One of them, a prophet of their own, said, "Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, lazy gluttons."
56_1_13: This testimony is true. Therefore rebuke them sharply, so that they may be sound in the faith,
56_1_14: not giving heed to Jewish myths and commandments of men who turn away from the truth.
56_1_15: To the pure all things are pure, but to those who are defiled and unbelieving nothing is pure; but both their minds and consciences are defiled.
56_1_16: They profess to know God, but in works they deny Him, being detestable, disobedient, and unfit for any good work.
31_1_1: The vision of Obadiah. Thus says the Lord GOD concerning Edom: We have heard a report from the LORD, and an envoy has been sent among the nations, saying, "Arise, and let us rise up against her for battle!"
31_1_2: "Behold, I will make you small among the nations; you shall be greatly despised.
31_1_3: The pride of your heart has deceived you, O you who dwell in the clefts of the rock, in the loftiness of your dwelling, who say in your heart, 'Who will bring me down to the ground?'
31_1_4: Though you soar aloft like the eagle, and though you set your nest among the stars, from there I will bring you down,' declares the LORD.
31_1_5: "If thieves came to you, if plunderers came by night — how you will be cut off! — would they not steal only what they wanted? If grape gatherers came to you, would they not leave some gleanings?
31_1_6: Oh, how Esau shall be ransacked! How his hidden treasures shall be sought after!
31_1_7: All your allies will force you to the border; those at peace with you will deceive you and prevail against you. Those who eat your bread have set a trap for you — there is no understanding in him.
31_1_8: "Will I not in that day," says the LORD, "destroy the wise men from Edom, and understanding from the mountains of Esau?
31_1_9: Then your mighty men, O Teman, shall be dismayed, so that everyone from the mountains of Esau may be cut off by slaughter.
31_1_10: "Because of the violence done to your brother Jacob, shame shall cover you, and you shall be cut off forever.
31_1_11: On the day that you stood aloof, on the day that strangers carried off his wealth and foreigners entered his gates and cast lots for Jerusalem — you were like one of them.
31_1_12: Do not gloat over your brother in the day of his calamity; do not rejoice over the people of Judah in the day of their destruction; do not boast in the day of distress.
31_1_13: Do not enter the gate of My people in the day of their disaster; do not gloat over their misery in the day of their calamity; do not loot their wealth in the day of their calamity.
31_1_14: Do not stand at the crossroads to cut off his fugitives; do not hand over his survivors in the day of distress.
31_1_15: "For the day of the LORD is near upon all the nations. As you have done, it shall be done to you; your deeds shall return upon your own head.
31_1_16: For as you drank on My holy mountain, so all the nations shall drink continually; they shall drink and swallow, and shall be as though they had never been.
31_1_17: But on Mount Zion there shall be deliverance, and it shall be holy; the house of Jacob shall possess their possessions.
31_1_18: The house of Jacob shall be a fire, and the house of Joseph a flame; but the house of Esau shall be stubble; they shall kindle them and devour them, and no survivor shall remain of the house of Esau," for the LORD has spoken.
31_1_19: Those of the Negev shall possess the mountains of Esau, and those of the Shephelah shall possess the land of the Philistines; they shall possess the territory of Ephraim and the territory of Samaria, and Benjamin shall possess Gilead.
31_1_20: The exiles of this host of the children of Israel shall possess the land of the Canaanites as far as Zarephath; the exiles of Jerusalem who are in Sepharad shall possess the cities of the Negev.
31_1_21: Then saviors shall ascend Mount Zion to judge the mountains of Esau, and the kingdom shall be the LORD's.
61_1_1: Simon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ, To those who have obtained a faith as precious as ours through the righteousness of our God and Savior Jesus Christ:
61_1_2: Grace and peace be multiplied to you in the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord,
61_1_3: as His divine power has given to us all things that pertain to life and godliness, through the knowledge of Him who called us by His own glory and excellence,
61_1_4: by which He has given to us exceedingly great and precious promises, so that through them you may be partakers of the divine nature, having escaped the corruption that is in the world through sinful desire.
61_1_5: For this very reason, giving all diligence, supplement your faith with virtue, and virtue with knowledge,
61_1_6: and knowledge with self-control, and self-control with patient endurance, and patient endurance with godliness,
61_1_7: and godliness with brotherly affection, and brotherly affection with love.
61_1_8: For if these qualities are yours and are increasing, they will keep you from being barren or unfruitful in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.
61_1_9: For he who lacks these things is shortsighted, even to blindness, and has forgotten that he was cleansed from his former sins.
61_1_10: Therefore, brethren, be all the more diligent to make your calling and election sure, for if you practice these things you will never stumble;
61_1_11: for in this way an entrance will be richly supplied to you into the everlasting kingdom of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ.
61_1_12: Therefore I will always be ready to remind you of these things, though you know and are established in the present truth.
61_1_13: Yes, I think it right, as long as I am in this tent, to stir you up by way of reminder,
61_1_14: knowing that shortly I must put off this tent, just as our Lord Jesus Christ has made clear to me.
61_1_15: Moreover, I will make every effort so that after my departure you may always be able to recall these things.
61_1_16: For we did not follow cleverly devised myths when we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, but we were eyewitnesses of His majesty.
61_1_17: For He received from God the Father honor and glory when such a voice came to Him from the Majestic Glory: "This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased."
61_1_18: And we ourselves heard this voice which came from heaven when we were with Him on the holy mountain.
61_1_19: And so we have the prophetic word more fully confirmed, to which you do well to pay attention as to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star arises in your hearts;
61_1_20: knowing this first of all, that no prophecy of Scripture is of any private interpretation,
61_1_21: for prophecy never came by the will of man, but holy men of God spoke as they were carried along by the Holy Spirit.
65_1_1: Jude, a bondservant of Jesus Christ and brother of James, To those who are called, beloved in God the Father and kept for Jesus Christ:
65_1_2: May mercy, peace, and love be multiplied to you.
65_1_3: Beloved, while I was very eager to write to you about our common salvation, I found it necessary to write to you exhorting you to contend earnestly for the faith which was once for all delivered to the saints.
65_1_4: For certain men have crept in unnoticed, who long ago were marked out for this condemnation — ungodly men who turn the grace of our God into sensuality and deny our only Master and Lord Jesus Christ.
65_1_5: But I want to remind you, though you once knew this, that the Lord, having saved the people out of the land of Egypt, afterward destroyed those who did not believe.
65_1_6: And the angels who did not keep their proper domain, but abandoned their own abode, He has kept in eternal chains under gloomy darkness for the judgment of the great day;
65_1_7: just as Sodom and Gomorrah and the cities around them, which likewise indulged in sexual immorality and pursued unnatural desire, are set forth as an example, suffering the punishment of eternal fire.
65_1_8: Yet in the same way, these dreamers also defile the flesh, reject authority, and blaspheme the glorious ones.
65_1_9: Yet Michael the archangel, in contending with the devil, when he disputed about the body of Moses, did not dare bring against him a reviling accusation, but said, "The Lord rebuke you!"
65_1_10: But these people speak evil of whatever they do not know, and what they understand naturally, like unreasoning animals, by these things they destroy themselves.
65_1_11: Woe to them! For they have gone in the way of Cain, have rushed headlong into the error of Balaam for profit, and perished in the rebellion of Korah.
65_1_12: These are hidden reefs at your love feasts, feasting with you without reverence, caring only for themselves — waterless clouds swept along by winds; late autumn trees without fruit, twice dead, uprooted;
65_1_13: raging waves of the sea, foaming up their own shame; wandering stars for whom the blackness of darkness is reserved forever.
65_1_14: It was also about these that Enoch, the seventh from Adam, prophesied, saying, "Behold, the Lord comes with ten thousands of His holy ones,
65_1_15: to execute judgment on all, and to convict all the ungodly of all their ungodly deeds which they have committed in an ungodly way, and of all the harsh things which ungodly sinners have spoken against Him."
65_1_16: These are grumblers, malcontents, walking according to their own sinful lusts; they mouth great swelling words, flattering people for the sake of their own advantage.
65_1_17: But you, beloved, remember the words spoken beforehand by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ,
65_1_18: how they told you that in the last time there would be mockers, walking according to their own ungodly lusts.
65_1_19: These are the ones who cause divisions, worldly-minded and devoid of the Spirit.
65_1_20: But you, beloved, building yourselves up on your most holy faith and praying in the Holy Spirit,
65_1_21: keep yourselves in the love of God, waiting expectantly for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ unto eternal life.
65_1_22: And have compassion on those who are doubting;
65_1_23: save others by snatching them out of the fire; and on some have mercy with fear, hating even the garment defiled by the flesh.
65_1_24: Now to Him who is able to keep you from stumbling, and to present you blameless before the presence of His glory with great joy,
65_1_25: to the only God our Savior, through Jesus Christ our Lord, be glory, majesty, dominion, and power, both now and forever. Amen.
58_1_1: Long ago, at many times and in many ways, God spoke to our fathers through the prophets,
58_1_2: but in these last days he has spoken to us by his Son, whom he has appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the universe.
58_1_3: He is the radiance of God's glory and the exact imprint of his nature, upholding all things by the word of his power. After making purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,
58_1_4: having become as much superior to the angels as the name he has inherited is more excellent than theirs.
58_1_5: For to which of the angels did God ever say, 'You are my Son, today I have begotten you'? Or again, 'I will be to him a Father, and he shall be to me a Son'?
58_1_6: And again, when he brings his firstborn into the world, he says, 'Let all God's angels worship him.'
58_1_7: Of the angels he says, 'He makes his angels winds, and his ministers a flame of fire.'
58_1_8: But to the Son he says, 'Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; a scepter of righteousness and justice is the scepter of your kingdom.
58_1_9: You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of joy beyond your companions.'
58_1_10: And, 'You, Lord, in the beginning laid the foundation of the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.
58_1_11: They will perish, but you remain; they will all wear out like a garment,
58_1_12: and like a cloak you will roll them up, and they will be changed. But you are the same, and your years will never end.'
58_1_13: And to which of the angels has he ever said, 'Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet'?
58_1_14: Are they not all ministering spirits sent out to serve for the sake of those who will inherit salvation?
58_2_1: Therefore we must pay much closer attention to what we have heard, lest we drift away from it.
58_2_2: For since the word spoken through angels proved steadfast, and every transgression and disobedience received a just penalty,
58_2_3: how shall we escape if we neglect such a great salvation? It was first declared by the Lord, and it was confirmed to us by those who heard him,
58_2_4: while God also testified by signs and wonders and various miracles and gifts of the Holy Spirit distributed according to his will.
58_2_5: For it was not to angels that God subjected the world to come, of which we are speaking.
58_2_6: But someone has testified somewhere, 'What is man that you are mindful of him, or the son of man that you care for him?
58_2_7: You made him for a little while lower than the angels; you crowned him with glory and honor, setting him over the works of your hands.
58_2_8: You have put all things in subjection under his feet.' Now in putting everything in subjection to him, he left nothing outside his control. But as it is, we do not yet see everything in subjection to him.
58_2_9: But we do see Jesus, who for a little while was made lower than the angels, now crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of death, so that by the grace of God he might taste death for everyone.
58_2_10: For it was fitting for him, for whom and by whom all things exist, in bringing many sons and daughters to glory, to make the founder and source of their salvation perfect through suffering.
58_2_11: For he who sanctifies and those who are sanctified all have one Father. That is why he is not ashamed to call them brothers and sisters,
58_2_12: saying, 'I will proclaim your name to my brothers and sisters; in the midst of the congregation I will sing your praise.'
58_2_13: And again, 'I will put my trust in him.' And again, 'Here I am with the children God has given me.'
58_2_14: Since the children share in flesh and blood, he himself likewise partook of the same, so that through death he might destroy the one who has the power of death, that is, the devil,
58_2_15: and deliver all those who through fear of death were subject to lifelong bondage.
58_2_16: For surely it is not angels that he helps, but he helps the offspring of Abraham.
58_2_17: Therefore he had to be made like his brothers and sisters in every respect, so that he might become a merciful and faithful High Priest in matters pertaining to God, to make atonement for the sins of the people.
58_2_18: For because he himself has suffered when tempted, he is able to help those who are being tempted.
58_3_1: Therefore, holy brothers and sisters who share in a heavenly calling, consider Jesus, the Apostle and High Priest of our confession,
58_3_2: who was faithful to him who appointed him, just as Moses also was faithful in all God's house.
58_3_3: For Jesus has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses, as much more glory as the builder of a house has more honor than the house itself.
58_3_4: For every house is built by someone, but the one who built all things is God.
58_3_5: Now Moses was faithful in all God's house as a servant, as a testimony to the things that were to be spoken afterward,
58_3_6: but Christ is faithful over God's house as a Son. And we are his house, if we hold fast our confidence and the hope of which we boast firm to the end.
58_3_7: Therefore, as the Holy Spirit says, 'Today, if you hear his voice,
58_3_8: do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion, on the day of testing in the wilderness,
58_3_9: where your fathers tested me and tried my patience, and saw my works for forty years.
58_3_10: Therefore I was provoked with that generation, and said, "They always go astray in their hearts, and they have not known my ways."
58_3_11: So I swore in my wrath, "They shall not enter my rest."'
58_3_12: Take care, brothers and sisters, lest there be in any of you an evil, unbelieving heart that turns you away from the living God.
58_3_13: But exhort one another daily, while it is called 'today,' lest any of you be hardened through the deceitfulness of sin.
58_3_14: For we have come to share in Christ, if indeed we hold the beginning of our confidence firm to the end,
58_3_15: as it is said, 'Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion.'
58_3_16: For who, having heard, rebelled? Was it not all who came out of Egypt, led by Moses?
58_3_17: And with whom was he provoked for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?
58_3_18: And to whom did he swear that they would not enter his rest, but to those who were disobedient?
58_3_19: So we see that they were unable to enter because of unbelief.
58_4_1: Therefore, since the promise of entering his rest still stands, let us beware lest any of you be found to have fallen short of it.
58_4_2: For the good news came to us just as to them, but the message they heard did not benefit them, because they were not united with those who listened in faith.
58_4_3: For we who have believed do enter that rest, just as he has said, 'So I swore in my wrath, "They shall not enter my rest,"' even though his works were finished from the foundation of the world.
58_4_4: For he has spoken somewhere concerning the seventh day in this way: 'And God rested on the seventh day from all his works.'
58_4_5: And again in this passage he said, 'They shall not enter my rest.'
58_4_6: Since therefore it remains for some to enter it, and those who formerly received the good news failed to enter because of disobedience,
58_4_7: he again designates a certain day — 'Today' — saying through David after such a long time, just as has been said, 'Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts.'
58_4_8: For if Joshua had given them rest, God would not have spoken afterward of another day.
58_4_9: So then, there remains a Sabbath rest for the people of God,
58_4_10: for whoever has entered God's rest has also rested from his own works as God did from his.
58_4_11: Let us therefore strive earnestly to enter that rest, so that no one will fall by the same pattern of disobedience.
58_4_12: For the word of God is living and active, sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing to the division of soul and spirit, of joints and marrow, and able to judge the thoughts and intentions of the heart.
58_4_13: And no creature is hidden from his sight, but all things are naked and exposed to the eyes of him to whom we must give an account.
58_4_14: Since then we have a great High Priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast our confession.
58_4_15: For we do not have a High Priest who is unable to sympathize with our weaknesses, but one who in every respect has been tempted as we are, yet without sin.
58_4_16: Let us therefore come boldly to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy and find grace to help in time of need.
58_5_1: For every high priest taken from among men is appointed to act on behalf of men in things pertaining to God, to offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.
58_5_2: He can deal gently with the ignorant and wayward, since he himself is also clothed with weakness.
58_5_3: Because of this he is required to offer sacrifices for sins as well for himself as for the people.
58_5_4: And no one takes this honor for himself, but he receives it when called by God, just as Aaron was.
58_5_5: So also Christ did not exalt himself to become High Priest, but it was God who said to him, 'You are my Son, today I have begotten you.'
58_5_6: As he also says in another place, 'You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.'
58_5_7: In the days of his earthly life, he offered up prayers and appeals with loud cries and tears to the one who was able to save him from death, and he was heard because of his deep reverence.
58_5_8: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience from the things which he suffered.
58_5_9: And having been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation to all who obey him,
58_5_10: having been designated by God as a High Priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
58_5_11: About him we have much to say, and it is hard to explain, since you have become dull of hearing.
58_5_12: For though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you again the basic principles of God's word. You have come to need milk and not solid food.
58_5_13: For everyone who lives on milk is inexperienced with the message about righteousness, for he is still an infant.
58_5_14: But solid food is for the mature, for those who by constant practice have their senses trained to discern both good and evil.
58_6_1: Therefore, leaving the elementary teaching about Christ, let us press on to maturity, not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works and of faith toward God,
58_6_2: instruction about washings, the laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment.
58_6_3: And this we will do if God permits.
58_6_4: For it is impossible to renew to repentance those who were once enlightened, who have tasted the heavenly gift, and have become partakers of the Holy Spirit,
58_6_5: and have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the powers of the age to come,
58_6_6: if they then fall away — since to their own harm they are recrucifying the Son of God and holding him up to contempt.
58_6_7: For ground that drinks the rain that often falls on it and bears crops useful to those for whose sake it is cultivated receives a blessing from God;
58_6_8: but if it bears thorns and thistles, it is worthless and near to being cursed, and its end is to be burned.
58_6_9: But, beloved, even though we speak this way, we are confident of better things concerning you — things that belong to salvation.
58_6_10: For God is not unjust so as to overlook your work and the love you have shown toward his name in serving the saints, as you still do.
58_6_11: And we desire each one of you to show the same earnestness to the full assurance of hope until the end,
58_6_12: so that you will not become sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and patient endurance inherit the promises.
58_6_13: For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he had no one greater by whom to swear, he swore by himself,
58_6_14: saying, 'Surely I will bless you and greatly multiply you.'
58_6_15: And so, after he had patiently endured, Abraham obtained the promise.
58_6_16: For people swear by something greater than themselves, and in all their disputes an oath is final for confirmation.
58_6_17: So when God desired to show even more clearly to the heirs of the promise the unchangeable character of his purpose, he guaranteed it with an oath,
58_6_18: so that by two unchangeable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we who have fled for refuge might have strong encouragement to seize the hope set before us.
58_6_19: We have this hope as an anchor for the soul, both sure and steadfast, a hope that enters into the inner sanctuary behind the curtain,
58_6_20: where Jesus has gone as a forerunner on our behalf, having become a High Priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.
58_7_1: For this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of the Most High God, met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him,
58_7_2: and to him Abraham apportioned a tenth of everything. He is first, by translation of his name, king of righteousness, and then also king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
58_7_3: Without father, without mother, without genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but resembling the Son of God, he remains a priest forever.
58_7_4: Now consider how great this man was — Abraham the patriarch himself gave him a tenth of the choicest spoils.
58_7_5: And those descendants of Levi who receive the priestly office have a commandment in the law to collect tithes from the people, that is, from their brothers and sisters, though these are also descended from Abraham.
58_7_6: But this man who does not trace his descent from them collected a tenth from Abraham and blessed the one who had the promises.
58_7_7: Without any dispute, the inferior is blessed by the superior.
58_7_8: In the one case, men who will die receive tithes; but in the other case, Scripture testifies that he lives on.
58_7_9: One might even say that Levi himself, who collects tithes, paid tithes through Abraham,
58_7_10: for he was still in the loins of his ancestor when Melchizedek met him.
58_7_11: Now if perfection could be attained through the Levitical priesthood — for on the basis of it the people received the law — what further need was there for another priest to arise according to the order of Melchizedek, rather than one named after the order of Aaron?
58_7_12: For when there is a change in the priesthood, there is necessarily a change in the law as well.
58_7_13: For the one of whom these things are spoken belonged to another tribe, from which no one has ever served at the altar.
58_7_14: For it is evident that our Lord descended from Judah, a tribe about which Moses said nothing concerning priests.
58_7_15: This becomes even clearer when another priest arises in the likeness of Melchizedek,
58_7_16: who has become a priest not based on a legal regulation about physical descent, but by the power of an indestructible life.
58_7_17: For it is attested of him, 'You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.'
58_7_18: For on the one hand, a former commandment is annulled because of its weakness and uselessness
58_7_19: (for the law made nothing perfect); but on the other hand, a better hope is introduced, through which we draw near to God.
58_7_20: And it was not without an oath. For those who formerly became priests were made such without an oath,
58_7_21: but this one was made a priest with an oath by the one who said to him, 'The Lord has sworn and will not change his mind, "You are a priest forever."'
58_7_22: This makes Jesus the guarantor of a better covenant.
58_7_23: The former priests were many in number, because they were prevented by death from continuing in office,
58_7_24: but because he continues forever, he holds his priesthood permanently.
58_7_25: Therefore he is able to save completely those who draw near to God through him, since he always lives to make intercession for them.
58_7_26: For it was fitting that we should have such a High Priest — holy, innocent, undefiled, separated from sinners, and exalted above the heavens.
58_7_27: He has no need, like those high priests, to offer sacrifices daily, first for his own sins and then for those of the people; this he did once for all when he offered up himself.
58_7_28: For the law appoints as high priests men who are weak, but the word of the oath, which came after the law, appoints a Son who has been perfected forever.
58_8_1: Now the main point of what we are saying is this: we have such a High Priest, who has taken his seat at the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens,
58_8_2: a minister of the true sanctuary and of the true tabernacle, which the Lord set up, not man.
58_8_3: For every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices; therefore it was necessary for this priest also to have something to offer.
58_8_4: Now if he were on earth, he would not be a priest at all, since there are already priests who offer the gifts required by the law.
58_8_5: They serve as a copy and shadow of the heavenly things, just as Moses was warned when he was about to complete the tabernacle. For God said, 'See that you make everything according to the pattern that was shown to you on the mountain.'
58_8_6: But as it is, Jesus has obtained a ministry that is as much more excellent as the covenant he mediates is better, since it is enacted on better promises.
58_8_7: For if that first covenant had been faultless, there would have been no need to look for a second.
58_8_8: But finding fault with his people, he says, 'Behold, the days are coming, declares the Lord, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah —
58_8_9: not like the covenant that I made with their fathers on the day when I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt. For they did not continue in my covenant, and so I showed no concern for them, says the Lord.
58_8_10: For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, declares the Lord: I will put my laws into their minds and write them on their hearts, and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.
58_8_11: And they will not teach, each one his neighbor and each one his brother, saying, "Know the Lord," for they shall all know me, from the least of them to the greatest.
58_8_12: For I will be merciful toward their iniquities, and I will remember their sins no more.'
58_8_13: In speaking of a new covenant, he has made the first obsolete. And what is becoming obsolete and growing old is ready to vanish away.
58_9_1: Now even the first covenant had regulations for worship and an earthly sanctuary.
58_9_2: For a tabernacle was prepared. In the first room, which is called the Holy Place, were the lampstand, the table, and the bread of the Presence.
58_9_3: Behind the second curtain was a room called the Most Holy Place,
58_9_4: which had the golden altar of incense and the ark of the covenant covered with gold on all sides. In the ark were a golden jar holding the manna, Aaron's staff that budded, and the tablets of the covenant.
58_9_5: Above the ark were the cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat. But of these things we cannot now speak in detail.
58_9_6: With these things arranged in this way, the priests go regularly into the first room, performing their ministry.
58_9_7: But into the second room only the high priest enters, and he but once a year, and never without blood, which he offers for himself and for the unintentional sins of the people.
58_9_8: By this the Holy Spirit was making it clear that the way into the Most Holy Place had not yet been opened while the first tabernacle was still standing.
58_9_9: This is a symbol for the present time, during which gifts and sacrifices are offered that cannot perfect the conscience of the worshiper,
58_9_10: since they deal only with food and drink and various washings — physical regulations imposed until the time of the new order.
58_9_11: But when Christ appeared as a High Priest of the good things to come, he entered through the greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands, that is, not of this creation,
58_9_12: and not by means of the blood of goats and calves, but through his own blood, he entered the Most Holy Place once for all, having obtained eternal redemption.
58_9_13: For if the blood of goats and bulls and the ashes of a heifer, sprinkling those who have been defiled, sanctify for the purification of the flesh,
58_9_14: how much more will the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without blemish to God, purify our consciences from dead works to serve the living God?
58_9_15: Therefore he is the mediator of a new covenant, so that those who are called may receive the promise of the eternal inheritance, since a death has occurred that redeems them from the transgressions committed under the first covenant.
58_9_16: For where a will is involved, the death of the one who made it must be established.
58_9_17: For a will takes effect only at death, since it is not in force while the one who made it is still alive.
58_9_18: Therefore even the first covenant was not inaugurated without blood.
58_9_19: For when Moses had declared every commandment of the law to all the people, he took the blood of calves and goats, with water and scarlet wool and hyssop, and sprinkled both the scroll itself and all the people,
58_9_20: saying, 'This is the blood of the covenant that God has ordained for you.'
58_9_21: And in the same way he sprinkled with blood both the tabernacle and all the vessels used in worship.
58_9_22: Indeed, under the law almost everything is purified with blood, and without the shedding of blood there is no forgiveness of sins.
58_9_23: Therefore it was necessary for the copies of the things in the heavens to be purified with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things themselves required better sacrifices than these.
58_9_24: For Christ did not enter a holy place made with hands — only a copy of the true one — but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God on our behalf.
58_9_25: Nor was it to offer himself repeatedly, as the high priest enters the Most Holy Place every year with blood not his own,
58_9_26: for then he would have had to suffer repeatedly since the foundation of the world. But now, once at the end of the ages, he has appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.
58_9_27: And just as it is appointed for people to die once, and after that comes judgment,
58_9_28: so Christ, having been offered once to bear the sins of many, will appear a second time, not to deal with sin but to bring salvation to those who are eagerly waiting for him.
58_10_1: For since the law has only a shadow of the good things to come and not the true form of these realities, it can never, by the same sacrifices offered continually year after year, make perfect those who draw near.
58_10_2: Otherwise, would they not have ceased to be offered, since the worshipers, once purified, would no longer have any consciousness of sins?
58_10_3: But in those sacrifices there is a reminder of sins year after year.
58_10_4: For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
58_10_5: Therefore, when Christ came into the world, he said, 'Sacrifice and offering you did not desire, but a body you have prepared for me.
58_10_6: In burnt offerings and sin offerings you have taken no pleasure.
58_10_7: Then I said, "Behold, I have come — as it is written of me in the scroll of the book — to do your will, O God."'
58_10_8: When he said above, 'Sacrifices and offerings, burnt offerings and sin offerings you have not desired, nor had pleasure in' — these are offered according to the law —
58_10_9: he then said, 'Behold, I have come to do your will.' He takes away the first to establish the second.
58_10_10: And by that will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
58_10_11: And every priest stands daily at his service, offering repeatedly the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.
58_10_12: But this man, after offering one sacrifice for sins forever, sat down at the right hand of God,
58_10_13: waiting from that time until his enemies are made a footstool for his feet.
58_10_14: For by a single offering he has perfected for all time those who are being sanctified.
58_10_15: And the Holy Spirit also bears witness to us; for after saying,
58_10_16: 'This is the covenant that I will make with them after those days, declares the Lord: I will put my laws on their hearts, and write them on their minds,'
58_10_17: he then adds, 'I will remember their sins and their lawless deeds no more.'
58_10_18: Where there is forgiveness of these, there is no longer any offering for sin.
58_10_19: Therefore, brothers and sisters, since we have boldness to enter the Most Holy Place by the blood of Jesus,
58_10_20: by the new and living way that he opened for us through the curtain — that is, through his flesh —
58_10_21: and since we have a great High Priest over the house of God,
58_10_22: let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
58_10_23: Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering, for he who promised is faithful.
58_10_24: And let us consider how to stir up one another to love and good works,
58_10_25: not neglecting to meet together, as is the habit of some, but encouraging one another, and all the more as you see the Day drawing near.
58_10_26: For if we go on sinning deliberately after receiving the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice for sins,
58_10_27: but a terrifying expectation of judgment, and the fury of a fire that will consume the adversaries.
58_10_28: Anyone who has set aside the law of Moses dies without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
58_10_29: How much worse punishment, do you think, will be deserved by the one who has trampled the Son of God underfoot, treated as common the blood of the covenant by which he was sanctified, and outraged the Spirit of grace?
58_10_30: For we know him who said, 'Vengeance is mine; I will repay,' and again, 'The Lord will judge his people.'
58_10_31: It is a terrifying thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
58_10_32: But recall the former days when, after you were enlightened, you endured a hard struggle with sufferings.
58_10_33: Sometimes you were publicly exposed to reproach and affliction, and sometimes you were partners with those who were so treated.
58_10_34: For you had compassion on those in prison, and you joyfully accepted the plundering of your property, knowing that you yourselves have a better and enduring possession.
58_10_35: Therefore do not throw away your confidence, which has a great reward.
58_10_36: For you have need of endurance, so that when you have done the will of God you may receive what was promised.
58_10_37: 'For yet a little while, and the Coming One will come and will not delay.
58_10_38: But my righteous one shall live by faith; and if he draws back, my soul has no pleasure in him.'
58_10_39: But we are not of those who shrink back to destruction, but of those who have faith and preserve their souls.
58_11_1: Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.
58_11_2: For by it the people of old received their commendation.
58_11_3: By faith we understand that the universe was created by the word of God, so that what is seen was not made from things that are visible.
58_11_4: By faith Abel offered to God a more acceptable sacrifice than Cain, through which he was commended as righteous, God testifying of his gifts. And through his faith, though he is dead, he still speaks.
58_11_5: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he would not see death, and he was not found, because God had taken him. For before he was taken he had this testimony, that he pleased God.
58_11_6: And without faith it is impossible to please God, for whoever would draw near to him must believe that he exists and that he rewards those who sincerely seek him.
58_11_7: By faith Noah, being warned by God about things not yet seen, moved with godly fear and built an ark for the saving of his household. By this he condemned the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes by faith.
58_11_8: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was to receive as an inheritance. And he went out, not knowing where he was going.
58_11_9: By faith he went to live in the land of promise as in a foreign land, dwelling in tents with Isaac and Jacob, heirs with him of the same promise.
58_11_10: For he was looking forward to the city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
58_11_11: By faith Sarah herself received power to conceive, even when she was past the age, since she considered him faithful who had promised.
58_11_12: Therefore from one man, and him as good as dead, were born descendants as many as the stars of heaven and as innumerable as the sand by the seashore.
58_11_13: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them and greeted them from a distance, and confessing that they were strangers and exiles on the earth.
58_11_14: For people who say such things make it clear that they are seeking a homeland.
58_11_15: If they had been thinking of that country from which they had gone out, they would have had opportunity to return.
58_11_16: But as it is, they desire a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore God is not ashamed to be called their God, for he has prepared a city for them.
58_11_17: By faith Abraham, when he was tested, offered up Isaac. He who had received the promises was offering up his only son,
58_11_18: of whom it was said, 'Through Isaac shall your offspring be called.'
58_11_19: He considered that God was able even to raise him from the dead, from which, figuratively speaking, he did receive him back.
58_11_20: By faith Isaac invoked future blessings on Jacob and Esau.
58_11_21: By faith Jacob, when he was dying, blessed each of the sons of Joseph and worshiped, leaning on the top of his staff.
58_11_22: By faith Joseph, when he was dying, mentioned the coming exodus of the Israelites and gave instructions concerning his bones.
58_11_23: By faith Moses, when he was born, was hidden for three months by his parents, because they saw that the child was beautiful, and they were not afraid of the king's command.
58_11_24: By faith Moses, when he had grown up, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter,
58_11_25: choosing rather to share the mistreatment of the people of God than to enjoy the fleeting pleasures of sin,
58_11_26: considering the reproach of Christ to be greater riches than the treasures of Egypt, for he was looking ahead to the reward.
58_11_27: By faith he left Egypt, not being afraid of the king's anger; for he persevered as one who sees him who is invisible.
58_11_28: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, so that the destroyer of the firstborn would not touch them.
58_11_29: By faith the people crossed the Red Sea as if on dry land, but the Egyptians, when they attempted to do the same, were drowned.
58_11_30: By faith the walls of Jericho fell down after the Israelites had marched around them for seven days.
58_11_31: By faith Rahab the prostitute did not perish with those who were disobedient, because she had given a friendly welcome to the spies.
58_11_32: And what more shall I say? For time would fail me to tell of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets —
58_11_33: who through faith conquered kingdoms, administered justice, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,
58_11_34: quenched the power of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, gained strength out of weakness, became mighty in war, and put foreign armies to flight.
58_11_35: Women received their dead raised to life. Others were tortured, refusing to accept release, so that they might obtain a better resurrection.
58_11_36: Still others suffered mockings and scourgings, and even chains and imprisonment.
58_11_37: They were stoned, they were sawed in two, they were killed with the sword. They went about in sheepskins and goatskins, destitute, afflicted, mistreated —
58_11_38: of whom the world was not worthy — wandering about in deserts and mountains, and in dens and caves of the earth.
58_11_39: And all these, though commended through their faith, did not receive what was promised,
58_11_40: since God had provided something better for us, so that apart from us they should not be made perfect.
58_12_1: Therefore, since we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight, and the sin that so easily entangles us, and let us run with endurance the race that is set before us,
58_12_2: fixing our eyes on Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and has sat down at the right hand of the throne of God.
58_12_3: Consider him who endured such hostility from sinners against himself, so that you will not grow weary and lose heart.
58_12_4: In your struggle against sin you have not yet resisted to the point of shedding your blood.
58_12_5: And have you forgotten the exhortation that speaks to you as to sons? 'My son, do not take the Lord's discipline lightly, nor lose heart when you are reproved by him,
58_12_6: for the Lord disciplines the one he loves, and he chastises every son whom he receives.'
58_12_7: Endure suffering as discipline; God is dealing with you as with sons. For what son is there whom a father does not discipline?
58_12_8: But if you are without discipline, of which all have become partakers, then you are illegitimate children and not true sons.
58_12_9: Furthermore, we have had earthly fathers who disciplined us, and we respected them. Shall we not much more readily be subject to the Father of spirits and live?
58_12_10: For they disciplined us for a short time as it seemed best to them, but he disciplines us for our benefit, so that we may share his holiness.
58_12_11: For the moment all discipline seems painful rather than pleasant, but later it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.
58_12_12: Therefore strengthen the hands that hang down and the feeble knees,
58_12_13: and make straight paths for your feet, so that what is lame may not be put out of joint but rather be healed.
58_12_14: Pursue peace with everyone, and the holiness without which no one will see the Lord.
58_12_15: See to it that no one falls short of the grace of God; that no root of bitterness springs up and causes trouble, and through it many become defiled;
58_12_16: that no one is sexually immoral or spiritually careless like Esau, who sold his birthright for a single meal.
58_12_17: For you know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected, for he found no opportunity for repentance, even though he sought it with tears.
58_12_18: For you have not come to a mountain that can be touched — to blazing fire, darkness, gloom, and tempest,
58_12_19: and the blast of a trumpet and a voice whose words made the hearers beg that no further word be spoken to them.
58_12_20: For they could not endure what was commanded: 'If even an animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned.'
58_12_21: Indeed, so terrifying was the sight that Moses said, 'I am trembling with fear.'
58_12_22: But you have come to Mount Zion and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to myriads of angels in joyful assembly,
58_12_23: and to the assembly of the firstborn whose names are written in heaven, and to God, the Judge of all, and to the spirits of the righteous made perfect,
58_12_24: and to Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks a better word than the blood of Abel.
58_12_25: See that you do not refuse him who is speaking. For if those did not escape when they refused him who warned them on earth, much less will we escape if we turn away from him who warns from heaven,
58_12_26: whose voice then shook the earth. But now he has promised, 'Yet once more I will shake not only the earth but also the heavens.'
58_12_27: This phrase, 'Yet once more,' indicates the removal of those things that can be shaken — that is, created things — so that what cannot be shaken may remain.
58_12_28: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cannot be shaken, let us be grateful, and through that gratitude offer to God acceptable worship, with reverence and awe,
58_12_29: for our God is a consuming fire.
58_13_2: Do not neglect to show hospitality to strangers, for by so doing some have entertained angels without knowing it.
58_13_3: Remember those who are in prison, as though you were in prison with them, and those who are being mistreated, as if you yourself felt their pain in your own body.
58_13_4: Let marriage be held in honor by all, and let the marriage bed be kept undefiled, for God will judge the sexually immoral and adulterous.
58_13_5: Keep your life free from the love of money, and be content with what you have, for God has said, 'I will never leave you nor forsake you.'
58_13_6: So we can say with confidence, 'The Lord is my helper; I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?'
58_13_7: Remember your leaders, those who spoke the word of God to you. Consider the outcome of their way of life, and imitate their faith.
58_13_8: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever.
58_13_9: Do not be led away by diverse and strange teachings, for it is good for the heart to be strengthened by grace, not by regulations about food, which have not benefited those who follow them.
58_13_10: We have an altar from which those who serve the tabernacle have no right to eat.
58_13_11: For the bodies of those animals whose blood is brought into the Most Holy Place by the high priest as a sin offering are burned outside the camp.
58_13_12: So Jesus also suffered outside the gate, that he might sanctify the people through his own blood.
58_13_13: Therefore let us go out to him outside the camp and bear the reproach he endured.
58_13_14: For here we have no enduring city, but we seek the one that is to come.
58_13_15: Through him then let us continually offer up a sacrifice of praise to God, that is, the fruit of lips that confess and give thanks to his name.
58_13_16: And do not neglect to do good and to share what you have, for such sacrifices are pleasing to God.
58_13_17: Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they keep watch over your souls as those who will give an account. Let them do this with joy and not with groaning, for that would be of no benefit to you.
58_13_18: Pray for us, for we are confident that we have a clear conscience, desiring to act honorably in all things.
58_13_19: And I urge you all the more to do this, so that I may be restored to you the sooner.
58_13_20: Now may the God of peace, who brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, the great Shepherd of the sheep, by the blood of the eternal covenant,
58_13_21: equip you with everything good that you may do his will, working in us what is pleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ, to whom be glory forever and ever. Amen.
58_13_22: And I urge you, brothers and sisters, bear with this word of exhortation, for I have written to you briefly.
58_13_23: You should know that our brother Timothy has been released, with whom, if he comes soon, I will see you.
58_13_24: Greet all your leaders and all the saints. Those from Italy send you greetings.
